data.body: Jaxzen Marion, better known as BoyNotHome, is a genre-bending artist and producer from the mountains of Rossland, BC, Canada. Since diving into music production in 2021, thanks to the influence of his long time friend and collaborator INNOCENT, he has been pushing sonic boundaries with his hypnotic originals and live sets. His music has found a home on respected labels such as Deep Dark & Dangerous, Sleeveless Records and Wavecraft Collective, earning him a place on massive sound systems and stages. Backed by consistent support from industry heavyweights like Kursa, Mala, Emalkay, Excision, Barclay Crenshaw, Rohaan and Sicaria. BoyNotHome is an artist on the rise, and his journey is only just beginning…

data.body: A fixture in the underground bass music scene in Denver, Trevor nurtured a fierce love for bass music and its intensely passionate followers.  Inspired by the brilliant musicians that rose from the Denver underground, Trevor decided to further explore his passion and returned to school to study electronic music production. Hitting the scene hard under the new moniker “Orenda,” he began to put his imprint on the scene with a unique fusion of tribal world music, and powerful west coast bass. In a live show context, Orenda conveys a marriage of the two opposing ideas, resulting in mixes that are ethereal and alluring, as well as powerful and aggressive. Straying from the predictable, rise and fall cliches of the modern dancefloor, Orenda embarks on a musical journey that leaves an otherworldly impression.

data.body:
Snuffy is the solo project of an innovative DJ and producer who has made a significant impact on the electronic music scene with his dynamic sound, rooted in 140 BPM and breaks. Fusing heavy basslines with vibrant, dancefloor-ready energy, his music bridges the underground and mainstream with a unique blend of grit and groove. Drawing influence from trailblazers like Bakey, Visages, and Skream, Snuffy crafts tracks that resonate with both the heady atmospherics and high-energy moments that define modern bass music.
Since making his mark with releases on labels like Memory Palace, Subsidia, Odyzey, Qualia, and Bite This, Snuffy has established himself as a forward-thinking artist with a talent for pushing boundaries. His collaborations with notable producers like EAZYBAKED, PlayerDave, JordnMoody, Snowy, and countless others further showcase his ability to blend his sound with a diverse range of styles, creating fresh and exciting music that continues to captivate global audiences.
With a rising presence on the U.S. festival circuit, Snuffy’s electrifying live performances further solidify his reputation, creating a visceral connection with fans and making him a staple in the festival scene. As he continues to evolve, Snuffy is dedicated to pushing the limits of bass-driven electronic music while keeping his roots firmly connected to the dancefloor.

data.body: Meet American born DJ / Producer SHOSH; An artist who has risen as a staple in the UK Garage scene, and has recently found her passion in the world of Dubstep! SHOSH brings an exciting new energy to her Dubstep production, bridging the sounds of UK and US culture, with a unique UKG/140 flavour. SHOSH hit #1 in the Dubstep charts with her debut release ‘This is Not a Test’ on UKF, and has quickly built a big international fanbase around her sound. 2025 saw SHOSH selected to support huge influential artists like Levity, Sippy and Mary Droppinz, followed by a series of releases with major International Labels, and a run of worldwide performances including Rampage (EU), Shambhala (CA) and Breakaway (USA), 2026 is looking very exciting for our girl SHOSH.

data.body: Villager is a project from prodigious DC-based producer Alex Young, permeating the globe since its inception, with support from Four Tet, Disclosure, Jamie XX, Bonobo, Afrojack, Floating Points, A-Trak, Jacques Greene, Salute, Bicep, and with releases on label imprints including Boys Noize, Pretty Weird, Ninja Tune, and Method808. Now 28, at age 15 Alex Young was performing at EDC and touring with Skrillex, Diplo, and Cashmere Cat under a previous project bearing his name. Villager has recently supported Skrillex, Sammy Virji, Barry Can’t Swim, Ross From Friends, SBTRKT, Sam Gellaitry, Four Tet, Disclosure, and Jamie XX. In a print interview from 2022, Future Music Magazine described Villager’s sound as “taking cues from the richly textured electronica of artists like Four Tet and Jon Hopkins, repurposing club rhythms into detailed, emotive music that rewards closer listening.” In a recent video interview, Jamie XX described Villager’s music as “lively, vital, and inspiring.”

data.body: Taiki Nulight is a British-Mongolian DJ and producer whose sound sits at the intersection of UK bass, garage, house and club music. Known for his unmistakable production style, genre-blending sets and heavyweight collaborations, he has become one of the standout names in contemporary UK electronic music, with support from key tastemakers and releases on labels including Night Bass, Deadbeats and Polydor.

data.body:
Hamdi has become one of the most exciting and versatile producers in global electronic music, blending a signature dubstep weight with genre‑bending productions that push the boundaries of modern dance music. Born in Oxford, Hamdi (real name Alexander Hamdi) has been producing electronic music for over a decade, first getting into DJing soon after his eighteenth birthday. He quickly established himself as one of the most in‑demand young producers in the UK scene through consistent EP releases and a run of well‑received free downloads on SoundCloud. During this period, he also earned a reputation as one of the most energetic DJs on the circuit, widely recognized for his rare ability to DJ and MC simultaneously.
Hamdi’s breakthrough moment came with “Show Me” alongside Sammy Virji, which firmly placed him on the map of the UK bass scene. That early success snowballed into worldwide recognition with “Skanka,” a track that became a modern dubstep classic and was heavily championed by artists such as Skrillex, Zeds Dead, Porter Robinson, Subtronics, NGHTMRE, Slander, and more, which found the track being played to crowds of tens of thousands across the United States. He followed this momentum with standout releases including “Counting,” further establishing his sound as both forward‑thinking and deeply rooted in UK bass culture.
Over the last three years, Hamdi’s rise has been nothing short of meteoric. Since beginning to tour extensively across North America, he has evolved from a respected underground producer into one of the most in‑demand names in global bass music. His rapid growth has been reflected on some of the world’s biggest stages, with performances at major festivals including EDC Las Vegas, Coachella, Bonnaroo, Electric Forest, Portola, Lightning in a Bottle and many more. At the same time, Hamdi has proven his ability to command rooms of his own, headlining some of the most iconic venues in the United States such as Mission Ballroom in Denver, Knockdown Center in New York, and The Novo in Los Angeles cementing his reputation as both a festival powerhouse and a true touring headliner.
In 2024, Hamdi’s momentum exploded, even further, globally. His collaboration with Skrillex, “Push,” became one of the biggest dance records of the year. It proved a defining festival anthem that cemented Hamdi as a heavyweight on the world stage. He followed this success with collaborations alongside Marshmello and other high‑profile artists, marking a clear transition from underground standout to mainstage force.
As a DJ, Hamdi is known for high‑energy, genre‑fluid sets that move effortlessly between dubstep, bassline, grime, garage, and beyond. This versatility has made him a standout act at some of the biggest festivals in the world, including EDC, Electric Forest, Shambhala, and Glastonbury, alongside headline tours across North America, Australia, India, and Europe. Culturally, Hamdi’s music has become some of the most relevant and influential of the past several years, playing a key role in the resurgence of dubstep and underground bass music across the UK and the globe.
Heading into 2026, Hamdi’s momentum shows no sign of slowing down. He is set to appear on a main stage at Coachella, one of the biggest and most influential festivals in the world, alongside global performances at Lollapalooza in South America and India, Ultra Music Festival in Miami, and more major announcements still to come. With his debut Album on the horizon and an ever‑expanding global presence, Hamdi has firmly established himself as a next‑generation leader in electronic music, with the energy and innovation that defined his early career now amplified on the world’s biggest stages.

data.body: Badger is one of the fastest rising stars in UK Dance Music. His engaging presence in the viral landscape of TikTok and Instagram has become hard to ignore, with a follower growth of more than 40x over the past 12 months. As a producer and DJ specialising in UK Garage, Bass House, and Jungle - Badger's unique playful style and ability to harness the power of trends and nostalgia have built him a highly-engaged audience at a rapid rate.

data.body:
BRIT-nominated DJ/producer, BL3SS, is back with his first single of 2026 — listen to ‘terms and conditions’ https://ffm.to/bl3sstermsandconditions

Written as a fresh, carefree ode to summer, ‘terms and conditions’ samples Mahalia’s 2023 track of the same name, flipping her vocals to create a fizzing, next-gen anthem that feels ready-made for everything from house parties to clubs to festival stages.

Landing as his first new material since the release of rowdy, club-skewed debut EP, ‘The Afters’, last November, ‘terms and conditions’ also dials back into BL3SS’ addictive brand of audacious, bedroom-made dance music. Laced with the sort of hi-energy euphoria that resonates with both his next-gen fanbase and across big stages worldwide, ‘terms and conditions’ only adds to an already rich and rapidly expanding discography.

BL3SS, who bagged a BRIT-nomination for his UK Top 5 breakout single ‘Kisses’ alongside CamrinWatsin & bbyclose last year, also released a series of quickfire singles in 2025, including ‘Craving 4 U’, ‘R 2 ME’ with Tchami and surprise NYE anthem, ‘567AM’.

He also made debuts at Tomorrowland, Longitude and Reading & Leeds last summer, following a maiden headline tour of Australia & NZ where he also played major festivals like Beyond The Valley, Rhythm & Vines and Field Day. BL3SS also recently supported Oliver Heldens at The Roundhouse in March.

Looking ahead, BL3SS plays his first London headline show at Night Tales on May 30th, and will soon announce a run of North American shows scheduled throughout the summer and into the autumn.

data.body: Joining a storied lineage of family entertainers like the Isleys, the Allmans and the Baldwins, Grammy-nominated siblings A-Trak and Dave 1 waited until the pandemic to finally make their parents happy and officially team up as The Brothers Macklovitch. The turntable icon and Chromeo frontman meld contemporary club culture and funky influences from their formative days to produce strictly sexy soulful sonic grooves.

data.body:
Hailing from the snow-covered peaks of Whistler, Canada, SkiiTour are known for their upbeat “feel-good house music that keeps the vibe groovy” (DJ Mag) while channelling a retro ski aesthetic. Their annual après-ski parties at Shambhala Music Festival, featuring snow cones & snow machines, are the stuff of legend, where the energy is so high it feels like the mountains themselves are dancing.

They have released successful singles on labels like Hou5trap, DND Recordings, Box Of Cats and have made Mixmag & DJ Mag’s “Track of the Month” multiple times while garnering recent support from industry heavyweights like Fedde Le Grand, Benny Benassi, Don Diablo, and Claptone, just to name a few.

They're a hot ticket on the live music scene, with a global touring schedule that packs in over 80+ shows each year including festival appearances at Friendship, Burning Man, Bass Coast, Snowbombing Canada and many more!

data.body:
The Sponges have become well known for their unmistakable signature sound in dance music. The combination of tasteful 70's funk & disco samples, psychedelic sound design, heavy basslines, and 4x4 house rhythms can instantly be recognized as one of The Sponges' finely tuned productions. After having the opportunity to work with many of the artists who originally influenced them including Purple Disco Machine, GRiZ, AC Slater, Kyle Watson, Opiuo, The Floozies, Manic Focus & Lettuce, The Sponges have now established themselves as a respected act of their own in the world of electronic music.

Since their arrival in 2019, The Sponges have achieved millions of streams, five #1 records on Beatport and a massive touring career. The duo, composed by the collective minds of Ryan Slepin and Nik Eaton, have been invited to play some of the top music festivals in the western hemisphere such as Electric Forest, Shambhala, Elements, Friendship AMF & Envision. After releasing their debut EP and completing their first ever headline tour, sights are set high for The Sponges as they continue to develop into an internationally renowned musical project.

data.body:
Meet Justin Jay!

A feel-good, free-spirited DJ and producer who loves to blur the lines between styles and genres. Bouncing between Berlin and London, Justin serves up groovy house and techno with a twist. His sets drift between hypnotic and playful, always with a few surprises thrown in.

Key releases include “Do I Like You Like That”, “Summer Groove” on Club Heart Broken, “Back to the Groove” on Hot Meal Records, EPs on TNT and RAW, and the launch of his own label, Clean Energy.

Whether he’s turning up a sweaty house party or soundtracking a sunrise, Justin keeps things light on his feet — but deep where it counts.

data.body:
Croatian-born producer Matroda is a defining force in the ever-evolving landscape of electronic music. Known for his distinctive "matrodasound", a unique blend of house, bass, and tech influences, Matroda has garnered a global following, with fans and indus- try leaders alike celebrating his innovative approach to production and DJing.
Influenced early on by iconic artists like Led Zeppelin, Frank Sinatra, and The Beatles, Matroda's musical journey began with classical training in violin, drums, and keyboard. This eclectic foundation has allowed him to craft a diverse and unmistakable sound, earn- ing him a reputation as one of electronic music's most exciting talents.
Matroda first made waves with his remix of SNBRN's "California" alongside Chris Lake, which quickly became a festival favorite, drawing support from heavyweights like Diplo, Calvin Harris, and Major Lazer. Since then he released numerous Beatport chart toppers, such as "Forget It", "Can't Find The Feeling", "Let's Go Dancing" in collaboration with Tiga, "4U", "La Pasion" with San Pacho, "Gimme Some Keys", "Britney" and official remixes for DJ Snake, Peso Pulma, Martin Garrix, and others. His status in the industry was cement- ed with releases on prominent labels such as Insomniac Records, Repopulate Mars, Mad Decent, UKF, and his own label Terminal Underground.
With standout performances at EDC Las Vegas, Nocturnal Wonderland, Ultra Europe, Hard Summer, Ushuaia Ibiza, Mysteryland, Beyond Wonderland, EDC Orlando, Elements Festival, Shambhala Music Festival and other top-tier festivals, as well as multiple solo sold-out shows at Brooklyn Mirage, Hollywood Palladium, Stereo Live, The Midway SF, and more, Matroda has proven his prowess on stages worldwide, from Ibiza to Los Angeles.
As Matroda continues to shape the future of electronic music, his journey is far from over. With a growing discography and a relentless drive to push boundaries, the matrodasound is set to leave an indelible mark on the industry for years to come.

performances.body:
Croatian-born producer Matroda is a defining force in the ever-evolving landscape of electronic music. Known for his distinctive "matrodasound", a unique blend of house, bass, and tech influences, Matroda has garnered a global following, with fans and indus- try leaders alike celebrating his innovative approach to production and DJing. 
Influenced early on by iconic artists like Led Zeppelin, Frank Sinatra, and The Beatles, Matroda's musical journey began with classical training in violin, drums, and keyboard. This eclectic foundation has allowed him to craft a diverse and unmistakable sound, earn- ing him a reputation as one of electronic music's most exciting talents. 
Matroda first made waves with his remix of SNBRN's "California" alongside Chris Lake, which quickly became a festival favorite, drawing support from heavyweights like Diplo, Calvin Harris, and Major Lazer. Since then he released numerous Beatport chart toppers, such as "Forget It", "Can't Find The Feeling", "Let's Go Dancing" in collaboration with Tiga, "4U", "La Pasion" with San Pacho, "Gimme Some Keys", "Britney" and official remixes for DJ Snake, Peso Pulma, Martin Garrix, and others. His status in the industry was cement- ed with releases on prominent labels such as Insomniac Records, Repopulate Mars, Mad Decent, UKF, and his own label Terminal Underground. 
With standout performances at EDC Las Vegas, Nocturnal Wonderland, Ultra Europe, Hard Summer, Ushuaia Ibiza, Mysteryland, Beyond Wonderland, EDC Orlando, Elements Festival, Shambhala Music Festival and other top-tier festivals, as well as multiple solo sold-out shows at Brooklyn Mirage, Hollywood Palladium, Stereo Live, The Midway SF, and more, Matroda has proven his prowess on stages worldwide, from Ibiza to Los Angeles. 
As Matroda continues to shape the future of electronic music, his journey is far from over. With a growing discography and a relentless drive to push boundaries, the matrodasound is set to leave an indelible mark on the industry for years to come.

data.body:
Since bursting onto the scene in 2019 with breakout releases like The Ciggie and his first Beatport #1 Be Good To Me, UK-born, LA-based DJ and producer Cloonee has carved out his place among the upper echelon of global tech house. From early support on imprints like solid grooves raw to becoming a regular on Chris lake's black book records, Cloonee's rise has been nothing short of meteoric.

His own imprint, Hellbent Records, launched in 2022 with the chart-topping Tripasia, has further cemented his reputation as a tastemaker with a razor-sharp ear for dancefloor weapons. That same year, his relentless touring schedule took him across North and South America and deep into Ibiza's iconic clubs, with standout appearances for Music On, Paradise, Ants, Elrow, and more.

In 2024, Cloonee reached new heights with a breakout performance on The Sahara stage at Coachella, marking a major milestone in his U.S. journey. His viral singles Sippin' Yak and Stephanie (feat. Young M.A) have amassed tens of millions of streams across platforms, showcasing his knack for fusing underground energy with mass appeal.

A true fixture of the international circuit, Cloonee continues to tour extensively, with annual summer runs across Europe's biggest festivals and clubs, including Kappa Futur Festival, UNVRS, Ushuaïa and Hï Ibiza, as well as high-profile slots at Lollapalooza, EDC Vegas, LIV Las Vegas, Brooklyn Mirage, and beyond.

Effortlessly balancing club credibility with crossover charisma, Cloonee is not just riding the wave of tech house – he's shaping its future.

data.body:
Dance music culture is built on the values of love, acceptance, and inclusivity, and throughout the culture’s evolution, there have been leaders who embody those values through their music.

In the scene today, DJ Susan is one of those leaders, and his charting release catalog, his celebrated DJ sets, and his champion record label, Hood Politics Records, all stem from his own mission to lift up the scene and the people in it. The common denominator among his releases remains a fiery sense of high energy that encourages people to connect on the dance floor. Whether the track is raw and bassline-driven or a smoother vocal cut, the feeling is uplifting and just plain fun. That feeling has led Glathe to land several charting releases including “TALKIN” which peaked at number one on the Beatport Bass House chart and “Secrets” hitting number one on the Tech House Hype chart. The same success is apparent amongst his overall catalog with offerings on impressive labels including Spinnin’ Records and Repopulate Mars with legends of the craft like Claude VonStroke and Calvin Harris rinsing his tracks to some of the largest audiences in the world.

Glathe’s philanthropic approach to house music culminates in his self-run grassroots record
label, Hood Politics Records. He set out to be the label he didn’t get to connect with when he
was coming up. A label that will welcome artists into a strong and supportive family, where the goal is nothing more than giving a platform for great music regardless of clout or connections.

True leaders don’t care for clout and connections. They care about love, acceptance, and
inclusivity. True leaders built dance music culture, and DJ Susan is building something big.
Glathe has curated over 150 releases since launching the label in 2017, welcoming established artists like John Summit, Max Styler, and Vanilla Ace to the roster along with a squad of exciting newcomers from all over the world like LA’s BINGEWATCH and the UK’s Brad Wood.

DJ Susan’s momentum is building everywhere you look—between his performances at
Coachella, Splash House, Day Trip and ADE, his slots supporting huge artists like DJ Snake,
Porter Robinson, Tchami, Malaa, Wuki and SIDEPIECE, and his recent signing to Patrick
Moxey’s Helix Records. The future for DJ Susan is looking very, very bright.

data.body:
Award-winning DJ and producer RUMPUS is pushing the boundaries of house music, seamlessly blending his productions with live drumming to create a unique and electrifying experience for his fans. His high-energy performances, influenced by his roots as a punk rock drummer, have earned him the moniker "RUMPUS"—meaning "a noisy uproar; a commotion."

With multiple Beatport-charting tracks and over 10 million streams, RUMPUS has released music on powerhouse labels including Insomniac, Club Sweat, Space Yacht, Box of Cats and Westwood Recordings. His tracks have been featured on Spotify's top dance playlists and championed by industry heavyweights like Tiësto, John Summit, Dom Dolla and Anyma. In 2025, his track Set It Off was featured in Season 3 of HBO’s hit series 'The White Lotus', and over the last two years his viral remix of RÜFÜS DU SOL’s Innerbloom was getting played on every main stage in dance music worldwide.

data.body:
Thomas Anthony is a seasoned music producer and DJ from Edmonton, AB, Canada, on a mission to effortlessly blend the commanding force of bass & tech house with the timeless elegance of classic piano.

His high-energy house music, known as Thicc House Music, has accumulated over 10 million streams and received support from top-tier artists such as Benny Benassi, Idris Elba, KSHMR, Don Diablo, Marten Horger, Dr. Fresch, Alok, Wuki, Cat Dealers, and Bijou at world-renowned music festivals (Tomorrowland, Creamfields, Shambhala, Beyond Wonderland, and Escape Halloween).

With official releases on Hexhibition, Confession, DND Recs, Box Of Cats, and Enormous Tunes, along with 11 chart-topping remixes, all signs point to more Thicc House Music.

data.body:
Wes Please has journeyed through the vast expanse of music, navigating galaxies of sound and energy to reach the heights he stands at today. His path has not been without gravity—challenges have pulled him in every direction, forcing him to evolve, adapt, and launch himself further into the unknown. Yet, like a star destined to shine, he has only grown brighter.

Each summer, Wes Please rockets across festival landscapes, bringing his energy to massive dancefloors and celebrations under the stars. From forests to electrifying club events he has shared the decks with artists he once only dreamed of standing beside. His presence has become a gravitational force in the BC Interior and West Coast electronic scene, grounding him while propelling him toward even greater horizons.

But no traveler soars alone. It is the unwavering support of his cosmic crew—his peers, fans, and kindred spirits—that fuels his ascent. As he continues to defy limits and push the boundaries of sound, one thing is certain: the journey is far from over. The universe is vast, and Wes Please is just getting started.

data.body:
Team Fractal veteran and resident DJ of the Fractal Forest. If you’ve been to “The Farm,” you already know the Forest isn’t just a stage. It’s a special kind of chaos dressed in lasers, bass, and smile filled shenanigans.

Justin’s Dream is Monday morning. The fun kind of morning, where shoes are optional and sleep was a suggestion. He’s on as the sun crawls over the mountains, lasers carve up the fog, and the infamous Monday Morning Shit Show hits full stride. Bass bumps, feet move, and he keeps the party pumping for one more beautiful, irresponsible dance before his brother Rich e Rich steps up to officially close it out.
Ask anyone who’s stayed till the end: the Fractal Forest is the funkiest place on earth and the best way to spend the last hours on the Farm. Justin treats that slot like a public service.
Outside the forest, he’s logged serious miles across Canada, sharing booths with legends and future legends alike. Style-wise, the vibe is constantly changing. House is the backbone, but don’t be shocked when it veers into techy corners, dirty club, disco switchbacks, or whatever else is causing fire on his USB. Staying in one lane too long makes him itchy, and a switch up always scratches that itch..
In short: long time Team Fractal family, Fractal Forest resident, Monday morning conductor, and genre wanderer. Come for the last dance, stay for the "Shit Show"

data.body:
Kaya Ko is a Filipinx first-generation settler born on Turtle Island. Their musical style blends between alt-folk, neo-RnB/Soul to HipHop with elements of Underground Bass Music. With a diverse background in music, a mix of classical training, digital audio education, exploration and self-teaching gives Kaya's music an undeniable sense of lived experience and professional fluency. 

A gifted songwriter since age 10, violinist since age 5, Kaya graduated at SAE Institute, completing their studies as an Electronic Music Producer and Audio Engineer. Bringing tasteful vocals and a rap style that is reminiscent of influences such as Lauryn Hill, Kaya shows versatility and style in all of their skills.

Known for their soulful vocals and thought-invoking lyricism, Kaya Ko has graced many stages across BC and is excited to make her debut as a solo artist at Shambhala Music Festival, 2026.

data.body: For the last 45 years Marty has performed across Western Canada as a founding member in an original Art Rock Reggae Blues Band called New Moon. He also has a Solo act and has performed as a professional side musician with numerous groups and DJs in the studio and in Live performance's. He is a Multi- Instrumentalist specializing in Flutes and Saxophone's and he also plays Drums, Keyboards,12 String Guitar, Harmonica, an EWI(Electric Wind Instrument) as well as various percussion instruments. He has produced two albums in his home studio and continues to compose original Music in many different generes. He has been at every Shambhala since before they named it Shambhala and has watched the whole evolution of this Amazing Music Festival.

data.body: Sijay James has been DJ’ing since 1996 when he caught the vinyl bug in the earlier days of the Vancouver electronic dance music scene. He has performed at venues around the world and has been a strong part of the west coast festival community for nearly 3 decades. A resident of Nelson BC since 2008, Sijay has also been a creative force in the graphic design and art direction of numerous festivals, including a tenure as art director for Shambhala, playing a large role in the development of electronic dance music culture behind the scenes.

data.body:
Natascha Polké is a Swiss musician and producer known for blending indie songwriting with emotional, melodic house. Based in Zurich, she has built an international presence with releases on labels like Anjunadeep, PIAS, and Universal Music. Her live shows have taken her across Europe, North America, and Asia — from supporting Kiasmos and Christian Löffler on tour to playing festivals like Fusion, Zürich Openair, ADE, and a standout Cercle show in LA. 2025 will ultimately see Natascha embarking on her very first headline tour across European metropolitan cities.

She lived in London from 2013 to 2017, where she studied songwriting, worked in a studio, and held a residency at the iconic Roundhouse. After returning to Zurich, she immersed herself in the city’s club scene — organizing events at Hive and DJing extensively. During this phase, her current artist project began to take shape. After completing her Master’s in music, she transitioned from DJing to performing fully live — and the project really took off. Her live show merges raw, intimate songwriting with atmospheric, melodic house.

Her upcoming EP - aimed to be released in November - with the third single 'Dawn' releasing September 12th via Universal Music CH and capping the year off in December with a release for Cercle Records, will certainly open a new chapter. With her mix of emotion and groove, she gets people dancing — and maybe even crying a little.

With her mix of emotion and groove, she gets people dancing — and maybe even crying a little.

data.body: Long-time DJ, and musicophile, Roxanne Roll has been injecting ideas into Club Land since she started DJing in London in the mid-2000s. Busy playing all over the world as the founder and chief remixer for the decade running Fleetmac Wood project, when at home in LA, she works on her cosmic Kaftan Discotheque mix series and DJing eclectic disco in places like Kit Kat Club Berlin (Disco Bizarre), The Rabbit Hole Glastonbury and the psychedelic desert joints of Joshua Tree. Her recent remixes for new artist Katy J Pearson and vintage Eurythmics have been played on LA’s eminent KCRW with recent disco sets at The Secret Garden Party, Burning Man and Pike’s Ibiza.

data.body:
Be Hazzy has been orbiting the Canadian West Coast music scene for nearly two decades, chasing the kind of nights that turn strangers into friends and dance floors into stories.

His journey started back in 2003 when, at just 17, he attended his first Shambhala Music Festival. Somewhere between the basslines, dusty dance floors, and sunrise smiles, he discovered what the underground music community was really about: feel-good energy, freedom, and people coming together for the love of music.

Years later in 2014, that love turned into action when he started collecting 45s and picked up a pair of Technics 1200s and a mixer. What started as spinning classic rock sing-alongs and funky soul grooves for friends quickly evolved into marathon sunrise sets, beach parties, and late-night generator-powered dance floors that only stopped when someone yelled “last track” or the gas ran out.

Today, Be Hazzy brings the same spirit wherever he plays. His sets are built for smiles, sing-alongs, and that moment when the whole crowd suddenly realizes they know every word. No strict genre rules, no overthinking it, just great music pulled from everywhere and stitched together for maximum good times.

Whether it’s sunshine on a patio, sand under your feet, or a dance floor under the stars, Be Hazzy
shows up ready to keep the energy flowing and the Boom Boom Room rockin!

“Don’t miss a chance to see what this legend will play next… because usually he doesn’t know either.”

data.body:
Over the years the Canadian group Black Tiger Sex Machine have established themselves as key figures in the bass music scene. Along with their music BTSM pushes the boundaries of what a live show can be, implementing an extremely immersive experience where visuals, lights, music and their signature Tiger Helmets are all controlled live. Their grassroots approach has fostered an extraordinarily faithful fan base called the BTSM Church.
BTSM are world-traveling artists having played hundreds of international shows across the US, Canada, Asia, Europe and Africa, as well as major festivals including Lollapalooza Chicago and Paris, EDC Las Vegas, Shambhala, Electric Forest, Osheaga, TomorrowWorld, Ultra and more. Their Futuristic Thriller Tour debuted the Movie Experience, which wove live action cinema and storytelling into their show. The following year, BTSM shared Chapter 2 through an equally impressive tour. The entire Movie Experience sold out at such venues as Bill Graham, Red Rocks and more.
Welcome To Our Church was BTSM’s 2015 debut album, which was hailed by the Huffington Post as “One of the best electronic albums of the year,” while the follow-up New Worlds in 2018 debuted at #6 on the Billboard Dance charts. The group’s third LP, Once Upon A Time In Cyberworld, has helped push the trio to over 100 million streams worldwide on Spotify alone.
In 2023 they debuted their PORTALS album show while headlining Ultra Miami and subsequently sold out every tour stop including The Armory and Brooklyn Mirage where PORTALS was shown in 4K Ultra Wide. This live experience utilized state-of-the-art motion capture visuals to expand on BTSM’s fictional storied universe, which further grew with new shows including BTSM & Kai Wachi’s b2b SKULL MACHINE, and BTSM’s legendary Anime Show that sold out its entire tour including LA’s Torch with 10,000 tickets and 18,000 tickets between two nights at WAMU in Seattle. The Anime Show is an epic crossover event that blends various universes with BTSM’s own.
With the arrival of 2025 the group entered their OMEN era with an EP, and a new live show that will tie-in to an original Manga series. OMEN Live debuted to a massive weekend at the legendary Tacoma Dome with 20,000 tickets, and the subsequent tour saw BTSM’s highly anticipated return to Red Rocks.
BTSM also manages Kannibalen Records, which has quickly become one of the top independent labels in dance music. Through Kannibalen they’ve shaped the careers of breakthrough artists like Apashe, Kai Wachi, Dabin, and more. Kannibalen Records has been recognized worldwide through major publication partnerships with Apple, Budweiser, adidas, T-Mobile, PS5, Fifa 2021, Netflix, Marvel, NBA, HBO, MTV, UFC, and more.

data.body: Nitepunk is a boundary-pushing force in electronic music, known for a sound that defies categorization—fusing elements of breakbeat, dubstep, trap, and beyond into something unmistakably his own. Originally from Tbilisi, Georgia and now based in London, he’s built a reputation as a fearless innovator with one of the most distinct sonic identities in the game. Nitepunk isn’t riding the wave—he’s redefining it.

data.body:
SIPPY is a Sydney-born bass music powerhouse whose name has become synonymous with fearless sound design, unapologetic intensity, and stage-commanding energy. Now based in Los Angeles, the ICON Collective alum continues to carve her own lane with her most recent release Scars in Stereo (Deadbeats, 2025) - her long-awaited debut album that captures both her raw power and emotional evolution as an artist.

SIPPY’s entered the next phase of her artistic evolution. Scars in Stereo showcases a more personal and vulnerable side of her artistry while elevating her technical production skills and emotional range. She continues to showcase raw storytelling, and bold bass design in a way unique to SIPPY.

She’s a proven favorite on labels like Deadbeats, Bassrush, and UKF, and with official remixes for The Chainsmokers, Zeds Dead, and Marshmello under her belt, SIPPY continues to push boundaries. With an extensive tour itinerary ahead, and appearances at high profile festivals, 2026 marks a bold new chapter for one of bass music’s most compelling voices.

data.body:
DOWNLO is a give no f*cks and take no prisoners trap and bass music persona created by Aaron Silverstein. He has been moving bass music and festival culture from behind the scenes for over a decade. Curating festival lineups, marketing big festival brands and elevating artists who need no introduction. He has been the architect. The operator. The person who makes the moments happen for others on the stage and in front of it. BUT his true passion has always been making the music with big dreams to have some moments of his own. It’s all been taking the back burner to his aforementioned work until now.

He’s already played around the world on epic stages, with huge names playing his tracks already… But now in 2026 finally has the time and the focus to dedicate fully to his own music and vision and take things to the next level. Shambhala 2026 is set to be the flash point that makes the whole arc land. It may not be a debut but it is an arrival of his full vision and you can’t miss it.

See you soon. Bring your gun fingers and trap arms.

data.body:
Christian Smith, better known as Crankdat, began as a teenager in Ohio uploading remixes to SoundCloud that quickly lit up the electronic music scene. Those early viral moments led to collaborations with global heavyweights like Marshmello on “Falling To Pieces” and DJ Snake on “Big Bang,” quickly establishing Crankdat as a rising force in electronic music and launching him into the spotlight with a growing catalog of originals, including fan favorites like “STFU.”
What sets Crankdat apart is his relentless focus on impact, both in the studio and on stage. His music fuses hard-hitting bass with melodic hooks, crafting tracks designed to dominate
streaming platforms and ignite festival crowds. That energy has taken him to the world’s biggest electronic music events, including EDC Vegas, Ultra Music Festival, and Tomorrowland, where his sets are celebrated for custom edits, unreleased tracks, and unpredictable moments that keep fans on their toes.

With each release, Crankdat continues to evolve while staying true to the sound and energy that first put him on the map, solidifying his place as one of the most exciting names in electronic music today.

data.body:
Tha Funk Junkie is the drum and bass alias of Rory Stewart, whose roots trace back to funky house and breakbeats, and has since evolved through a different perspective on the industry: fifteen years touring the world as a leading systems engineer with PK Sound, and touring the world with some of the largest EDM producers ad DJ's.

Working closely with some of electronic music’s biggest artists and festivals, Stewart spent over a decade immersed in the technical and sonic side of large-scale live sound. Night after night in front of massive systems and high-energy crowds reshaped his musical direction, pulling him toward the speed, weight, and precision of drum and bass.

Under the name Tha Funk Junkie, he now channels that experience into high-impact DnB sets that blend tight low-end, rolling percussion, and the groove sensibilities of his earlier house and breakbeat days. The result is a style that keeps the funk in the rhythm while pushing the intensity of modern drum and bass—built for powerful sound systems and dance floors that don’t want to stop moving.

data.body:
Shakya Wijesinghe
Qi Gong Instructor | Movement Educator
Holistic Wellness Coach | Lifestyle Development

Shakya Wijesinghe has dedicated his life to the study and practice of movement, breathwork, meditation, and the internal arts. Shaped by an intercultural upbringing spanning Abu Dhabi, Sri Lanka, and the Coast Salish territories of Vancouver and the Sea to Sky region, his work reflects a convergence of Eastern philosophy, embodied practice, and modern wellness education.

From a young age, martial arts and movement became central to his life. Over time, this evolved into a deeper immersion within Taoist internal arts traditions, where he apprenticed for seven years in practices centered around breath, energetic cultivation, meditation, and the awakening arts. These teachings continue to inform both his personal practice and the systems he now shares through classes, workshops, retreats, and private sessions.

Known for creating grounded, practical, and experiential spaces, Shakya has guided workshops, retreats, collaborative projects, and wellness experiences in studios, forests, and online spaces. His teachings combine Qi Gong, breathwork, meditation, mobility training, contemplative practice, and body awareness to help people cultivate vitality, clarity, resilience, and a healthier relationship with themselves.

Alongside his work, Shakya continues studies in Taoist philosophy, energetics, and wellness disciplines under the guidance of practitioners within the Chinese energetic medicine tradition. His approach represents a convergence of the internal arts, mobility training, and breathwork adapted for modern life.

He is also the founder of the Sunrise Mobility Series, a holistic movement and wellness system developed to help elders rebuild mobility, strength, balance, confidence, and quality of life. His work with aging populations, retreat participants, athletes, and everyday individuals reflects a broader vision centered around longevity, vitality, and living with more ease and grace.

Alongside his wellness work, Shakya continues his journey of learning and collaboration rooted in listening, reciprocity, and respect. Whether leading Qi Gong at sunrise, restorative movement at dusk, or sharing tea, he brings grounded presence and a steady commitment to the path.

Today, Shakya continues to evolve his work through workshops, retreats, online education, collaborative projects, and wellness experiences that bridge ancient wisdom with modern application; helping people align with the seasons, the natural rhythms of life and the primordial light within.

data.body:
John Early is the founder of ViYarte Retreat Center (www.viyarte.com) in Playa Marsella Nicaragua and Brenna Bote is the founder of Pure Living Yoga (www.purelivingyoga.com) studios in Canada. Get ready for some high vibe activations with their festival classes and workshops ensuring connection and physical embodiment.

Matt Yausie is from Saskatoon SK where he owns and operates a gym space. At 10 Training & Athletics he provides personal training, coaching, online programming and mobility. He also duals as a yoga teacher at Pure Living Yoga. Matt is known from his athletics career in the sport of football.

He brings a high energy, positive attitude, knowledge and motivation that impacts others. In the gym, yoga studio, or dance floor as a dj, Matt always shares his authenticity and compassion with others. You may remember him from previous years, Transformational Reset and Ascension ecstatic yoga workshops at the Secret Garden. Be sure to check out his workshop this year if you want to level up and rise to Ascension in the realm of yoga, music, healing and dance.

Matt Yausie is from Saskatoon SK where he owns and operates a gym space. At 10 Training & Athletics he provides personal training, coaching, online programming and mobility. He also duals as a yoga teacher at Pure Living Yoga. Matt is known from his athletics career in the sport of football.

He brings a high energy, positive attitude, knowledge and motivation that impacts others. In the gym, yoga studio, or dance floor as a dj, Matt always shares his authenticity and compassion with others. You may remember him from previous years, Transformational Reset and Ascension ecstatic yoga workshops at the Secret Garden. Be sure to check out his workshop this year if you want to level up and rise to Ascension in the realm of yoga, music, healing and dance.

John Early is the founder of ViYarte Retreat Center (www.viyarte.com) in Playa Marsella Nicaragua and Brenna Bote is the founder of Pure Living Yoga (www.purelivingyoga.com) studios in Canada. Get ready for some high vibe activations with their festival classes and workshops ensuring connection and physical embodiment.
data.name: Matthew Yausie

data.body:
Creek St. is a downtempo, electro-acoustic band weaving their diverse influences into ambient-leaning soundscapes, trip-hop–inspired beats, and live looping. Their experimental performances feature live guitar, violin, beat production, and ethereal vocals. Based in Nelson, BC, on the traditional, unceded territories of the Sinixt Peoples, Creek St.’s music transcends genres and has been showcased at a range of folk and electronic festivals. Their sound has drawn comparisons to CocoRosie, Massive Attack, and Godspeed You! Black Emperor.

Featuring Jeff Riel (More Like Space) on guitar, Chris Rosso on production and live beats, Devon Ecru on vocals, and Nella Banner on violin and vocals.
data.name: Creek St.

data.body:
Alex is a yoga teacher, pole dance studio owner, performing artist and forever student of life. Being a pole artist has led to a beautiful journey of self discovery and empowerment, which created her intention for sharing movement and wellness; finding your radiance.

Through overcoming alopecia, building a studio and community for over a decade, and traveling the world as a facilitator and artist, she has developed her workshops to reflect what has brought her the most joy and connection.

She is a RYT 500, Thai massage practitioner through Navina, Embodied dance coach and certified pole ninja! Inviting you to channel your primal energy, get silly, feel powerful and move boldly into your next chapter. Let's dance
data.name: Alex Embodiment

data.body:
Sierra Steele is a clinical herbalist, medicine maker, and avid forager based on the Southern Gulf Islands. Her journey into plant medicine began in 2015, and after completing her Diploma of Phytotherapy at Pacific Rim College, Sierra started a herbal practice and product business called Earth and Bone Botanicals.
Sierra's passion lies in "bioregional herbalism", understanding and working with the healing plants that grow naturally in our own backyards. She creates most of the remedies used in her herbal practice from local plants she either grows or forages from the forests around her home.

Sierra’s guided plant walks are designed to be slow, sensory, and highly interactive. Sierra combines advice on how to safely identify, harvest, and craft remedies from local plants, while inviting participants to sample medicines made from the plants they discover.

In her workshops, Sierra fosters an inclusive, hands-on environment that empowers participants to build a deeper, reciprocal relationship with the local land and the plants growing right in their backyards. Her teachings blend traditional folk herbalism with evidence-based medicine to help participants understand how local plants can support their health and well-being.
data.name: Herbalism Wisdom

data.body: Cheyenne Sapphire is an international Prayerformance artist, ritual theatre director, and Tantra coach creating immersive rites of passage for modern initiates. Through ritual, embodiment, music, dance & mythic storytelling, her work invites participants into radical self-expression and remembrance-into their divinity. She has prayerformed and taught across North America, Europe, the UK, Mexico, and Costa Rica.

data.body:
Zha is a British DJ, producer, and curator best known as the founder and driving force behind White Peach Records, an influential independent label in the UK underground electronic music scene. Emerging in the early 2010s, Zha has built a reputation as both a creative artist and a tastemaker, helping shape the modern sound of dubstep, grime, and experimental bass music.

As a producer, Zha creates music that blends elements of dubstep, grime, garage, and ambient textures. His work often emphasizes mood, space, and subtle rhythm rather than high-energy drops, contributing to a more introspective and experimental side of UK bass music. Zha runs 'NAAN', which is an imprint for releasing his own music.

In the wider music community, Zha is regarded as a consistent and influential figure who bridges multiple genres within UK electronic music. His work, both as an artist and as the head of White Peach, has contributed to the evolution of modern bass music, particularly in nurturing a more experimental and emotionally nuanced sound.

Today, Zha continues to release music, support new artists, and expand White Peach’s reach, maintaining its reputation as one of the most respected independent labels in the underground electronic scene

data.body:
Gaz Frost is a Nottingham-based music producer, audio engineer, and arts practitioner, best known as one half of the dubstep production duo Congi. Since forming in 2010, Congi has carved out a distinct sound within the 140 BPM spectrum, blending deep, atmospheric textures with intricate percussion. Their music has been released on respected labels such as White Peach, Deep Heads, Fent Plates, Brownswood Recordings, The Chikara Project, and Chord Marauders.

In 2013, Gaz and Congi collaborator Tulip co-founded Chord Marauders, a collective and label dedicated to pushing forward-thinking bass music with jazz and soul influences. Through this platform, Congi has helped shape a unique corner of the dubstep scene, collaborating with artists like Yazmin Lacey, Mystic State, DE-TÜ, Occult, Kercha, Geode, and Jafu. Their work has taken them across the UK, Europe, and New Zealand, performing at key venues and festivals known for championing underground sounds. Beyond his work with Congi, Gaz also releases solo Grime and Dubstep under the alias Frost. His productions have landed on Pondlife Party and White Peach, with his biggest track, “CKY RIP”, achieving cult status in the scene. The track has been repressed three times on vinyl due to high demand and has amassed over 1 million streams.

Alongside his production career, Gaz is an established arts practitioner and an audio engineer at DTR Studios in Nottingham, working closely with artists across various genres. His expertise in sound design, recording, mixing, and mastering has made him a key figure in the UK underground music community.

data.body:
Two Girls One Laptop is your new favorite duo serving bass, UKG, and big main character energy. Known for their sexy, pants-less suits and scandalously fun sets, these two femme fatales curate a tightly selective archive of slappers that will leave the dance floor soaking wet. Blending UK bass, garage, and experimental sounds with a slutty flavor, they’re redefining the bass scene one song at a time.
Known for their bold sound and even bolder energy, the duo made their debut with Sugar & Spice on Odyzey Records — a release as sweet and savage as its name suggests. With performances at Apocalypse Music Festival, Shambhala, and direct support slots for artists like GG Magree, Two Girls One Laptop are establishing themselves as the hottest new bass duo on the rise. Type something to get started

data.body:
AHEE, a rising star in the bass music scene has captivated audiences worldwide with his unique blend of intricate sound design and infectious energy. With a career marked by relentless innovation and a mad-scientist level understanding of electronic music, AHEE is a guiding light for artists and fans alike with his ability to combine high energy bass music with positive messaging and vibrations.
Hailing from the vibrant music hub of Los Angeles, AHEE’s journey began with a deep-rooted passion for sound exploration. His background in audio engineering and synthesis quickly set him apart, allowing him to craft tracks that are as technically impressive as they are sonically rich. This dedication to his craft has led to a signature style that fuses dubstep, trap, and future bass into a cohesive, electrifying experience. AHEE’s musical flexibility has allowed him to support a wide range of music acts like Excision, Subtronics, Shpongle, Liquid Stranger, and more
AHEE’s discography is a testament to his versatility and creative prowess. Amassing over 15 million plays and releasing on esteemed labels like Bassrush, Subsidia, Circus Records, Wakaan, and more – his tracks have garnered support from die-hard fans and industry heavyweights alike.
AHEE’s influence extends beyond the stage and studio. Amassing +2.5 millions views on YouTube, he shares his traditionally secret gate-kept producer knowledge through his online tutorials and producer tools, aiming to inspire the next generation of producers.
His commitment to community and education has solidified his reputation as not just an artist, but a mentor, innovator, and role model in the electronic music scene.
data.name: AHEE

data.body:
Amp Live is a genre-defying producer and live performer who has forged a signature sound he calls Bass Hop—a bold fusion of traditional hip hop and bass music Grounded in classical musicianship and technical precision, Amp bridges the raw spirit of hip hop and early electronic music with the forward-leaning energy of modern bass music, creating a sound that feels both deeply rooted and unmistakably futuristic.
That hybrid vision fully comes alive on stage. Amp Live’s performances are immersive, high-impact experiences, blending a modern DJ set with his custom, “Frankenstein-style” beat machines—delivering a show that’s tactile, dynamic, and impossible to phone in. His live sets have captivated audiences worldwide, resonating equally with hip-hop heads, bass music fans, and electronic festival crowds.

data.body:
Comisar is a Colorado-based producer shaping a sound that blurs the lines between bass music and storytelling. Drawing from a palette of natural textures and science fiction soundscapes, his productions weave luminous melodies into layered, low-end grooves. Each release—whether it’s Vision Realms, Hidden Origins, or Magnetism—serves as a chapter in a growing body of work that invites listeners to step into a world built on mood, movement, and emotion.

On stage, Comisar crafts sets that feel like a journey. His performances balance carefully arranged originals with fluid improvisation, moving through moments of calm and intensity without losing focus. Whether in an intimate venue or an open-air festival setting, his approach remains rooted in intention: to connect with audiences through music that feels personal, exploratory, and timeless.

EDM.com describes the project as the work of two producers with over a decade of experience, using satire and anonymity to push back against traditional artist branding while keeping the focus on the music.

data.body:
EAZYBAKED is less a project and more a dialogue. A dialogue between two lifelong friends from Orlando, Florida. Between raw instinct and precision. Between the underground and the unknown. Eric Ray and Andrew Principe have spent over a decade shaping a sound that feels unfiltered, spatial, and emotionally exposed.
Inspired by the blueprint of artists like Flume, Mr. Carmack, and Tsuruda, their music is not designed to fit neatly into a genre. It is designed to feel. Low-end driven but patient. Experimental but rooted.
Their 2022 debut album INTERTWINED, featuring their standout collaboration with Of The Trees, “Sapped,” did not just introduce a sound. It opened a window into a process. From there, they continued expanding their world with releases on Deadbeats, HypnoVizion, Memory Palace, and Bassrush Records, alongside collaborations with Caspa, Montycler, Wreckno, and VCTRE.
They carried that momentum into 2025 with the independently released EP RESPAWN, their most introspective and cohesive body of work to date. The year continued with a run of collaborations including WYAWYS with DAGGZ, CODED with Digital Ethos, BRAKES with Snuffy, and SNAKE PLANT with Ranz, all leading into their upcoming LP The Antennae. Together, these releases formed a connected year-end body of work that reflects both their artistic evolution and the community growing around the project.
Built with care and carried by impact, EAZYBAKED’s presence cuts through without forcing itself forward. Their music has earned support from artists like Zeds Dead, Subtronics, NGHTMRE, Tape B, Levity, and Of The Trees.
Their work has been championed by outlets at the forefront of electronic music culture, with coverage from UKF, Beatport, EDM.com, and YourEDM recognizing their role in shaping a more thoughtful, forward-leaning corner of the bass landscape.
Their live experience is not a replication of the record. It is a reimagination. Five sets at Electric Forest across a single weekend. Their biggest headline shows to date at Mission Ballroom in Denver, Ramova Theatre in Chicago, 3 Dollar Bill in New York, Realm in Portland, along with festival appearances at Seven Stars Festival, Wakaan, Eternal NYE, and more.

data.body:
If the first thing you know about Craze is that he's the only solo DJ to win the DMC World Championships 3 times over, prepare to get yourself schooled. From day one, every uphill battle that Craze faced was met with intense adversity; every kick while he was down only served to help him rise up even stronger. You want proof? Fine.

His family fled Nicaragua for the US when he was 3 in the midst of a civil war and suffered through Hurricane Andrew in '92. Craze was a total nerd through his teenage years. But while others would chalk this up to a lifetime of hurt, Craze let it fuels his passion. By the age of 20, Craze had won his first DMC World Championship. Two more consecutive wins later, he was (and remains to this day) the only three-time winner of the world's most respected international DJ competition, the DMC World Championship - earning the attention of Time Magazine who proclaimed him "America's Best DJ." From Jamaica to Australia, Japan to Nicaragua, Craze has toured the world over, stunning audiences and DJs alike with his top-notch showmanship and masterfully genre-bending sets.

One of those worldwide treks happened to be Kanye West's 2008 Glow In The Dark Tour, where Craze accompanied West as his stage DJ across the globe. Always evolving, Craze founded Slow Roast Records with Kill The Noise, an imprint of the infamous Fool's Gold Records (A-Trak, Kid Cudi, Danny Brown) in 2010. With two releases in 2012 - the Selekta EP and Deeper with Codes, he didn't slow down and instead in April of 2015, released a 4 track EP with the title track called "Baby Gurl" featuring a young superstar on the rise named Troyboi. The EP also boasted a bonus track with Miami rap legend Trick Daddy called "Bow Down". As if that wasn't enough it was also the year, that he brought us one of the most prolific DJ routine videos to date: "New Slaves".

2018 brought the release of his EP entitled K.O.S. Knowledge of Self on Slow Roast Records and his Master Class routine video for Serato, "I Got The Juice" where he revealed not just his DJ skills, but his comedic chops, which begs the question, is there anything Craze can't do? With remixes such as Tchami’s “Proud” and “Yangmei” on Vision Recordings, Craze has set his sights on furthering his work in production and putting out music under Slow Roast. Always humble and putting the performance above all else, Craze has earned his place as an international ambassador of the art of real djing. So when someone asks you "Whatchu know about DJ Craze?", feel free to let them know a thing or two.
data.name: Craze

performances.body:
If the first thing you know about Craze is that he's the only solo DJ to win the DMC World Championships 3 times over, prepare to get yourself schooled. From day one, every uphill battle that Craze faced was met with intense adversity; every kick while he was down only served to help him rise up even stronger. You want proof? Fine.

His family fled Nicaragua for the US when he was 3 in the midst of a civil war and suffered through Hurricane Andrew in '92. Craze was a total nerd through his teenage years. But while others would chalk this up to a lifetime of hurt, Craze let it fuels his passion. By the age of 20, Craze had won his first DMC World Championship. Two more consecutive wins later, he was (and remains to this day) the only three-time winner of the world's most respected international DJ competition, the DMC World Championship - earning the attention of Time Magazine who proclaimed him "America's Best DJ." From Jamaica to Australia, Japan to Nicaragua, Craze has toured the world over, stunning audiences and DJs alike with his top-notch showmanship and masterfully genre-bending sets.

One of those worldwide treks happened to be Kanye West's 2008 Glow In The Dark Tour, where Craze accompanied West as his stage DJ across the globe. Always evolving, Craze founded Slow Roast Records with Kill The Noise, an imprint of the infamous Fool's Gold Records (A-Trak, Kid Cudi, Danny Brown) in 2010. With two releases in 2012 - the Selekta EP and Deeper with Codes, he didn't slow down and instead in April of 2015, released a 4 track EP with the title track called "Baby Gurl" featuring a young superstar on the rise named Troyboi. The EP also boasted a bonus track with Miami rap legend Trick Daddy called "Bow Down". As if that wasn't enough it was also the year, that he brought us one of the most prolific DJ routine videos to date: "New Slaves".

2018 brought the release of his EP entitled K.O.S. Knowledge of Self on Slow Roast Records and his Master Class routine video for Serato, "I Got The Juice" where he revealed not just his DJ skills, but his comedic chops, which begs the question, is there anything Craze can't do? With remixes such as Tchami’s “Proud” and “Yangmei” on Vision Recordings, Craze has set his sights on furthering his work in production and putting out music under Slow Roast. Always humble and putting the performance above all else, Craze has earned his place as an international ambassador of the art of real djing. So when someone asks you "Whatchu know about DJ Craze?", feel free to let them know a thing or two.

data.body: Longwalkshortdock essentially started the first time Dave King heard gritty electronic music in early eighties video games. Strongly influenced by these sounds and melodies, Dave started recording and looping segments of this material and experimenting with effects and sound editing as a child. Currently, a veteran of sound design, electronic music production and Live PA performance for well over 2 decades, Longwalkshortdock's music has stepped into a genre of its own. Strongly influences by vintage analog synthesis, film and video games scores, virtually all genres of music in addition to new technologies, LWSD stacks layers of melody in his tracks until they implode and reform. For his live performances, driving drums and morphing synth lines join forces with rolling grooves and melodic lead lines to create a wide variety of dance floor friendly originals. While he may be best known by the festival crowd as a not-to-miss act for his spirited antics and bass-heavy dance music, Longwalkshortdock’s productions crosses into many territories. His vast catalogue of hundreds of compositions dives into everything from Ambient, IDM, Electro, Drum and Bass, Dubstep, Halftime, Techno, Left Field Bass, Electronica, Down tempo and Film Score work. Dave holds two degrees from the Art Institute of Burnaby for Music Recording and Production and has been continuing to improve and hone his skills since he made music his full time occupation in 2005. An ongoing resident artist at top-tier festivals such as Shambhala, Bass Coast, Electric Love and Wicked Woods, LWSD has made a name for himself putting on extremely energetic, yet personal and emotionally engaging live shows all over North America. His performances are live in every sense of the word, with Dave manning his own synthesizers, drum machines, samplers, vocals, effects, computers and lighting. In the studio LWSD is always looking to blend the old and the new combining cutting edge digital technology with his passion for the lush character and sound of vintage analog gear. Just ask him about his synth collection. With four albums and dozens of other collaborations, remixes and one-off releases to his name—not to mention countless gigs at festivals, bars and other venues across North American and beyond, Longwalkshortdock is one of the hardest-working and creative people in the electronic music scene. His raw, high energy performances and openness with his fans have created an almost cult-like following. This mix of unique performance talent and authenticity make Longwalkshortdock a one-of-a-kind artist, as anyone who has seen him play or even shaken his hand will attest.

data.body: Jaq Taforo and Eric Sasaki are two local kootenay musicians that take you on a musical adventure of keyboard and drum sounds drawing inspiration from multiple musical genres in a unique blend of jazz adjacent groove music. You can find both artists performing with various other groups or solo projects but their duo setup allows enormous creative freedom as Jaq and Eric dive into new realms of improvised soundscapes and synthy explorations. Their music has seldom been rehearsed or recorded and every set is a new journey that one can only experience in the moment it is created.

data.body: Born within days and a few blocks away from where the Apache break was recorded Hoola was off to a good start at a very young age. When he was gifted Michael Jacksons “Off the Wall” album in 94 it started a serious life long obsession with acquiring every disco funk record ever made in the world. Also that year moved to Vancity from Nelson to experience the heyday of the “rave” scene and witness the impact that House music had on a culture that was happening on a fairly large scale. Dickie Doo,T-Bone, Minute Maid, Markem, Doc Martin, Ali, Czech, Little T, Lace, Tripwire, Hebegebe ,Dee Lite, Garth, Jeno, Spun-K (aka Vinyl Richie) and all the other DJ’s who played the legendary Lux Theatre on Hastings were the earliest inspirations and introduction to the underground.

data.body:
HA HA is no joke when it comes to moving energy in every form. By day, she’s an electrical
designer and yogi. By night, she channels that same current onto the dance floor
through the 4x4 rhythms of all things house.

From entering her first DJ competition in 2017 to now sharing stages with some of her biggest
inspirations in house music, HA HA has risen as a feminine pillar in Alberta’s electronic music
scene. Her journey has taken her across some of the province’s most iconic events where she
has been lighting up stages at The Womp Music Festival, Downtown Defrost, BOMFEST, Y
Afterhours, Scream and more.

She brings intention to the underground by weaving together sets that are a blend of rolling
house basslines, deep grooves, and pop vocals—crafting an experience that keeps dance floors
moving. Guided by rhythm, HA HA transforms
the dance floor into a space where movement becomes medicine and house music meets
healing.

data.body:
Mama Miche and Kenzie Clarke have been lighting up dance floors together as Bitchin' since 2004. With decades of combined musical knowledge and an instinct for reading a crowd, the duo crafts sets that span deep across musical genres, including house, bass, techno and breaks.

Their reputation for unforgettable sets has taken them around the world, from dance floors across Canada to parties in the UK, and the legendary gathering at Burning Man. Wherever they play, Mama Miche and Kenzie Clarke bring the same mission: to create a shared musical experience that connects people and delivers high-energy, feel-good journeys designed to keep bodies moving and spirits high, making it impossible not to dance.

With style, attitude, and a serious love of music, Bitchin' continues to prove that great DJs don’t just play tracks—they create moments.

data.body: DJ Foxy Moron (Ace Mackay-Smith) is a Whistler-based DJ specializing in not taking things too seriously and playing delightfully unexpected music. With breaks as the backbone, her sets wander through genres to whatever strange gem feels right in the moment, keeping dancefloors moving, confused, and occasionally laughing. Still rocking turntables and often spinning all-45 vinyl sets, this is a great way to kick off your Shambhala. When not curating dancefloor drollery, she can often be found pursuing the perfect grilled cheese sandwich.

data.body:
SonReal is a Canadian recording artist and songwriter. Across his previous releases, SonReal has garnered over 350m global streams and 200m video views. He's been nominated for multiple JUNO Awards and his previous release 'Can I Get A Witness' has gone on to be certified Double-Platinum in Canada. In addition to these numbers, SonReal also has extensive touring history within North America, most recently completing a headline tour of 48 dates across USA and Canada. He's also collaborated with acclaimed artists such as, Jessie Reyez, Snotty Nose Rez Kids and toured alongside Jon Bellion and more.
Most recently, SonReal released his album All Things Aside and followed it with a steady run of new music, including 2026 singles “The Lay Up,” “Eavesdrop,” and “Peace Of Mind.” He also brought All Things Aside on tour across North America, with 26 U.S. dates followed by 22 Canadian shows. He is currently gearing up to release his next studio album ‘Honest Work’ in 2026.

data.body:
Shawna began performing as a DJ in 1999 while living in the San Francisco Bay area, brewing eclectic fusions of global/future bass/ukg,dancehall, cumbia, house, techno, disco, minimal, an eternal eclectic selector.
She quickly became a favorite DJ on the west coast and beyond, performing at some of the most respected festivals including The BPM Festival, Miami Music Week, Garbicz, Fusion, Amsterdam Dance Event, Lightning in a Bottle, Global Eclipse, Art With Me, Summit Series, Air Festival, Art Basel, Symbiosis Gathering, Shambhala, Wonderfruit, The Gardens Of Babylon, Subsonic, Meadows in the Mountains, and legendary Burning Man art cars such as Robot Heart, Mayan Warrior and Abraxis.

She has also played noteworthy venues The Brooklyn Mirage,
Flash Club, Klein, The Grand Factory, Scorpios, Woomoon, Papaya Playa, Do Not Sit on the Furniture, The Midway, Monarch, 1015 Folsom, Public Works, Katerblau, Open Studios, and Sisyphos.
In 2013, Shawna combined forces with Italian DJ/producer Tooker as the groupKMLN
(pronounced “chameleon”), known for their coveted hybrid/live performance that pioneered the organic house sound thriving today. The duo’s releases on major labels like
Sol Selectas, Crosstown Rebels, Ouïe, Touch of Class, and Bar 25, have received support from artists such as Solomun, Acid Pauli, Damian Lazarus, &ME, Rampa, David Meyer, and Bedouin . Additionally, they have collaborated with the likes of Mira, Christopher Schwarzwalder, Nico Stojan, NU, Sabo, Viken Arman, Francesca Lombardo, and Eduardo de La Calle.
In 2020, Shawna and Tooker have began reinventing their solo projects and exploring other collaborations.

Always cross-pollinating through various scenes around the globe and pioneering the cutting-edge sounds of the future, Shawna has proven herself as a multi-faceted artist that has left her mark and
continues to push boundaries. Keep your eyes peeled for her psychedelic alter egoFräulein Hofmann.
data.name: Shawna (KMLN)

data.body: GALEN, one of the great living pillars of West Coast dance music, San Francisco’s own true hero and legend. His creation and shared evolution of Sunset Sound System have quite literally catapulted a local scene into the global sphere. Which has since magnetically attracted the world’s top DJs & producers to the west, playing for the Sunset annuals, boat parties, & Campout Festival. These events are renowned for establishing and maintaining the gold standard in sound and production, fostering a deep, tangible pride that is immediately palpable. As a preeminent organizer, GALEN is the force that brings the experience into crystallized perfection.   And within this historic landscape, his dedication to the craft of djing has not wavered. By playing his extensive vinyl collection at renegade and warehouse parties of the 90s and 2000s,  GALEN has honed his own proficiency —ranging from deep acid house to rare disco in a fluid signature sound, known for assertive mixing techniques inspired by each discerning dancefloor. His rich, precise taste has also translated into his own studio productions, with contributions to labels peppered across the globe. Currently, his latest 3-track ‘Magic Bus’ EP on the WhatToDo Label has been received with worldwide acclaim.   When this veritable stalwart is not firmly holding court in his native SF, you can find him rocking pivotal events and festivals in North America while also touring Europe from his new home base in Amsterdam. His recent headlining performances at Lightning In A Bottle Festival have helped pave the way for a new generation of audiences to experience GALEN’s bold and distinctive dancefloor style.

data.body:
One half of the mighty Orbital, and the heart, in Hartnoll, Phil Hartnoll is true rave royalty.

Phil helped soundtrack an entire generation with tracks like ‘Chime’, ‘Halycon’ and Belfast, and with their legendary performances at Glastonbury Phil and his brother Paul can lay claim to kicking down the doors for an entire electronic music scene.

As a DJ, the glint in Phil’s eye is just as bright as his trademark Orbital glasses.

He has a mischievous tiggerish energy and an unrivalled connection between the people
and music he loves, that is absolutely impossible to resist.

For over 30 years he has been inviting audiences to embark on a journey through the past, present, and future of electronic music and it’s a journey he obviously loves making as much as the crowd.

Fresh from a residency at Radio 1, touring Australia and New Zealand with Ministry of Sound, and storming series of sets alongside CONRADICAL across UK the Ireland - it’s clear the beat never stops and Phil never sleeps.

What to expect? Orbital classics, unreleased gems, and the unexpected.

Phil’s sets bring feet to the floor, tears to eyes and joy to your heart.

The party goes on + on + on...

AFTER YEARS SPENT EXPLORING GLOBAL FESTIVALS LIKE BURNING MAN AND SNOWBOMBING, HE MOVED TO LA IN 2016 TO SHARPEN HIS SKILLS AT DUBSPOT MUSIC ACADEMY. FROM SHAMBHALA'S RIVERSIDE STAGE TO THE PAGODA STAGE AT LIGHTNING IN A BOTTLE, CHRIS' PATH AS A RISING ARTIST HAS ONLY JUST BEGUN.

data.body: Edward Lane is an emerging producer and curator based out of Kelowna, BC. Driven by impulse, he channels facets of techno and beyond to create eclectic rhythms with captivating sound design. With an ear for storytelling and a taste for the underground, Edward crafts sets that feature brooding atmospheres and powerful rolling basslines that draw listeners into his carefully constructed sonic world. Since co-founding RAISED BY RAVES in 2020 Edward has directed a multitude of dance floors, some of which include Chasing Summer Music Festival, The Red Room, Bloom Nightclub, Gorg-O-Mish Afterhours, and most notably the Pagoda Stage as part of Shambhala Music Festival’s 25th anniversary. As a curator, he continues to champion forward-thinking club culture through community-focused events and projects. This can most recently be shown with his work founding and leading the Okanagan Underground Music Association (OUMA) as an educational and advocational pillar for artists and creators in his community. Edward’s unique blends and deep cuts, distinguished by their depth and unpredictability, are sure to keep any dancefloor healthy.

data.body:
A rising force in electronic music, KÖL made his debut at just 17 with a groundbreaking set at the iconic Shambhala Music Festival. Rooted in European techno and underground hip-hop, he has captivated audiences across Canada with emotionally charged performances and innovative productions.

His journey has included major festival appearances at Shambhala, Badlands, Chasing Summer, and Electric Love, along with residencies at Gorg-O-Mish Afterhours and Deep Down Inside. He has also played renowned venues like Darkstar Theatre, Commodore
Ballroom, Palace Theatre, Village Studios, and countless underground raves nationwide.

KÖL’s sound is dark, euphoric techno, house, and breaks - deep, seductive, and ethereal, always driven by a four-on-the-floor groove blending driving rhythms with hypnotic, emotive atmospheres. His music is inspired by artists such as KAS:ST, Bart Skils, Mac Declos, ZHU, Solomun, Adam Beyer, Enrico Sangiuliano, Kauffman, and many more.

Beyond performing, KÖL is the Co-Founder and Creative Director of RAISED BY RAVES, Founder of Subform Agency, and an accomplished talent buyer. Through Subform, he specializes in club and festival consulting, branding, web design, and social media marketing, shaping the scene both on and off the decks.

Dedicated to creating unforgettable musical experiences, KÖL continues to push the boundaries of electronic music, cementing his place as a rising name in the global scene.
data.name: KÖL

performances.body:
A rising force in electronic music, KÖL made his debut at just 17 with a groundbreaking set at the iconic Shambhala Music Festival. Rooted in European techno and underground hip-hop, he has captivated audiences across Canada with emotionally charged performances and innovative productions.

His journey has included major festival appearances at Shambhala, Badlands, Chasing Summer, and Electric Love, along with residencies at Gorg-O-Mish Afterhours and Deep Down Inside. He has also played renowned venues like Darkstar Theatre, Commodore
Ballroom, Palace Theatre, Village Studios, and countless underground raves nationwide.

KÖL’s sound is dark, euphoric techno, house, and breaks - deep, seductive, and ethereal, always driven by a four-on-the-floor groove blending driving rhythms with hypnotic, emotive atmospheres. His music is inspired by artists such as KAS:ST, Bart Skils, Mac Declos, ZHU, Solomun, Adam Beyer, Enrico Sangiuliano, Kauffman, and many more.

Beyond performing, KÖL is the Co-Founder and Creative Director of RAISED BY RAVES, Founder of Subform Agency, and an accomplished talent buyer. Through Subform, he specializes in club and festival consulting, branding, web design, and social media marketing, shaping the scene both on and off the decks.

Dedicated to creating unforgettable musical experiences, KÖL continues to push the boundaries of electronic music, cementing his place as a rising name in the global scene.

data.body: Cerdin is a Calgary-based electronic music producer and DJ forging a bold path through the modern dance music landscape. Cerdin blends thunderous bass, dynamic sound design, and genre-defying creativity into a signature 140-oriented sound that keeps listeners hooked from first beat to last. The RBR affiliated artist has garnered co-signs and support from the likes of Dillon Francis, DJ Snake, Alison Wonderland, and Sable Valley founder, RL Grime, in which Cerdin released with in 2024. With a strong ethos and a passion for pushing boundaries, his work reflects a deep commitment exploration and an ever-expanding creative vision.
