data.body:
No Thanks is the high-energy house project from Canadian West Coast producer Giuliano Rascan — an evolving project built on over 15 years of production experience and a relentless commitment to the dancefloor.
Originally launched as a duo, No Thanks has entered a new chapter as a solo project, with Giuliano continuing to lead the vision forward. Staying true to the sound and spirit that fans have come to know, he remains focused on delivering the same electrifying performances and polished, high-impact records that define the project.
Known for blending tech house, bass house, and pop influences, No Thanks has developed a distinct signature style dubbed “Polite House” — a vocal-driven, groove-heavy sound built on tight drums, infectious toplines, and commanding low-end energy. This forward-thinking approach has earned support from top-tier DJs and captivated audiences at major festivals including Shambhala, Beyond Wonderland, Fvded In The Park, EDC Las Vegas, and a standout mainstage performance at EDC Orlando in 2025.
With more than 100 shows in the past two years and releases on respected labels such as, Breakaway Projects, Ultra Records, Insomniac's In Rotation and Terminal Underground, No Thanks continues to build momentum on both the live and recorded front.
As the project moves into its next era, Giuliano remains focused helping shape the future of house music — breaking genre boundaries while staying grounded in a simple philosophy: say No Thanks to the rules. Welcome to the Polite Club.
data.name: No Thanks

performances.body:
No Thanks is the high-energy house project from Canadian West Coast producer Giuliano Rascan — an evolving project built on over 15 years of production experience and a relentless commitment to the dancefloor.
Originally launched as a duo, No Thanks has entered a new chapter as a solo project, with Giuliano continuing to lead the vision forward. Staying true to the sound and spirit that fans have come to know, he remains focused on delivering the same electrifying performances and polished, high-impact records that define the project.
Known for blending tech house, bass house, and pop influences, No Thanks has developed a distinct signature style dubbed “Polite House” — a vocal-driven, groove-heavy sound built on tight drums, infectious toplines, and commanding low-end energy. This forward-thinking approach has earned support from top-tier DJs and captivated audiences at major festivals including Shambhala, Beyond Wonderland, Fvded In The Park, EDC Las Vegas, and a standout mainstage performance at EDC Orlando in 2025.
With more than 100 shows in the past two years and releases on respected labels such as, Breakaway Projects, Ultra Records, Insomniac's In Rotation and Terminal Underground, No Thanks continues to build momentum on both the live and recorded front.
As the project moves into its next era, Giuliano remains focused helping shape the future of house music — breaking genre boundaries while staying grounded in a simple philosophy: say No Thanks to the rules. Welcome to the Polite Club.

tracks.title: Chill Like That - No Thanks Remix

data.body:
STUND is a force emerging from the underground, blending dark, groovy house with a hypnotic edge that commands the dancefloor. His sound weaves heavy low-end energy with entrancing rhythms and textures, pulling listeners deep into a world where movement takes over and time blurs.

STUND has become a familiar name in Canada’s west coast electronic scene, known for delivering magnetic, high-energy sets that bridge the gap between the underground and the main stage.

On the production side, STUND’s releases have found homes on respected labels such as Do Not Duplicate Recordings, Box of Cats, Westwood Recordings, Uprise Music, and Hood Politics — showcasing a sound that’s as refined in the studio as it is raw behind the decks.

Unapologetically authentic and relentlessly driven, STUND continues to carve his path through the global house scene — one hypnotic groove at a time.

data.body:
Armanni's introduction to drum and bass was a pivotal turning point, marked by a seminal
experience at Sutpen's Jungle in February 2000. His first mixtape, "DJ SS Live at Sutpen's
Jungle," featuring the vocal skills of MC Skibadee and MC Foxy, opened his eyes to the lyrical potential of drum and bass. MC Skibadee became a significant influence, shaping Armanni's powerful and diverse style of lyrical delivery, earning respect from peers and top drum and bass MCs and DJs worldwide.

Staying true to his hip-hop roots, Armanni Reign infuses his performances with bounce, flavor, and lyrical wizardry. He effortlessly bridges the gap between genres, captivating audiences with his dynamic presence.
As a recording artist, Armanni Reign's versatility shines through. He has lent his vocal talents to tracks spanning multiple genres, collaborating with artists such as Ray Volpe, Spag Heddy, Barely Alive, Riot, Zomboy, Virtual Riot, Stereolize, A Boy and a Girl, Mark Instinct, AC Slater,and many more. His ability to adapt and experiment has earned him a place in various music scenes.

Armanni Reign's music has transcended the stage, finding a home in movies, video games, and television. His tracks have been featured in advertising campaigns for "Fast and Furious 9,"Square Enix's "Just Cause 4," Gearbox's "Borderlands 3," Ubisoft's "Ghost Recon:Breakpoint," and more. His extensive catalog extends across platforms, from Netflix to various sports channels and various TV series.

Whether as a solo artist, freestyler, rap battler, or collaborator in projects like Society of
Numbers with The Upbeats or Last Rhetoric with Nick Weiller (Bro Safari), Armanni Reign's dedication to his craft has spanned over two decades and continues to captivate audiences.

data.body:
A longtime skateboarder and hip hop devotee, Geoff “Stylust” Reich has always been a rebel with a passion for his art form, so it’s no surprise that this Canadian DJ/producer’s talent for fusing hip hop flavor and an endless energy with heavy bass has earned him the rep as a creative renegade carving his own path.
Stylust’s uncanny ability to melt together multiple influences and genres has led to music that’s reached millions of listeners, earning him collabs with artists including Ganja White Night, Of The Trees, Truth, and Buku - and releases with labels such as Deadbeats, Deep Dark & Dangerous, Subcarbon, and Bassrush.
Geoff’s keen sense for eclectic, yet cohesive beats has also translated to the stage, building him a reputation for delivering a dynamic, standout set every time with past tours across the United States, Canada, and Europe, and appearances at major festivals such as Coachella, Shambhala, Outlook, LIB, and Envision. With a bass-heavy yet timeless sound, Stylust electrifies both the dancefloor, as well as headphones with his impeccable taste and rebellious energy.

data.body:
Emerging from Jackson, Mississippi with a personality larger than an eighteen wheeler dump truck, comes TVBOO, pronounced “taboo.” Mitch Draper, known as TVBOO, is quickly becoming one of the rising stars in the bass scene. His ability to weave distorte sounds through incredibly unique basslines, accompanied by infamous vocal samples, are just a few of the elements that make TVBOO, TVBOO. His shows are curated with high energy sets that are guaranteed to have the crowd moving on their feet from start to finish.

His growing success is undeniable, as seen from his co-headlining tour with DMVU, the “Eat My Sass” Tour. Accompanied by the success of his releases “Wook Worm,” “Skrawberries,” “Got Dat” and “Eat My Sass,” along with his collaboration resume with some of the biggest artists in the bass scene, TVBOO will continue to make Category 5 hurricane surge sized waves in the bass music world.

If you only follow TVBOO for his growing discography, you are depriving yourself of understanding the complete TVBOO experience. Like a shot of the strongest espresso you can find, Mitch’s podcast, “TVLKS with TVBOO,” is a straight punch of hilarity, chaos, mild takes and entertaining guests all strung together with his outspoken southern personality. Season 1 featured high-caliber guests such as Kai Wachi, Dirt Monkey, Boogie T, and more.

data.body:
DJ, producer and festival creator Excision (Jeff Abel) resides at the epicenter of an ever-expanding realm of Bass Music. Hailing from Kelowna, British Columbia, Abel is a long-standing pioneer of the North American Dubstep scene, cultivating a massive, passionate community of fans over nearly 20 years of relentless touring. His expansive discography traverses a wide range of bass sounds across five full-length studio albums, countless EPs and singles, and fan-favorite mixes and numerous live recordings, amassing over 2 billion streams globally.

Under Excision’s event production arm Excision Presents, hundreds of thousands of headbangers attend his events each year. He has created world-renowned music festivals Lost Lands, Bass Canyon and Paradise Blue, along with major regional concerts like The Thunderdome of Seattle, The Armory in Minneapolis, Mile High in Denver and more. Wider audiences catch Excision headlining legendary festivals like EDC Las Vegas, Ultra, Tomorrowland, Electric Forest, Lollapalooza, Shambhala and more.

data.body:
Grafix is one of the most respected producers in Drum & Bass, who has spent his career pushing the boundaries of the genre. With a unique sound that blends high-energy dancefloor beats with intricate melodies and driving ethereal, melancholic elements, Grafix has established himself as a true innovator in the scene.
2025 marked a standout year for Grafix, with the release of his second solo album Don’t Slow Down, which has amassed over twelve million streams, featuring six singles and eight album tracks, five of which feature vocals from the likes of Nu-La, Raphaella and WHAT EVA, alongside a highly sought-after limited-edition vinyl release. The title track earned BBC Radio 1’s Hottest Record, complemented by a sold out headline AV album show in London, ‘DSD.AV’, and a run of shows and tours internationally.
As for 2026, Grafix has international dates across Thailand, Switzerland, Netherlands, Slovakia, Czech Republic, Finland, Belgium, Spain, Hungary and USA, as well as a headline tour of Australia and New Zealand. With momentum continuing to build off the back of Don’t Slow Down, Grafix’s new music in 2026 is already highly anticipated.

data.body:
A renown force in the drum and bass scene from the UK. Subsonic has been building his craft from the tender age of 16, and has been going from strength to strength with his electronic music production ever since.

With originals under his belt such as ‘Last Time’ (15 million streams) & ‘Need Me’ (16 million streams) to recent collabs with the likes of Sub Focus, Dimension, Friction & K Motionz - his versatility, creativity and general production quality is being thoroughly showcased. Cementing his status as one of the biggest & most consistent streaming artists in the genre.

Not to mention with his jam packed touring schedule, travelling all across the world including a string of seismic UKF dates.

Subsonics biggest projects to date, are still yet to come. So this is an artist you don’t want to take your eye off of right now.

If you don’t know Subsonic, get to know.

data.body:
The Church of Central Raving is an ecstatic dance throwdown mixed with a local variety show wrapped in a rave survival workshop. Designed around our humming bird attention spans, we deliver the goods with high velocity hilarity. From DNB mist-isms to outrageous hymn-nastics to the 11 Rave Recommendations, we bring you seven years of the best COCR sauce.

This is Hayoka medicine. We believe in radical sincerity, respecting the Land and supporting the People. We honour the Rave Saints by sharing the ways to celebrate with intention and to ride the waves with restraint. If this is your first time at Shambhala this is where you’ll find practical tips to make your journey transformational and safe. If you’re walking the path of the Fest of All this is where you’ll find your community of weirdos.

At the tail end of the Grove’s Opening Ceremony you might wander back through the trees or just wander around to the gate of the Secret Garden. We welcome you to join the Raverend Giovanni Jones Junior and the Church Choir-mistress Tianna J to open this sacred clown space up right. You might find a Party Priest named Water or hear a testimonial from a shiny guy named Tipping. For sure you’ll meet a whole host of our local Shambhala superstars.

We are all on the same grassy stage so get up and dance your ass off or sit back in the shade to witness the glorious chaos. We all praise the rave in our own unique ways.

At the Church of Central Raving we only have three mandates: Be Free. Keep the PRrrrr (Peace and Respect). Help In, Help Out, Help Through.

Can I get a Shambhala!?!?

data.body: Cloak & Dagger are eclectic selectors and collectors known for weaving together sounds from across genres and eras—often within a single set. While their roots lie in dub, their selections move fluidly through jazz, hip-hop, ambient, experimental and electronic, alongside timeless cuts from the ’70s and ’80s. Once strictly vinyl, their approach has since expanded beyond the format while keeping that same deep-digging spirit. The duo also hosts Something That’s Not Nothing, airing every Wednesday from 10pm–12am PST on Kootenay Co-op Radio.

data.body:
Ellyssa Marie - Creatura, is a DJ, Producer, and Label owner based in Berlin & Canada.

Completing a Bachelor in Electronic Production at Catalyst Berlin, she has so far released four soulful albums to date.

Influenced by Thom Yorke, Radiohead, and Lex Amor, she released her latest single this year, LAURA PALMER, an ode to David Lynch and a love of Twin Peaks. Alongside her own work, she recently launched - SECOND SOUL - a label featuring soulful electronic artists from North America & Berlin.

Although her productions hold a more soulful and ambient sound, her mixes are a journey in deep melodic bass, soothing vocals, ÚKG, and slow-burning 140. Recently opening her favourite club in Berlin - Gretchen, and closing the Mushroom stage at Future Forest festival, her career has accelerated as a bass music DJ.

Creatura's career continues to grow in depth while pushing creative boundaries, engaging audiences in her haunting tone.

data.body:
Born and raised amidst the towering redwoods of Kings Mountain, California, and now thriving in Denver, Colorado, CØNTRA crafts a sound deeply rooted in nature and influenced by a life of exploration. With over a decade immersed in Denver’s vibrant bass music culture—one of the richest in the United States—Ezra Arenson (CØNTRA) has honed an organic style that bridges deep dubstep and world bass, that hits hard and resonates deeper, reflecting the sonic diversity of his journey.

CØNTRA’s evolving catalog features releases on prestigious labels such as Deep Dark & Dangerous, DUPLOC, Wheel & Deal, UKF, SubCarbon, Odyzey, Gravitas, and Deadbeats. Drawing comparisons to artists like The Widdler and Pushloop, CØNTRA’s music merges deep, powerful bass with global influences. His tracks are marked by dark, spacious soundscapes that build tension, blending hypnotic low-end frequencies with subtle melodic elements. This unique fusion creates a raw, immersive experience, drawing from the atmospheric depth of dubstep and the intricate design of world bass.

CØNTRA’s dynamic performances have graced some of the most renowned venues and festivals across the country. Most notably festivals like Lightning in a Bottle, Lost Lands, Infrasound, Wicked Woods and The Untz, with standout performances at venues like Meow Wolf and the iconic Cervantes in Denver, his shows leave a lasting impact every time.

data.body: Explore the world with Yelda and Biplov – an Afghan and Nepalese DJ duo inspired by the true-life historic Silk Road that wound its way through Europe, Asia, and Africa. They combine forces to create a journey that showcases instruments and languages from across the world in an effort to spark cultural curiosity and inclusivity. Their musical style favours a variety of South Asian & Middle Eastern vocals combined with rhythmic dance beats & eclectic basslines. Their fluid approach mixes global sounds with Hip Hop, Rap, Amapiano, Afrobeat, House, Jersey Club, Dancehall, Reggaeton, Deep Dub, and other bass-heavy genres.
data.name: The Silk Road

data.body: DRAMA is the genre-blending duo of producer Na’el Shehade and vocalist/songwriter Via Rosa, hailing from Chicago. Known for their signature mix of soulful R&B, dance, and electronic influences, the pair weaves together elements of pop, hip-hop, jazz, and bossa nova into emotionally rich, endlessly danceable tracks. With a fiercely independent spirit and a global fanbase, DRAMA continues to carve out a sound that’s entirely their own.

performances.body: DRAMA is the genre-blending duo of producer Na’el Shehade and vocalist/songwriter Via Rosa, hailing from Chicago. Known for their signature mix of soulful R&B, dance, and electronic influences, the pair weaves together elements of pop, hip-hop, jazz, and bossa nova into emotionally rich, endlessly danceable tracks. With a fiercely independent spirit and a global fanbase, DRAMA continues to carve out a sound that’s entirely their own.

data.body: As Tycho, Scott Hansen blends swirling melodies into vaguely triumphant arcs that crisscross between stuttering beats and vocal samples, creating rolling sonic landscapes that extend into the horizon. The San Francisco based Hansen began releasing music in the early 2000’s with The Science of Patterns EP. His first full-length, Sunrise Projector, came out in 2004, and the critical acclaim continued in 2006 with the release of Past Is Prologue. In 2011 with the addition of collaborator and guitarist Zac Brown, Hansen’s meticulous process saw the release of Dive, the first record in a trilogy that was followed by Awake in 2014 and the GRAMMY-Nominated Epoch in 2016, two albums that featured Rory O’Connor on drums. Hansen expanded the scope of his project with the addition of vocalist Saint Sinner on 2019’s Weather, Tycho’s second GRAMMY nomination for Best Dance/Electronic Album. That release was quickly followed by its instrumental companion record Simulcast, a collaboration with alt-R&B artist rum.gold and the Weather Remixes project all in 2020.  In addition to his original works, Tycho has remixed Odesza & Leon Bridges, Maggie Rogers, Spoon, The Knocks & Muna, Little Dragon, Thievery Corporation and Death Cab For Cutie.

data.body:
Jacques Greene has been making music about the club for over a decade. Across multiple EPs, singles, and two acclaimed full-length albums, Feel Infinite (2017) and Dawn Chorus (2019) his sound has evolved into an emotional, atmospheric haze that balances intimacy with physicality.
In 2020, he released ANTH01, a collection of unearthed early works and previously unreleased tracks that traced the foundations of his musical language.

Alongside his solo work, Greene is one half of Verses GT, a fully realized collaboration with
Nosaj Thing that exists as the group, the album, the live show, and the visual world surrounding it. Built through in-person sessions across London, Los Angeles, Tokyo, Paris, and Montreal, Verses GT is a heavyweight, intentional body of work rooted in human connection, shared headspace, and distilled emotion, featuring contributions from George Riley, Kučka, and TYSON.

Greene also hosts a monthly residency on Rinse FM, now running for over three years, where
he explores deep club music, new material, and long-form mood-driven selections. Beyond his own releases, he has collaborated with Bonobo, remixed Radiohead, produced for Jessy Lanza, arlo parks, Tinashe, and toured internationally with The xx, continually refining a sound that is both deeply personal and built for collective experience.

data.body:
The music of Cameo Blush resides in the inquisitive, genre-melding spaces in between.

Melding electronic music with live instruments, Cameo Blush’s music moves freely between genres across a string of EPs, refining a style that prioritises punchy production, chopped-up vocals and “drums that are interesting enough to tell a story by themselves”, now the London musician presents ‘Just Concentrate’: a giant leap forward both in scope and concept.

Structured to mirror the experience of flicking through radio stations on a late night drive, across the EP’s dusky transmissions you’ll find hip hop-leaning beats (‘All In Good Time’) and straight up bangers (‘4me’); trip-hop nods (‘Whatever Happens’), happy hardcore (‘Just Concentrate’) and forays into ambient territory (‘Still In Time’), all held together by Cameo Blush’s production lens. Throughout, crackles and snippets of radio voice-over tie the whole piece together, with the presenter introducing ‘All In Good Time’ with “Your listening to Just Concentrate Radio, the only radio specialising in Existential Dread, direct to your ears”.
The result is a project that’s highly stylised but also loose and free-sounding; that embraces a world of possibility much like its author, leaving the door wide open for where he could go next.
data.name: Cameo Blush

data.body:
Since 2018, Charles Sahm, known as Player Dave, has evolved his artistic project from a heavier electronic sound to a refined dance-driven electronic sound that fuses garage and breakbeat rhythms with immersive, atmospheric textures. His tracks balance raw energy with introspection, offering a sound that feels equally at home on a dancefloor or in a reflective late-night listen.

His release history is a story in its own. From his debut Album Where the Wind Blew me, to the breath of fresh air you find in his unique singles with vocals from himself, Georgie O’Brien, JAWNZA, & Elohim. Player Dave’s forthcoming music is nothing short of its own individual sound.

Fresh off of direct support for Jacques Greene & Nosaj Things Tour, Player Dave is set to headline multiple standalone shows across the US.

Over the years, Player Dave has played numerous headline shows at notable venues like Elsewhere in New York, The Ground in Miami, Spin in San Diego, and more, and has shared festival lineups with artists like Joy Orbison, Sammy Virji, Sara Landry, Skee Mask, and many alike in the experimental dance lane.

With festival appearances at Coachella, Hard Summer, iii points, Okeechobee, EDSEA, Bass Coast, Shambhala, and other multi-genre festival lineups, Player Dave has stood out on a ROS with his range in his musical style.

Additionally, Player Dave launched Daisy Chain Recordings three years ago, a label and party series showcasing some of the best eclectic club music in the U.S. This year, Daisy Chain Recordings hosted a full label takeover at Lightning in a Bottle, marking Player Dave’s third appearance at the festival. This focus on aligning with the brand’s vision ensures that every performance is meaningful and represents a dedication to quality and tastefulness in the music scene.

data.body:
Originally from Bristol, Jubba will be on shutdown duties at the Secret Garden on Sunday night. Bringing his signature blend of reggae influenced UK party bangers.

Over the past 2 years, Jubba has turned the Secret Garden into his Shambhala home. Running the sound and managing the stage, he has helped transform the Secret Garden into Shambhala's hidden gem.

Except dancehall, UKG and DnB. Gunfingers are mandatory.

data.body:
VCTRE (pronounced “vector”) is the stage name of Aaron Pugh, an American electronic music producer and DJ known primarily for his work in dubstep, bass music, and glitch influenced electronic music. He has been active since around 2017 and has built a reputation for pushing genre boundaries through detailed sound design and emotionally driven compositions. Originally from Birmingham, Alabama, VCTRE has released music on respected labels within the bass music scene, including Deadbeats, Bassrush Records, and Monstercat to name a few.
VCTRE’s sound is defined by a balance of heaviness and atmosphere. His productions often feature aggressive low end bass, intricate rhythmic structures, and layered synth textures, paired with melodic and emotional undertones. This combination allows his music to function both as high energy live material and as deeply immersive listening experiences. His work draws influence from dubstep, heavy metal, hip-hop, and progressive rock, resulting in tracks that range from dark and aggressive to introspective and melodic.
In short, there is no sound or genre that binds VCTRE to its rules.
VCTRE’s discography includes albums, EPs, and singles that chart his artistic growth. He has released two full-length albums: Palindrome & East Kings Point (Palindrome considered his breakthrough project). Both albums are noted for their emotional depth and refined production. Reciprocity, Die Happy, and Duskborne are three EP's that further explore his experimental sound design and stylistic range.
In addition to studio releases, VCTRE has performed at legendary venues such as Red Rocks and major festivals such as Okeechobee, supporting and sharing stages with other prominent artists of all genres. His live sets emphasize heavy bass impact while maintaining the intricate sound design presented in his recorded work.
VCTRE is widely regarded as an innovative producer within the experimental bass community. He is recognized for his attention to detail in sound design, emotional storytelling through music, and willingness to explore outside traditional genre boundaries. His work has translated to both underground listeners and larger commercial audiences alike. It is hard to go to a festival nowadays and not hear his music being played.

asáu is a Seattle-based artist with support from artists like Skream & Benga, Of The Trees, Zingara, LYNY, and Eazybaked at events such as Boiler Room, Lost Lands, Electric Forest, and Shambhala.

Inspired by artists like Tsuruda and Mr. Carmack, asáu creates various styles of bass music, ranging from the heavy-hitting trap action in ‘Bender’ to left-field distortion in ‘Doom Scrolling.’

asáu’s discography includes dozens of tracks and releases on labels such as DPMO, Jadū Dala, and Dome of Doom. He’s also released two EPs: Burn Notice and Definitely Not Trap (with Golden Child).

data.body:
atebaki brings to the stage an ever evolving universe, weaving sounds inspired by flow states and melodies found in dreams.

With an aim to create an immersive experience for the listener, each track unfolds like an origami of emotional states, blooming into atebaki’s unique fusion of deep low-end, futuristic synths and soulful melodies.

atebaki’s soundscape is nectar for the senses, capturing the dance floor inside a bubble, ready to make bodies flow and spirits wander.

With releases on Gravitas Recordings, Subciety Presents, Cimmerian Records and UnderGround Sound, atebaki has been pushing the boundaries of the downtempo side of bass music. His search for a deeper connection with his community led him to organize inner space: a downtempo night focused on deep bass, immersive art installations and live performances. The motto? come to dance, come to chill, come to unwind.

data.body:
Hip-hop with drops; dynamic vocals and hard-hitting beats. For years, tastemakers like Headbang Society have said that Torbjørn is “giving both rap fans and bass music fans a reason to put their hands up,” and his new Tonmaling EP is no exception. Having been awarded the Tacoma Artist Initiative Project for 2025, Tonmaling blends explosive sounds of North American dubstep, high-energy hip-hop/rap, and traditional Scandinavian folk music. This bombastic fusion has been met with both critical acclaim and packed dance floors
throughout North America and Europe.

data.body:
KNOW GOOD is the product of two kindred spirits bound by both blood and bass. Cousins Tanner and Sylas Morgan are the latest in a family legacy of musicians, crafting a genre-defying sound that’s as dark and dynamic as it is unexpected. Their music pulses with low frequencies woven through pop, rock, and rap elements.
Both veterans in their own right, Sylas has spent the past decade DJing nightclubs and bars across the U.S., known for his taste in sample selection and sharp verses, while Tanner brings versatility as a vocalist and multi-instrumentalist, mastering over ten instruments.
Since joining forces in 2020, KNOW GOOD has created a fresh sound that’s quickly gaining traction. Their live performances blend the intensity of a DJ set with the richness of live instrumentation, incorporating drums, guitar, violin, and vocals. Set to tour nationally in 2025, KNOW GOOD is making waves with a style that’s as dynamic as it is innovative, captivating listeners and earning spins from the world’s top DJs.

data.body:
INZO, a dynamic electronic artist based in Denver, is celebrated for his genre-defying sound that seamlessly blends luminous synths, thunderous basslines, and nostalgic vocal samples to evoke powerful emotions. Born Michael Inzano in Chicago, he cultivated a rich musical foundation through classical piano and drums before transitioning from a metal band to electronic production during college. His breakthrough came with the 2017 single “Visionaries,” which cemented his unique artistic identity, followed by the hit “Overthinker” and his acclaimed debut EP, Multiverse.

Since then, INZO has embarked on multiple North American tours, collaborated with leading industry artists, and headlined prominent festivals and venues. His discography continues to grow with compelling releases such as Visionquest (2024) and the collaborative album NEXUM (2025) with Blookah. Known for his innovative sound and captivating live performances, INZO remains at the forefront of the electronic music scene.

data.body:
Liquid Stranger has never fit a mold — instead, he's spent his career melting them. Widely regarded as the pioneering architect of freeform bass, Martin Stääf has spent decades cultivating a global reputation as one of the most innovative and unpredictable artists in electronic music. His legacy, founded upon the cornerstones of instinct, exploration, and an unshakeable belief in the transformative power of sound, transcends conventional trends and pulses through the liminal moments where music shifts something elemental within us.
Since his earliest days producing music in Sweden, his fascination with sound as a storytelling medium has guided his creative evolution. From introspective downtempo meditations to explosive, floor-splintering bass, Liquid Stranger's discography exists as an archive of uncharted sonic territory; a sprawling map etched in feeling and frequency. His creations don't adhere to genre classifications, but ignite something raw and visceral. At the helm of bass music's most dynamic movement, Liquid Stranger turns sound into a shared human experience.
It was the same uncompromising vision that gave rise to both WAKAAN and SSKWAN; imagined not as traditional record labels, but as refuges for radical artistic expression. Together, they manifest the belief that music is an expansive, multidimensional language — one that acts as a powerful vessel for connection. This philosophy ultimately materialized on a larger scale with the introduction of WAKAAN Music Festival, a rare space where experimentation is encouraged and the unexpected is always welcome. For those who seek the unknown, Liquid Stranger is both the conduit and invitation.
A true voyager of sound, Liquid Stranger's celebrated history has spanned continents and generations. From underground European clubs to international festival stages and revered U.S. venues including Red Rocks Amphitheater and WAMU Theater, his performances leave no audience unchanged. Every set is a reminder that music isn't just a product for entertainment — it's alchemy, and Liquid Stranger is its master craftsman.
What Liquid Stranger has built stretches far beyond the framework of a career — it’s a living, breathing ecosystem where boundaries dissolve, curiosity is celebrated, and the strange becomes sacred. In an ever-shifting landscape, Liquid Stranger remains a timeless figure outside of the margins; not as an outsider, but as the gravitational pull itself, drawing others into orbit where collective experience takes root in the invisible current between every note.

data.body:
With billions of streams to his name, Whethan is a dynamic artist and producer with multiple gold and platinum certifications. By 18, he took the dance scene by storm, executive-produced rising star Oliver Tree, and orchestrated one of Coachella’s iconic moments with viral craze Mason Ramsey. Whethan’s DJ project has featured global artists (Dua Lipa, Portugal the Man, NLE Choppa, Rico Nasty, Yeat, MAX) and built momentum for almost a decade - currently with 7+ million monthly listeners and single Money on the Dash independently charting, which is now a RIAA gold certified record and charted on UK Dance and Billboard Dance Charts in 2024.

A robust live presence complements his streaming success - with multiple appearances at marquee festivals and venues like Red Rocks, Ultra, and Lollapalooza in his hometown of Chicago. Whethan has also produced close to 100 songs for other artists, including household names Joji, Meg Thee Stallion, Kygo, X Ambassadors, etc., and full albums for Oliver Tree, The Chainsmokers, glaive, and ericdoa. He also thrives in sync, with 50+ placements across the landscape of sports, TV, film, and commercials (Apple, Euphoria, Fifty Shades of Grey, Sony Playstation, NFL). At only 24, Whethan's successes fuel his ceaseless ambitions as a producer - with a multifaceted skill set to excel in numerous genres and a unique ability to blend them, whether for other artists' projects or his own.

In 2024, Whethan gave fans a long-awaited return to form. Life of a Wallflower, Vol. 2 acts as more than just a nod to his roots, but a showcase of his growth from then to now. LOAW2 highlights the reflection and maturation of the sound that made Whethan what he is today. In 2024, he stopped in seven key cities across the country for his LOAW2 Tour.

In February 2025, after 10 years, Whethan officially released the song that changed his life forever: MssingNo XE3 (Whethan Turn). This release relaunched a new era, focusing on bass and dubstep remixes. Earlier this year, he released his WAREHOUSE.WAVS EP that includes a number of remixes such as Swag Surfin’, No Broke Boys, Sticky, Blick Sum, Nissan Altima and In Da Club. Since his resurgence into the bass and dubstep scene, Whethan’s socials have had exponential growth with over 90K new followers on Instagram and 170K on TikTok.


On the live side in 2025, Whethan drew in massive crowds with surprise sets at Electric Forest and Lightning in a Bottle, and packed out underground pop-ups in Atlanta, LA, and NYC, all of which reached capacity in under 30 minutes. He did a Halloween festival run, performing at Escape, Hulaween, Mississippi Underground, and Get Freaky SLC. On top of all of that, he sold out all of his announced headline shows in less than 24 hrs on presale in Chicago (Ramova Theatre), LA (The Palladium), NYC (Webster Hall), SF (The Midway), Denver (Cervantes’ Masterpiece Ballroom), and Toronto (Noir). He also performed at both Decadence and To The Future.


In 2026, Whethan has maintained a festival-heavy touring strategy while sprinkling in select headline and shows. He will be playing festivals including EDC Las Vegas, Lollapalooza, Electric Forest, Shambhala Music Festival, Seven Stars and Veld Music Festival, while also tapping into bass-focused events like Forbidden Kingdom Festival and Wobbleland. Around these, he played a handful one-off shows at the Do LaB stage weekend 1 of Coachella and direct support for Crankdat at CRANKDOME Tacoma. On top of all of this, Whethan has his own headline tour rolling out with 25 stops in major markets across the US throughout the year and will be direct support for Zeds Dead in Montreal this summer. Overall, the year reflects a strong presence on the touring side with strategic headline stops and festival looks, reinforcing his position in the upper tier of touring electronic acts.

data.body: Jkyl & Hyde is a project defined by contrast. Rooted in the tension between chaos and control, beauty and intensity, the sound spans the full emotional and sonic spectrum. From emotionally charged melodic anthems to uncompromising heavy-hitting drops, Jkyl & Hyde delivers music that resonates in the heart and hits in the chest. With 50 million+ views across social platforms and recent official remixes for huge records like “Put It Down” and “Borderline”, the project has become a consistent source of high-impact, highly demanded music. Whether crafting cinematic, vocal-driven moments or explosive festival weapons, Jkyl & Hyde brings a distinct identity to everything he touches. His music is regularly supported by top-tier artists including Zeds Dead, Marshmello, Excision, LSDream, and Subtronics, and he continues to connect with a rapidly growing global audience. At the center of his expanding ecosystem is HYDDEN GEMS RADIO, a monthly platform that highlights rising talent and exclusive material through expertly curated, genre-fluid mixes. The show reflects his broader vision: to elevate the underground while pushing the boundaries of modern bass. Jkyl & Hyde stands at the intersection of artistry and impact, offering a fresh and fearless take on bass music. With each release, he continues to shape the future of the the scene, one track at a time.

data.body: Eclectic is an understatement when trying to describe the sound of Civillian. While  always staying true to his roots in Hip Hop, his taste and collection have expanded  massively to allow him to become a party-rocking chameleon - no situation outside of  his scope. Beside his proven taste and wide range in selection, his ability to work in  elements of Turntablism into a dance-floor friendly set is what separates him from other  DJ’s. Whether it’s the main stage, an impromptu sunrise set, house party or  underground rave, Civillian is always prepared.

data.body:
Tone Ranger crafts infectious dance music rooted in the mysterious beauty of the American
Southwest. A composer, producer, and sought-after collaborator, he draws on a decade of wanderings through desert plateaus, remote forests, and multicultural borderlands to create soundscapes that are at once ancient and futuristic.

Since his 2018 debut EP, Tone Ranger has become one of the most distinctive artists in the Southwest. From opening slots for electronic icons Orbital and Thievery Corporation to appearances at Shambhala and Burning Man, he has forged a reputation for transporting crowds into vivid sonic landscapes.

data.body:
Lion S, also known as Sara Spicer, is a respected pillar of the West Coast underground electronic music scene. With over 25 years of dedication, her journey began in the mid-90s and has since included curating sets and stages in renowned spaces—most notably The Living Room at Shambhala Music Festival. Her deep-rooted connection to music from a young age continues to influence her broad, genre-fluid style. Whether behind the decks or curating an experience, Lion-S is known for delivering meticulously tailored sets that align with the energy of the venue and the moment, all while radiating a confident, feminine stage presence.

Sara’s passion for her craft extends beyond performance. As a successful entrepreneur, she’s deeply involved in nurturing the dance music community across Canada and internationally. Her work as an event producer and curator is driven by a genuine love for supporting fellow artists—DJs, producers, and performers alike. Behind the decks, she thrives on the dynamic exchange with the dance floor, shaping sets organically in response to the crowd.

A founding member of Meowmix, the iconic all-female DJ crew from Nelson, BC, Sara has been instrumental in creating space and visibility for women in the industry for over two decades. She also collaborates with her brother Birdriot under the moniker Nür Tür, producing deep bass frequencies and immersive soundscapes that reflect her holistic connection to music as both and expressive art form and transformative medium for healing

data.body:
A longtime skateboarder and hip hop devotee, Geoff “Stylust” Reich has always been a rebel with a passion for his art form, so it’s no surprise that this Canadian DJ/producer’s talent for fusing hip hop flavor and an endless energy with heavy bass has earned him the rep as a creative renegade carving his own path.
Stylust’s uncanny ability to melt together multiple influences and genres has led to music that’s reached millions of listeners, earning him collabs with artists including Ganja White Night, Of The Trees, Truth, and Buku - and releases with labels such as Deadbeats, Deep Dark & Dangerous, Subcarbon, and Bassrush.
Geoff’s keen sense for eclectic, yet cohesive beats has also translated to the stage, building him a reputation for delivering a dynamic, standout set every time with past tours across the United States, Canada, and Europe, and appearances at major festivals such as Coachella, Shambhala, Outlook, LIB, and Envision. With a bass-heavy yet timeless sound, Stylust electrifies both the dancefloor, as well as headphones with his impeccable taste and rebellious energy.

data.body:
Based in the heart of Kelowna’s vibrant scene, JGirl & Manousos are a powerhouse defined by their deep-rooted passion and an undeniable synergy. With a lifelong affinity to House music, they have spent decades immersing themselves in the genre’s evolving landscape, cultivating a sound that is both nostalgic and cutting-edge.

What sets them apart is their exceptional selection. Their sets are a masterclass in versatility that seamlessly blends Deep & Soulful cuts, which lay the foundation with sophisticated and rhythmic texture, Classic Anthems that pay homage to the roots that sparked their initial love for the culture, and modern Tech-House that injects quirky, driving beats. The duo is known for their "crate-digging" mentality so expect a few hidden gems that you won't hear anywhere else.

Whether they are performing at sun-drenched festival sites or intimate city spaces, JGirl & Manousos bring an energy that reflects their genuine love for the craft. Their reputation in the Okanagan and beyond is built on a simple promise: a sophisticated, high-vibe musical experience that resonates long after the final track fades.

data.body:
A curator of Genre-Bending Soundscapes

With over a decade of experience behind the decks, DeepStrut is a force to be reckoned with. A mixing style that transcends genres, DeepStrut crafts unpredictable and electrifying sets that keep crowds eagerly engaged, anticipating what’s coming next. Whether it’s soulful grooves, hard-hitting bangers, or experimental bass music, you can expect the unexpected.

Beyond the DJ booth, you may have spotted DeepStrut behind the scenes, orchestrating stunning lighting and visuals as part of Wax Candy's legendary productions. Combining technical mastery with a deep love for the craft, DeepStrut creates immersive experiences in all aspects of the BC music scene.

data.body: Coming from the beautiful Slocan Valley British Columbia. His journey on the turntables began in 2003 in small town in southern Ontario. In 2006 he landed in Nelson British Columbia, what he found was a thriving music scene which felt like the perfect place to fully explore and express his love music. His flow on the turntables creates a positive atmosphere that moves the floor every time.  Fattycakes has been immersed in the Kootenay music scene for the past 15 years. He loves playing all the dope venues that the Kootenay’s have the offer, whether it’s small and intimate or party rocking nightclub vibes, His whole life revolves around digging for music, he’s always forging for the goods that make the dance floor bounce. Fattycakes is true story teller expect to hear a smooth blend of Funk, Hip Hop, House and D&B and everything in between with fat rolling baselines that are lightly garnished with a bit of turntablism for good measure.

data.body: Charly Hustle is an award-winning DJ from Saskatoon, SK. The 2015 Red Bull Thre3style Canadian champion and World Finalist is known to use his dexterous skill and creativity to make a dance party in ANY situation. He's got a penchant for taking left turns at the right time. Any genre is fair game so long as it makes the crowd (and him) smile and dance.

data.body:
The Gaff is a lifelong DJ with tight skills & cuts, a lengthy list of accomplishments, tonnes of dope records, heart & class. One foot in the future, and one foot in the past. The Gaff's purpose is to elevate Hip Hop, Dance, Club, and Electronic Sounds to a level that can be easily combined with Funk, Soul & Jazz.

The result is amazing live performances & instrumental productions, giving a story of music that ebbs and flows. The Gaff has rocked parties and clubs worldwide: either by all vinyl 45 sets, or destroying it with tight scratching, live drumming on the sampler, and turntable kung fu. A teacher of the essentials, peers and fans alike are often caught overlooking The Gaff's shoulder to see just what it is he’s up to.

data.body:
Starjunk 95 is centered around a fictional, anime-inspired crew of ragtag misfits that join forces to pilot an intergalactic radio station. Think Cowboy Bebop meets The Gorillaz & Daft Punk. Each crew member represents a different sound and style of music, and together they transmit the newest sounds of today drenched with vintage nostalgia of yesterday.
Each song showcases a dynamic mix of colorful, genre-bending music and a playful pallette of rapping, pop vocals, turntable scratching, & groovy basslines. Starjunk 95 has crafted a unique & recognizable sound that is resonating deeply with fans across the world (and galaxy) Every music release coincides with a chapter of the story - painting a snapshot for listeners into the universe that has been created around Starjunk 95.

data.body: Montreal natives Dave 1 and P-Thugg have turned their childhood friendship into a 20-year musical career, launching them from Montreal basements to main stages worldwide. Through six critically acclaimed studio albums, Chromeo are credited for pioneering the 80s-tinged electrofunk sound that permeates radio airwaves today. Known for their analog synths, infectious hooks and iconic visuals, Chromeo are also a relentless live act: 2023 marked their fifth performance at Coachella (they’ve also played Lollapalooza and Bonnaroo four times) and 2024 capped a 150-date touring cycle for their latest release, Adult Contemporary. Last Fall, the duo returned to their roots and performed their seminal record Fancy Footwork front to back for six sold-out nights. Expect new music from the Funklordz this year, and for years to come.

data.body: A-Trak, a.k.a. Alain Macklovitch, is the quintessential cultural connector. His 25-year career has seen him take turns as an internationally renowned DJ, World Champion turntablist, founder of New York’s celebrated Fool’s Gold Records, documentary filmmaker, and half of the Grammy nominated duo Duck Sauce as well as The Brothers Macklovitch (with his brother Dave 1 of Chromeo). He continues to reshape the very role of the DJ as tastemaker and storyteller.

In the dynamic realm of electronic dance music, few acts have made as profound an impact‬
‭ as the unstoppable force that is Solardo. Comprising the dynamic duo, Mark Richards and‬
‭ James Eliot, Solardo continues to redefine the boundaries of house and techno, captivating‬
‭ audiences worldwide with their infectious beats and unparalleled energy.‬
‭ As 2024 unfolds, Solardo stands at the forefront of the global electronic music scene. With a‬ career spanning over a decade, Mark and James have evolved into an iconic powerhouse,‬
‭ consistently pushing the envelope of what's possible in the world of dance music. Their sonic‬ odyssey has seen them rise from the underground to become a household name, headlining‬ the world's most prestigious festivals and clubs.‬
‭ Solardo's chart-topping success is a testament to their production prowess. Their releases‬
‭ consistently dominate the charts, with tracks like Move Your Body with Marshall Jefferson,‬
‭ Big Talk with Idris Elba & XTC with Eli Brown‬‭ becoming‬‭ anthems for electronic music‬
‭ enthusiasts. In 2024, they continue to shape the sound of the future with a fusion of‬
‭ cutting-edge beats and innovative production techniques.‬

data.body:
Neon Steve has carved out a reputation as one of Canada’s most influential House Music artists, amassing over 50 million streams worldwide. Hailing from the Pacific Northwest, he holds the title of Beatport’s top-selling Canadian Bass House artist of all time. His self-described sound—“House Music That Bangs!”—pushes boundaries with a high-energy blend of Tech House, Bass House, Techno, and beyond.

Known for explosive tracks and undeniable dancefloor appeal, Neon Steve’s releases on top-tier labels have earned consistent support from global heavyweights including Kaskade, RL Grime, Tchami, Malaa, Dr. Fresch, Dillon Francis, Zeds Dead, Matroda, and AC Slater. His music continues to thrive across streaming platforms and social media, with viral moments and fan-favorite remixes helping fuel his growing influence in the scene.

Loaded with an arsenal of originals, collaborations and remixes lined up for release in the coming months, 2026 is looking to be yet another big year for Neon Steve

data.body:
Starting his career in an underground New York-inspired café in his original hometown of Brisbane, Australia, to his first International appearance at Shambhala's Fractal Forest in 2009, continues to refine his skills, experimenting and educating himself and his fans with mash-up productions of classic hip hop, ingrained with rare and funky, rhythmically savvy grooves.
With the overwhelming support and love from the world’s most respected funky breaks producers behind him, Slynk's reputation has been growing strong with numerous self-releases and with established labels Westwood Recordings, Jalapeno Records, Goodgroove, and Discocakes, just to name a few.
His timeless nu-funk, electro-boogie bravado has met with overwhelming enthusiasm. His Fractal Forest sets brought his unstoppable Forward Momentum to a new level that not only constitutes his best credentials today but also consecrated his name to the West Coast Funk Nation.
Nothing more than another step in spreading his instant, smile-provoking, supreme '80s-funk-flavored boogie, Slynk is now based in Vancouver, British Columbia, to complete his conquering of the New World and, as an Ambassador of Funk.

data.body: El Toro is a versatile selector with deep roots in the Kootenay bass scene, he's known for blending UKG, Dubstep, Trap, and Hip-Hop with smooth precision and a deep sense of flow. With a lifelong connection to soundsystem culture, El Toro has built a style that’s equal parts grounded and exploratory—honouring the roots of bass music while always pushing into fresh territory. Known for thoughtful track selection and a genuine love for the music, El Toro crafts immersive experiences that resonate on and off the dance floor.

data.body:
Self-described "Brass House" trio Too Many Zooz make manically kinetic instrumental music that combines avant-garde jazz, EDM, punk rock, and sundry other traditions into their own distinctive brand of high-energy dance music. A viral sensation since they first emerged as New York subway buskers in 2013, Too Many Zooz have evolved into a globally recognized phenomenon who tour often and produce YouTube videos that have garnered millions of views.
Formed in 2013 in New York City, Too Many Zooz features the talents of baritone saxophonist Leo "Leo P" Pellegrino, trumpeter Matt "Doe" Muirhead, and drummer David "King of Sludge" Parks. Pellegrino and Muirhead initially met while students at the Manhattan School of Music where they studied jazz. They eventually joined forces with percussionist Parks, whom Pellegrino had played with in a separate busking outfit. They began playing live at various New York subway stations and quickly attracted crowds with their aggressive instrumental sound and Pellegrino's ability to dance and hype the audience like a DJ while playing. In 2014, a video of the band went viral and helped set the stage for the group’s meteoric rise.
In 2016, the trio released their debut full-length album, Subway Gawdz, which featured guest spots from Kreayshawn, Armani White, members of Galactic, and Beats Antique. That same year they were featured on Beyoncé’s Lemonade album, and performed with Beyonce and The Dixie Chicks at the CMA’s.
Over the course of the next several years, Too Many Zooz continued to release EP’s and singles - including ZombiEP (2019), a concept album about a fictional band playing a music festival during a zombie outbreak, plus collaborations and remixes with The Floozies, Beats Antique, Spencer Ludwig, Moon Hooch, Dot Cromwell, Nitty Scott, Lester London, and RoRo.
All of that set the stage for the band’s 2024 sophomore album Retail Therapy - a 13-song chronicle of auditory subway cinema that features appearances from Moon Hooch, Thumpasaurus, Lucky Chops, and Too Many T’s.

data.body:
Best known for his wide array of genre-bending song interpretations and improvisations, Mark Woodyard uses looping machines to harness guitars, vocals, trumpet, beat machines, and sub bass sounds that create a style unique to him. Looping every layer on the spot, Mark explores funky house, disco, hip hop, and genre-less absurdities.

Born and raised in North Vancouver, Mark Woodyard is no rookie to the live music scene having played live for the better part of the last two decades reaching some of the worlds most unique festival stages including BassCoast, Shambhala, and Envision.

You can also catch Mark streaming sets on Twitch; taking requests of all genres and improvising songs from scratch and using the chat to create an interactive creative experience with audience.

data.body:
PRSN is a longstanding DJ and event curator based in Portland, OR. As a key mind behind Wake the Town, a decade-long event series centered on contemporary bass music, PRSN has helped shine light on both emerging talent and established artists from around the world.

Earlier this year, PRSN launched Pockets alongside longtime friend Annu, which leans on self-expression, provides a platform to mix their bag of design aesthetics, and celebrates the evolving landscapes of global club music.

With his ongoing work, PRSN has made a vital impact within the local and regional dance communities. There’s an underlying soul to the dance floors he cultivates and his flow and crate-digging sensibilities as a DJ are deeply compelling.

data.body:
EARTHGANG is a unique rap duo that has been making an impact on the music industry since their formation in 2008. Comprised of Olu and WowGr8, the pair met as freshmen at Mays High School in Southwest Atlanta and were deeply influenced by their surroundings. Their music is a fusion of various genres, including R&B, jazz, gospel, and funk, which they use to create innovative albums, experimental EPs, and masterful Mixtapes.

With a name rooted in the idea of bringing people together, EARTHGANG has gained a
massive following, with more than 600 thousand followers on Instagram, 3 Million monthly
listeners on Spotify, and 127,507,652 views on YouTube. After signing with J. Cole's Dreamville Records in 2017, EARTHGANG's popularity grew even further, with their music reaching the top of the Billboard charts.

EARTHGANG's commitment to community activism, supporting emerging artists, and
dedication to pushing boundaries in their music has earned them critical acclaim and a devoted fanbase. Their sound continues to evolve, with each project showcasing their eclectic rhythm that cannot be placed in a box or attached to any one genre.

data.body:
V.I.V.E.K is a cornerstone of contemporary UK bass music. A producer, selector and soundman whose work is defined by precision, depth and an unshakeable commitment to soundsystem heritage. Since the early 2000s he’s been quietly but consistently shaping the landscape, channeling the weight and warmth of classic dub lineage into future-minded low-end architecture.

His releases across System Music, Deep Medi, Tectonic and reggae institution Greensleeves trace a sound that refuses easy categorisation: meditative pressure, steppy swing, dub atmospheres and stripped-back hypnosis built for big rigs and late-night spaces.Every element is engineered with intention, reflecting his lifelong study of frequency and detail.

That same ethos drives SYSTEM, the custom-built soundsystem he introduced as a new chapter in the UK’s longstanding tradition of DIY rig culture. Quickly becoming one of the most influential modern stacks in the country, SYSTEM has become a gathering point for heads, selectors and engineers from across the bass continuum.

On the decks, V.I.V.E.K is a deep digger with a singular ear, drawing for dubs, rarities and connections that span decades of underground lineage. His sets are immersive, patient and uncompromising, a reminder of how powerful and transportive system-rooted music can be in the right hands.

Respected for his craft, loved for his consistency and trusted for his vision, V.I.V.E.K remains one of the genre’s most vital figures. He doesn’t just operate within soundsystem culture, he actively extends it.

data.body: ATYYA (pronounced ah-tie-ah) is the project of Canadian producer, DJ, and music production educator Tyy Clark. His sound pushes the bounds of UK bass, UKG, dubstep, & rap, with an emphasis on deep, innovative basslines, hypnotic percussive grooves, and forward thinking sound design. ATYYA is well respected in the scene with over a decade of professional experience as an internationally touring act and over 10M streams across 140+ songs. ATYYA’s fans have much to look forward to this year with an array of new music releases, live mixes, and tour dates, supporting ATYYA’s motive to bring people together through music.

data.body: Raised on amens, Reese basslines and ragga chat in Toronto's vibrant jungle scene, Dubconscious now calls Nelson his home where he continues to weave cohesive sound tapestries coloured by his unique influences and handpicked sounds & styles - generally rooted in reggae / dub / soundsystem culture. You may have caught a Dubconscious set at Shambhala (either on the Living Room Stage, in the Grove or at The Village), Bass Coast, Wicked Woods, Victoria Ska & Reggae Festival, WEMF (Ontario), Dragon Festival (Spain) or at any one of a number of prestigious nightclubs/venues across western Canada.

data.body:
UK-born and British Columbia-based producer and DJ Gl0bal (Mickhel Patterson) has evolved from a bass music powerhouse into one of Canada’s most influential and fastest-rising DJ personalities.

Breaking through with hard-hitting dubstep and trap releases on some of the world’s leading bass labels, Gl0bal earned support from DJ Snake, Tiësto, Hardwell, The Chainsmokers, and Major Lazer, while landing major festival appearances including Lost Lands and Shambhala, along with multiple nominations for Electronic Artist of the Year at the Western Canadian Music Awards.

In recent years, he has taken social media by storm — generating over 100 million monthly views through high-energy DJ performances, mashups, and viral transitions, building one of the largest DJ-driven audiences globally. His sets now seamlessly fuse bass, Afro-house, Afrobeats, dancehall, and global club sounds — a reflection of his UK roots and international reach.

With over 10 million Spotify streams and a catalog that bridges underground credibility with global impact, Gl0bal represents the modern evolution of DJ culture — where influence, energy, and production collide.

As DJ Mag puts it,

“If all of his drops are this relentless, it’s only a matter of time before Gl0bal has the whole earth quaking.”

data.body:
Rob Paine’s passion for music runs deep. From the moment he fell in love with music as a youth, he knew what his life’s journey would be, to bring people together through music. That passion has provided the foundation for a diverse and eclectic musical immersion ever since. He has been on a rapid rise from bedroom mix tapes to underground events to DJing internationally since he started out in 1993. He, along with his Circle Production crew (Kevin Gimble, Keith Landis, and Sean Thomas), created and curated some of the most epic raves & club nights in Philadelphia throughout the 90’s. In parallel, he began to flex his music theory education from Temple University and production expertise by creating a broad range of songs across a myriad of genres such as house, techno, reggae, broken beat, dub, and down-tempo.

Rob formed WORSHIP RECORDINGS in 1998 alongside partners Dan Thompson and Zack Eberz, focusing on the dub aspect of house & techno and weaving in the techniques used by King Tubby & Lee ’Scratch’ Perry into modern dance music. During that time, he and Zack began using the SOLOMONIC SOUND name for their productions and as a vehicle for releasing their dub-influenced house and techno tracks on Worship, as well as running a traditional reggae sound system under the same moniker. (Please see Solomonic Sound’s full bio for all if his work with in the reggae community)

Worship Recordings has had the pleasure of releasing music by a heap of talented & successful top-billed producers from likes of Garth & Eric James, King Britt, Håkan Lidbo, Jay Tripwire, Pete Moss, Grant Dell, Universal Agents, Adam Tenenbaum (Nectar, Succulent aliases), Joey Youngman aka Wolfgang Gartner (Frequent Flyer alias), Gary Beck, Hipp-e, Rithma, Seinan, Rootz Underground, Army Of One & Rick Preston.

Rob also released as a solo artist and collaborated with Wamdue Kids’ Chris Udoh for early Worship releases. Now, with his passion reinforced by the labels’ success and critical industry acclaim, Rob’s unique sounds were soon being released on labels such as Large, Tango, Shaboom, and Grayhound to underground and commercial success. In addition, his productions were also licensed to labels such as Bedrock, Fabric, and NRK as part of compilations.

During this time, another canvas was unveiled as a tapestry to showcase the Worship Recordings productions and artists: the SHAKEDOWN parties. Rob, Willyum (Will Putney), and Zack Eberz launched the monthly Shakedown event in October of 2001 and they are still going strong today 23 years later. Evidence that music – done right - is enduring. Rob’s events always feature incredible sound via the “Solomonic Sound System” that he and his crew developed and have fine-tuned to audiophile quality for discerning DJs and dancers. That same uniqueness and quality is evident in his custom-designed studio, which is instantly apparent when you hear Rob’s production and remix work.

The world has been the recipient of Rob’s talents through his performances all across the U.S., Canada, Mexico, Jamaica, Japan and London in iconic clubs/parties such as Fabric, PS1, Wicked and Deep Space. Rob has also held down residencies in New York, Washington D.C., San Francisco, and Miami, and has played at festivals such as the massive Shambhala & Soundwave festivals in British Columbia. In 2012, Rob and Francisco Collazo established ‘goodie’… a series of special monthly underground events highlighting the facets of house & techno while hosting local and international talent.

Collaborations, productions, remixes, and releases continue to abound for Rob on labels such as Worship Recordings, LeftRoom, I Recordings, Om Recordings, Hallucienda, Yoruba, Doin’ Work, Dubwise, ZamZam Sounds, Kaato and on compilations like one done by Maceo Plex in 2013. He has done remixes for Miguel Migs featuring Capleton, Fort Knox Five, Razoof and Mike Steva just to name a few. Releases under the CIRCLE moniker with partner Keith Landis, a RISE 8 collaboration with Gerhardt Koerner, and through his HEIGHTS & WORSHIP (with Ras Heights) and RMP (with Matpat) aliases… are making chart waves worldwide.

Rob has also worked extensively & consistently with legendary vocalist, fellow Philadelphian and most of all ‘sister for life’ Lady Alma over the past 25 years. On productions like “This Is The Life” & “Love Right Now” with Joey Crawford on Worship or “All The Way Far Away” with David Marston on BBE. He has also been her ‘go to’ engineer for most of her songs for hits like “It’s House Music” for Osunlade, “A Time & A Place” for Ralf Gum, “Make You Move” for Low Steppa & Terry Hunter, “Call Him” for Phil Asher and “Merry Christmas Baby” with Jordan Mailata for the Philadelphia Eagles album ‘A Philly Special Christmas’. Other honorable collaboration mentions are with Rich Medina on “Philadelphia”, Hipp-e on “Dem A Come” on Worship & “New Beginnings” on Large Recordings, Hollis P. Monroe with "Feeling Good” & Sean O’Neil (rip)’aka Someone Else’ on “Blazin” & “High Grade Roll featuring El Feco B”.

You can currently find Rob every second Saturday for the Shakedown, at goodie & Solomonic Sound events, plus random one off events at various venues through out Philadelphia and abroad. He slowed his touring down after his third child was born to stay closer to his family, but you can still catch him on the road once or twice a month.

With his musical roots, proven success as a label owner / producer / DJ / event creator, and with his innovative approach to melding electronic dance music, Rob Paine is one of the music icons in the City of Brotherly & Sisterly Love and across the world. His commitment to the city and the world’s music lovers is unwavering, and his unique and diverse vision will ensure his success and our enjoyment far into the future.
data.name: Rob Paine

performances.body:
Rob Paine’s passion for music runs deep. From the moment he fell in love with music as a youth, he knew what his life’s journey would be, to bring people together through music. That passion has provided the foundation for a diverse and eclectic musical immersion ever since. He has been on a rapid rise from bedroom mix tapes to underground events to DJing internationally since he started out in 1993. He, along with his Circle Production crew (Kevin Gimble, Keith Landis, and Sean Thomas), created and curated some of the most epic raves & club nights in Philadelphia throughout the 90’s. In parallel, he began to flex his music theory education from Temple University and production expertise by creating a broad range of songs across a myriad of genres such as house, techno, reggae, broken beat, dub, and down-tempo.

   Rob formed WORSHIP RECORDINGS in 1998 alongside partners Dan Thompson and Zack Eberz, focusing on the dub aspect of house & techno and weaving in the techniques used by King Tubby & Lee ’Scratch’ Perry into modern dance music. During that time, he and Zack began using the SOLOMONIC SOUND name for their productions and as a vehicle for releasing their dub-influenced house and techno tracks on Worship, as well as running a traditional reggae sound system under the same moniker. (Please see Solomonic Sound’s full bio for all if his work with in the reggae community) 

   Worship Recordings has had the pleasure of releasing music by a heap of talented & successful top-billed producers from likes of Garth & Eric James, King Britt, Håkan Lidbo, Jay Tripwire, Pete Moss, Grant Dell, Universal Agents, Adam Tenenbaum (Nectar, Succulent aliases), Joey Youngman aka Wolfgang Gartner (Frequent Flyer alias), Gary Beck, Hipp-e, Rithma, Seinan, Rootz Underground, Army Of One & Rick Preston. 

   Rob also released as a solo artist and collaborated with Wamdue Kids’ Chris Udoh for early Worship releases. Now, with his passion reinforced by the labels’ success and critical industry acclaim, Rob’s unique sounds were soon being released on labels such as Large, Tango, Shaboom, and Grayhound to underground and commercial success. In addition, his productions were also licensed to labels such as Bedrock, Fabric, and NRK as part of compilations.

   During this time, another canvas was unveiled as a tapestry to showcase the Worship Recordings productions and artists: the SHAKEDOWN parties. Rob, Willyum (Will Putney), and Zack Eberz launched the monthly Shakedown event in October of 2001 and they are still going strong today 23 years later. Evidence that music – done right - is enduring. Rob’s events always feature incredible sound via the “Solomonic Sound System” that he and his crew developed and have fine-tuned to audiophile quality for discerning DJs and dancers. That same uniqueness and quality is evident in his custom-designed studio, which is instantly apparent when you hear Rob’s production and remix work.

data.body:
To know the music of SOOHAN (Stephen Suchan) is to be flooded with a rush of the vague and familiar: nostalgia meets unique and unknowable, as he deftly weaves worldwide influences into dance floor and festival-ready settings. To the unfamiliar, SOOHAN is the key holder to a
catalog which dives into and celebrates what all too often lies in the periphery. Drawing from the traditional and popular music of countless cultures, SOOHAN’s signature interpretation of dance music ignores borders, favoring a shared community dance floor experience that enthusiastically exposes audiences to new musical territory with every performance. Starting as a grassroots project in Baltimore, SOOHAN has taken off as an identity in global dance floor fusion, effortlessly combining diverse rarities with familiar 808s and bass lines. Since his initial east coast success, SOOHAN finds himself touring the US and Canada on a consistent and constant basis, with festival appearances at the likes of Envision, Shambhala, Lightning In A Bottle, Symbiosis, Burning Man, and numerous others. With some seven albums, 20 million streams, and a widely celebrated DJ mix series with Global Bass Radio, SOOHAN has hit his stride and shows no signs of slowing. SOOHAN has hit his stride and shows no signs of slowing. SOOHAN’s most ardent fans will have even more to look forward to in 2026, as a steady output of remix compilations, DJ mixes, and original material looms on the horizon. Cultivating an appreciation for the music which lies beneath the surfaces all around us, driving a direct line to celebration of global culture, and bringing people together in rapturous dance remain in SOOHAN’s sights, virtually everywhere he goes.

data.body:
Hailing from the land of compromise in the heart of Europe, the four friends of Stavroz found each other in the east of Flanders. In its inception, IJsbrand De Wilde & Gert Beazar focussed mainly on playing DJ sets and creating music in which they loved colliding worlds. Only a few years later they hooked up with Maxim Helincks & Pieter De Meester to expand their DJ activities to a performing band and continued to try and find an unbeaten path in electronic music. This culminated in their
delicately acoustic, organic and cinematic approach to electronic music. As a result they were awarded second place for “best producer” and “best breakthrough artist” in national awards for Belgium’s electronic scene. In the years that followed, the four kept expanding their catalogue and continued to develop that uniquely own style. Their EP “The Ginning” resulted in an early breakthrough for the four-some. It reached gold status in France, dominated the nr. 1 spot on the Hype Machine and was chosen as vinyl of the month by Germany’s “Faze Magazine”, mainly thanks to the B-side “The Finishing” starring a compelling solo on the tenor saxophone. In order to keep freedom in their music production and to be able to support other artists with a likeminded vision, they started their record label “Moodfamily”. It was on this label that their second hit single “Gold Town” was released. It kept the nr. 1 spot on Beatport’s “Electronic Top 100” for several months and was picked up by Rockstar Entertainment and added to GTA 5’s (already) excellent in-game music. Although Stavroz is now 11 years old, many of their songs are still relevant and are quietly nudging towards timelessness. Over half million monthly listeners and over 100.000 followers on Spotify alone will concur and 100 million plays across the major streaming platforms speaks volumes. Their intimate live performance grew more dramatic and their unique dj sets spread like wildfire as they started frequenting many international stages. Coachella (US), Fusion Festival (DE), Wonderfruit (THA), Pukkelpop (BE), Strawberry Fields (AUS), Tropico (MX), Three Points Festival (FL, USA) all fell victim to Stavroz’s unusual dramatic, melancholic yet determined approach. The band was quickly invited by the acclaimed Youtube channel “Cercle” to perform a streaming concert at Fort Saint-Eynard in France where once again, they managed to create a wholesome, comforting soundtrack for a place, which no words can describe.

data.body:
A true pillar of the Shambhala Music Festival experience, Adham Shaikh has been contributing to the sound of the Living Room for over two decades—returning to the stage an incredible 24 times and counting.
Blending deep dub, global rhythms, and lush, meditative basslines, his sets are less performances and more rituals—fluid, emotional, and deeply connected to the moment. Sunrise, sunset, or somewhere in between, Adham knows how to read the river, the crowd, and the energy, guiding dancers through journeys that are both grounding and expansive.
A Juno-nominated composer and lifelong explorer of sound, his music bridges cultures and traditions while staying rooted in the heartbeat of the dancefloor. There’s a reason he’s been invited back year after year: trust.
At the Living Room, Adham Shaikh isn’t just part of the lineup—he’s part of the foundation.

data.body: Uncle Waffles has propelled the Amapiano Genre with her stellar performances and visuals, being the first woman to break through many notable festivals and tour headlines – the first black female Amapiano act to play Coachella 2022 & 2024, The first Amapiano Act & DJ to headline Wireless in 7 years, the first Black female DJ and Amapiano act to headline at the Brooklyn Mirage, to name a few. After achieving so much as an upcoming artist in a short space of time, Uncle Waffles is looking to expand her creativity through film, presenting and fashion. She has expressed hopes of diversifying her brand from just a DJ. She is also looking forward to collaborating with more international artists in the hopes of helping spread the AmaPiano sound to the international market.
