data.body:
Hubert Ameme Sodoganji aka AMEME, is a West African born DJ/producer who splits his time between Brooklyn and Berlin.
Fueled by an innate determination, drive and passion, with attuned intuition applied to both his music and life, West African native AMÉMÉ channels his cultural roots and ancestry into everything he does. Born and raised in Benin, AMÉMÉ (Hubert Sodogandji) helms One Tribe, a global community and brand identity centred on unity and a positive outlook. His visionary capabilities and unwavering dedication have been the catalyst behind the rapid growth and proliferation of both his musical endeavours and One Tribe as a whole. Specialising in Afro-leaning electronic music, with flourishes of tribal percussion and spiritual vocals built upon a foundation of contemporary deep house and tech, AMÉMÉ’s DJ sets are eclectic, energetic and nourishing for the soul. Releases on his One Tribe label found their way to Black Coffee, who supported the music in Tulum back in 2019. With such an influential figure backing his productions, it was inevitable that AMÉMÉ’s name would soon be ringing in the ears of other key tastemakers. Over the past few years he’s been signed to Watergate Records, Blond:Ish has released his music on her Abracadabra label and the mighty MoBlack has also brought the West African wonder on board. Recently he was drafted in to remix fellow African artist Omah Lay’s song ‘Understand’ for Sire Records (Warner). AMÉMÉ was chosen in order to maintain the connection to the motherland, injecting Omah’s original with unique influences from his own lineage. The remix breathes new life into the song, while holding true to the energy of their nation.
Meanwhile, he has demonstrated his prowess as a selector, touring the globe to spin in global epicentres for electronic music; Ibiza, Miami, Berlin, New York, Paris, Mykonos and Tulum through to events like Burning Man, where his tribal rhythms, spiritually-enriching vocals and life affirming melodies were right at home. AMÉMÉ’s outgoing persona and contagious energy behind the decks complement his rapturous selections, creating the ideal combination to fill the dance floor with uplifting vibrations.
In his homeland Benin, West Africa, the ancient art of voodoo still has a presence in everyday life. The spiritual practice centres on human connection with the natural world; plants, animals and inanimate objects. Everything has energy, vibration and life force. Many of its core rituals are rooted in drums and chants, the voice and percussion, deeply innate human modes of expression. AMÉMÉ’s early life was indelibly marked by the influence of this ceremonial tradition and wider African music culture.
As a child he competed with his siblings to produce mixtapes that could be played in the car on the way to school. Whoever had the best selection was bestowed with the honour of soundtracking their journey. Later he became part of a hip hop group in his home nation, achieving nationwide success and supporting French superstars when they toured in Benin. These two pivotal points instilled in AMÉMÉ crucial skills that would bolster his DJ career; selecting music and performing.
In 2005 his older brother moved to France after high school and returned to his homeland with electronic music from the European nation, which, of course, he played in the car, because he was the oldest sibling. Over the three months of summer, AMÉMÉ couldn’t escape from French house and (eventually) came to love it. This introduction to the greats of the French scene forms the bedrock of his shift into electronic music.
Fast forward a few years and AMÉMÉ moved to New York. There he utilised his new surroundings to begin exploring the possibilities of becoming a DJ, which had been his dream since he was 12. He bought a controller, taught himself to play and began spinning at small parties, later becoming proficient with playing vinyl, too. A few twists and turns, including a hiatus from DJing to concentrate on a career in banking, eventually led to AMÉMÉ’s decision to go for broke and embrace his dream wholeheartedly. The early stages and influences in his life imbue his approach to DJing and production; chants, drums, African musical culture, impeccable selection, performance, French house… it’s all there in the DNA of his unique sonic identity.
Thanks to his analytical mind, he was aware that it was crucial to establish other creative outlets alongside his DJing. This led to the birth of One Tribe, a multifaceted platform which includes an events wing and a fashion label. He became connected with New York’s highly influential House Of Yes, which served as a launchpad for his vision. In 2019 he moved to Berlin and made contact with Rise, the subdivision of Watergate, forming a bond with the afrohouse outfit. Living in the German city further cemented his love for electronic music culture, and fueled his growth as an artist.
AMÉMÉ’s ability to connect with people and make things happen has been evident throughout his entire career. He has a considered approach to his ambitions, and plans his movements accordingly. This natural aptitude for strategising and forward thinking, combined with his passion for making and sharing music has put him in a position where he has been the master of his own destiny. For a relative newcomer to the electronic music world, his rise has been astounding, galvanized by this enlightened approach.
AMÉMÉ’s empire continues to grow in tandem with his profile, demonstrating that true talent rises to the top. His sincere and humble determination places music and community at the forefront of his actions, with positive intent behind every new shift and evolution. From Benin to Brooklyn to Berlin, his worldly outlook and unrelenting drive means AMÉMÉ will be hosting his own percussion-fueled rituals on dance floors across the world for many years to come - uniting the global community and reminding us that we are all One Tribe.
data.name: AMÉMÉ

performances.body:

A few twists and turns, including a hiatus from DJing to concentrate on a career in banking, eventually led to AMÉMÉ’s decision to go for broke and embrace his dream wholeheartedly. The early stages and influences in his life imbue his approach to DJing and production; chants, drums, African musical culture, impeccable selection, performance, French house… it’s all there in the DNA of his unique sonic identity.
Thanks to his analytical mind, he was aware that it was crucial to establish other creative outlets alongside his DJing. This led to the birth of One Tribe, a multifaceted platform which includes an events wing and a fashion label. He became connected with New York’s highly influential House Of Yes, which served as a launchpad for his vision. In 2019 he moved to Berlin and made contact with Rise, the subdivision of Watergate, forming a bond with the afrohouse outfit. Living in the German city further cemented his love for electronic music culture, and fueled his growth as an artist.
AMÉMÉ’s ability to connect with people and make things happen has been evident throughout his entire career. He has a considered approach to his ambitions, and plans his movements accordingly. This natural aptitude for strategising and forward thinking, combined with his passion for making and sharing music has put him in a position where he has been the master of his own destiny. For a relative newcomer to the electronic music world, his rise has been astounding, galvanized by this enlightened approach.
AMÉMÉ’s empire continues to grow in tandem with his profile, demonstrating that true talent rises to the top. His sincere and humble determination places music and community at the forefront of his actions, with positive intent behind every new shift and evolution. From Benin to Brooklyn to Berlin, his worldly outlook and unrelenting drive means AMÉMÉ will be hosting his own percussion-fueled rituals on dance floors across the world for many years to come - uniting the global community and reminding us that we are all One Tribe.

Immersed in jazz and folk since toddler times and fuelled by his passion for music, Soren has been DJ-ing under the alias 'Thomas Workshop' while in cohesion touring as a founding member, vocalist, writer and producer of Lazy Syrup Orchestra.

Additionally he co-produces events with Soul Hop Productions. Hosting artists in VanCity and co-creating dance opportunities. With LSO, Soren is performing on an array of festival stages as the group continues to evolve and develop new music. He’s happiest when surrounded by aquatic house music, basketball and plant based hygiene products.

data.body:
If you’ve danced in the dirt at the Salmo River Ranch anytime over the last quarter-century, chances are you’ve already grooved to DJ Just-B. A true cornerstone of the Kootenay electronic music scene, Just-B has spent over two decades cultivating a signature sound built on irresistible grooves, deep basslines, and unyielding positivity. Calling Nelson, BC home since 1999, he is widely celebrated by the Farmily for his innate ability to read a festival crowd and deliver the perfect beats for the exact moment.

Just-B’s musical roots trace back to his childhood near Toronto, Ontario, but his true calling was discovered in the early ‘90s. Immersed in the underground house, techno, and jungle scenes, he picked up a pair of used Technics 1200s and never looked back. Today, his sound is defined by a deep love for the "human element" in electronic music. He expertly weaves together chunky basslines, slick vocal hooks, live instrumental solos, and driving, funky rhythms to create solid, seamlessly blended house sets that are "dirty-sexy-funky-chunky-irresistibly-danceable" and effortlessly uplifting.

His legacy is inextricably linked to Shambhala Music Festival. Over the past 25 years, Just-B has delivered an astonishing 40 performances across 10 different stages, cementing his status as a beloved festival veteran. Beyond the Salmo River Valley, his career has taken him to stages in Europe and the Americas, sharing the bill with heavyweights like Mark Farina, Justin Martin, Questlove, Kid Koala, and Luke Solomon.

While deeply rooted in solid, seamlessly blended house music, Just-B’s vast crate-digging history allows him to navigate fluidly through downtempo, nu-disco, breakbeats, and drum n’ bass. Whether he's curating lush, sun-drenched daytime lounge mixes alongside selectress Lady V or commanding a packed stage at 2:00 AM, his mission remains the same: to inspire genuine, happy vibes, keep the dancefloor moving, and "just be".
Real. Authentic. Just-B.
data.name: DJ Just-B

data.body: TKO is a Calgary, Canada-based producer and DJ celebrated for his genre-fluid approach to electronic music. Since 2012, he's been making waves with releases on respected labels like Dirtybird, Slothacid, Force Of Habit, Feral One, Tall Door Records and more. His music has not only captured attention but has also led him to share the stage with some of the industry's biggest names, solidifying his status as an innovative force in the scene. When it comes to his performances, he keeps things fresh and unpredictable. His sets are a dynamic mix of unreleased edits, original tracks, and clever genre blends, reflecting his profound love for music and his talent for engaging crowds. His commitment to crafting memorable experiences sets him apart in the world of electronic music.

data.body: Deblock and Mihajic Touch joined forces to form Double Trouble. a BC-based house music duo delivering high-energy, groove-driven sets across the Kootenavs and bevond. Dedicated selectors and emerging producers, they craft deep rhythms and punchy basslines built for a driving dancefloor. Since their Shambhala debut in 2024. they've continued to build momentum with a refined, late-night sound.

data.body:
Based between the Kootenays, BC, and the Baja California Sur, Mexico, DJ Armonía creates emotional and euphoric dance experiences that flow between melodic house, tech house, and bass-driven beats. Her sets are guided by energy and environment—ranging from deep, heartfelt journeys to high-vibe, bass-infused rhythms that move the dancefloor.

She’s performed at Shambhala Music Festival, the Secret Stage at Ikigai Mori, and opened for Justin Martin in the club scene. Inspired by astrology and cosmic cycles, Armonía releases her more melodic and introspective mixes on SoundCloud in alignment with astrological events—translating the language of the stars into sound that connects both heart and body.

Her name, Armonía—meaning “harmony” in Spanish—embodies her mission to unite emotion, rhythm, and spirit through music. Whether under the open stars or in the pulse of a packed club, DJ Armonía channels a frequency of emotional liberation, inviting dancers to feel, release, and remember the harmony within.

data.body:
Known for his high-energy sets and innovative productions, Stresshead pushes the boundaries of intensive pop music. Stresshead has shared stages with Fred Again, Pluko, Kelly Lee Owens, Yunè Pinku, and Duskus. Last summer, as well as playing alongside Jamie XX at Lido and playing Reading festival, he supported SBTRKT and TOKiMONSTA on their EU Tours.
His dynamic sound has also caught the attention of publications such as Mixmag, where he earned a spotlight in their December 2024 addition of “Five Artists to Check Outˮ. Signed to AMF, Stresshead has released two singles; ‘Pull Me Into Youʼ and ‘Want (Need)ʼ, followed by his EP ‘Colourʼ - earning significant spotify support.

data.body:
After a hot streak of unmissable single releases this year, rising electronic producer & DJ ATRIP is now ready to share his highly anticipated new mixtape, TANZPARTEI II, dropping on October 4th.
Following the success of his first TANZPARTEI mixtape in 2023, TANZPARTEI II represents an exciting evolution in ATRIP’s sound and artistic direction. The new mixtape showcases the producer’s deepened ambition to push boundaries, incorporating a diverse range of collaborators from both inside and outside the electronic music sphere.
ATRIP comments on the mixtape: "Following my first mixtape release in May 2023, I wanted to be more ambitious in collaborating with artists outside my lane, including rappers like Bawo and KAM-BU, in order to push my own artistic identity into new territories as well as open the TANZPARTEI world to new emotions. Poté and TeaMarrr also contributed amazing vocals for the tape. A core part of this mixtape sequence is the community I have been curating around it — it feels like it’s finally coming to life with this second mixtape, playing shows and testing the music in front of crowds this time has been a big part of the process."
TANZPARTEI II features standout collaborations from ‘Ocean’ and ‘Dun That’ with KAM-BU & TeaMarr, as well as Bawo on ‘Talking Too Much’; a reflection of ATRIP’s ethos to blend genres and emotions. The mixtape captures a broad spectrum of energy, from euphoric high points to introspective moments, all while staying true to his trademark blend of vibrant, kinetic production.
‘Friday (Alone Right Now)’, the latest single and focus track for the mixtape, has already become a staple in ATRIP’s DJ sets throughout the year, earning heavy rotation from prominent DJs such as Barry Can’t Swim, Joy Anonymous, and salute. Its infectious groove and dance floor-ready energy have made it a fan favourite, setting the stage for the larger-than-life sound of TANZPARTEI II.
ATRIP will be wrapping up an incredible year by hitting the road alongside some of the biggest names in electronic music. He will be joining Disclosure at their night for Warehouse Project, supporting Barry Can’t Swim at his three O2 Academy Brixton shows on 1st, 2nd & 3rd November, before making his debut across North America, with a 7-date tour where he will touch down in New York, Chicago, San Francisco, Washington DC amongst others. He will wrap up the year with his headline show at Phonox on 28th November as he continues to build on the incredible momentum he’s developed over the past year, solidifying his position as one of the UK’s most exciting and innovative names on the circuit.
Having already gained recognition from the likes of HAAi, Bonobo, Disclosure, Barry Can’t Swim, DJ Seinfeld and Lil Silva, as well as millions of organic streams, ATRIP is poised to make a major impact with TANZPARTEI II. His commitment to cultivating a genuine community around his music - from his renowned pub meet-ups to fostering relationships with fans via WhatsApp - is a testament to his dedication to creating a shared experience that transcends the club.
data.name: ATRIP

data.body:
At just 24, Lavern is one of the faces of emotive dance music, blending storytelling, nostalgia and dancefloor energy into a sound that’s amassed hundreds of millions of streams and earned him spots on iconic stages worldwide.
The Netherlands has famously produced some of the world’s biggest DJs, and Lavern is well on track to be the next. A naturally gifted musician and producer, his work captures the attention of both fans and fellow artists, with everyone wanting to hear what he’s doing next.
His journey into music was unexpected - originally pursuing law, he made a bold leap into production when his 2021 viral hit Move Me exploded with 60 million streams. Follow-up track Hold Me solidified his status in the emotional house movement, earning over 200 million streams. Major artists like Kygo, Ava Max, and Becky G have since enlisted him for remixes, proving his influence across the industry.
2026 is set to be LAVERN’s biggest year yet. With an EP coming out later in the year and a fresh collab, just played on the Sahara stage at Coachella where Lavern joined Kaskade on stage to premiere the new collab , his music wil keep on coming. With shows played like the mainstage at EDC Vegas, Sziget in Budapest, support for Martin Garrix at his 45k capacity stadium show in Mumbai, Veld in Toronto and a 5 show tour in Australia he is having a great run. Lavern also just secured his very first Las Vegas residency with Tao Group including shows at their iconic clubs - Omnia, Marquee, Hakkasan, and Omnia day club. As LAVERN continues to push the boundaries of emotive dance music, one thing is certain — he’s a global force in the making.

data.body:
Boris Brejcha was born in Ludwigshafen (Germany) in 1981 and already had to experience some strokes of fate in his childhood. At the age of six, he was seriously injured in the Ramstein air disaster and spent months in hospital. Today, he still bears extensive scars from the severe burns. Because of teasing from classmates, Boris withdrew and discovered his interest in music. After a classmate of his brought a "Thunderdome" CD in middle school, he was hooked. He was dying to know how to make this new kind of electronic music. So at the age of twelve, he started producing electronic music himself as a hobby.

He made his first releases in 2006, the same year he had the first performance of his entire career. At the Universo Paralello Festival in Brazil. Since then, his mask is also indispensable. Inspired by the Brazilian Carnival, Boris chose the Joker mask as his trademark. In the international foreign countries his music found fast approval. Numerous gigs, for example South America and Russia, followed.

Since then, Boris has made a full-time living from music and has continued to develop as an artist. With the specially created genre "High-Tech-Minimal" Boris gave his extraordinary music a name. He went on to release on Autist Records and later on Harthouse. Through the music magazine "Raveline" he was named "exceptional talent 2007".

“High-Tech Minimal”
When Boris founded his own music label Fckng Serious in 2015, he really took off.
He released more music than almost anyone else.

“Fckng Serious”
He then achieved two notable breakthroughs in his career through the recorded sets with the French streaming channel "Cercle" and his set from the world-renowned electro festival "Tomorrowland". Together, the videos on YouTube have about 140 million views.

In the meantime, it is impossible to imagine the music scene without Boris. He has achieved international fame and plays every weekend on the biggest stages in the world. Label bus tours in the nightliner have also taken him across Europe with his colleagues. His specially arranged concerts, with a special concept, always attract tens of thousands of fans everywhere. His own fashion brand has also already achieved great fame.

Another highlight: his personalized squeaky duck with original Joker mask. He regularly hands them out at concerts or hides them in the world's biggest cities, where fans can then search for them.

Most recently, he released his tenth studio album on Ultra Music and was ranked 42nd in the DJ-Mag voting of the Top100 DJs. Boris still plays only his own produced music at his gigs - and the success proves him right. Sold-out halls, screaming fans, TV appearances and weeks of touring - around the globe, he is eagerly awaited at all major festivals.

In 2024, the single "Gravity" went gold in Mexico.

“The future will be Fckng Serious”

data.body:
Equal parts a master producer and veritable phenomenon, Eli Brown is a sonic force in a category of his own.

Since bursting onto the scene in 2016, the techno powerhouse’s brooding sound and visceral energy have catapulted him from the UK’s underground to global icon status at meteoritic pace - But you would be remiss to call him an overnight sensation. Boasting a career that spans over two decades, the enigmatic persona is yet another incarnation of the ingenuity, intensity and innovation of the man behind the music, distilled into the singular artistic vision that is Eli Brown.
Unimpressed with conformity, Brown’s career is not just a story of continued evolution: it's a testament to his indomitable spirit and unstoppable drive to carve out his space, on his terms.

The genesis of Eli Brown’s distinct auditory expression can be found veiled in the shadows of Bristol's famed clandestine realms, where a passion for subversive soundscapes quickly progressed into an unrelenting obsession with the craft. Balancing self-taught technical mastery with raw instinct, his genre-defying productions capture the rebellious edge that defined the city’s electronic legacy, while simultaneously forging a new frontier that beckons listeners into the furthest reaches of his own creative depths. Rooted in the sinister, his sophisticated compositions offer a high-frequency exploration of the darkest sonic recesses in a way that resonates with loyal fans and new audiences alike, leaving an indelible mark on the industry as a whole.

Brown’s penchant for breaking boundaries has undeniably cemented the DJ, producer and record label head as one of music’s most in-demand names and highest streamed techno artists in the world with unprecedented fervor. To date, the multi-hyphenate has amassed a cult-like following with over 3 million monthly listeners on Spotify, more than 300 million cumulative streams and ardent support from industry legends such as Carl Cox, Calvin Harris, Reinier Zonneveld, Adam Beyer and more. His magnetic stage presence and the sheer power of his sound consistently sell out some of the world’s most prestigious stages in massive markets ranging from New York and Los Angeles, to London and Berlin, including the likes of Tomorrowland, Ultra Music Festival, Glastonbury Festival and more. The visionaire is also the founder of Arcane, currently the second biggest techno label in the world, a forward-thinking imprint championing both emerging and established acts who are similarly bridging the gap between the underground and mainstream worlds.
With a discography as versatile as his artistry, the serial hit-maker is no stranger to sensation-level release response. 2022 saw the release of Eli Brown’s massive hit ‘Believe’, which soared to the top of charts at breakneck speed, proving to be an ardent tonesetter for the major year ahead. In 2023, Brown dropped two dynamite releases - his crossover techno track ‘Be the One’ and the infectious lyrical banger ‘Diamonds On My Mind’. Both tracks would ignite viral social media moments, garnering over 100 million cumulative streams / views per release and unequivocally cementing Eli Brown’s status as an underground powerhouse with mainstream appeal. After a string of top releases in 2024, Eli Brown set the stage for a monumental 2025, a year that has seen his track Wavey be chosen as the official anthem of EDC Las Vegas and standout collaborations including Drip with Legzdina, Gimme Gimme with HNTR, Done Searching with Anna Reusch and Latin-techno crossovers Papi and Me Gusta with GeezLy.

With a steady pulse of new music releases, and determined to push the sound and diversity of techno, Eli Brown leads the way on a storied road to solidifying his positioning as the world’s leading mainstage techno artist.

The genesis of Eli Brown’s distinct auditory expression can be found veiled in the shadows of Bristol's famed clandestine realms, where a passion for subversive soundscapes quickly progressed into an unrelenting obsession with the craft. Balancing self-taught technical mastery with raw instinct, his genre-defying productions capture the rebellious edge that defined the city’s electronic legacy, while simultaneously forging a new frontier that beckons listeners into the furthest reaches of his own creative depths. Rooted in the sinister, his sophisticated compositions offer a high-frequency exploration of the darkest sonic recesses in a way that resonates with loyal fans and new audiences alike, leaving an indelible mark on the industry as a whole.

Brown’s penchant for breaking boundaries has undeniably cemented the DJ, producer and record label head as one of music’s most in-demand names and highest streamed techno artists in the world with unprecedented fervor. To date, the multi-hyphenate has amassed a cult-like following with over 3 million monthly listeners on Spotify, more than 300 million cumulative streams and ardent support from industry legends such as Carl Cox, Calvin Harris, Reinier Zonneveld, Adam Beyer and more. His magnetic stage presence and the sheer power of his sound consistently sell out some of the world’s most prestigious stages in massive markets ranging from New York and Los Angeles, to London and Berlin, including the likes of Tomorrowland, Ultra Music Festival, Glastonbury Festival and more. The visionaire is also the founder of Arcane, currently the second biggest techno label in the world, a forward-thinking imprint championing both emerging and established acts who are similarly bridging the gap between the underground and mainstream worlds.
With a discography as versatile as his artistry, the serial hit-maker is no stranger to sensation-level release response. 2022 saw the release of Eli Brown’s massive hit ‘Believe’, which soared to the top of charts at breakneck speed, proving to be an ardent tonesetter for the major year ahead. In 2023, Brown dropped two dynamite releases - his crossover techno track ‘Be the One’ and the infectious lyrical banger ‘Diamonds On My Mind’. Both tracks would ignite viral social media moments, garnering over 100 million cumulative streams / views per release and unequivocally cementing Eli Brown’s status as an underground powerhouse with mainstream appeal. After a string of top releases in 2024, Eli Brown set the stage for a monumental 2025, a year that has seen his track Wavey be chosen as the official anthem of EDC Las Vegas and standout collaborations including Drip with Legzdina, Gimme Gimme with HNTR, Done Searching with Anna Reusch and Latin-techno crossovers Papi and Me Gusta with GeezLy. 

With a steady pulse of new music releases, and determined to push the sound and diversity of techno, Eli Brown leads the way on a storied road to solidifying his positioning as the world’s leading mainstage techno artist.

data.body: The Dare—the solo project of vocalist, songwriter and producer Harrison Patrick Smith—took the world by storm with the release of his debut album, What's Wrong With New York?, in 2024, drawing extensive praise from critics and fellow artists alike. The LP, which arrived via Republic Records, features The Dare's ubiquitous single “Girls”—now a bona fide hit with 63 million streams on Spotify alone—and fan favorites “Perfume” and “Good Time.” He's spent the months since touring the record across the world, selling out every single show worldwide, with a bevy of festival performances throughout the summer, including sets at Coachella, Primavera Sound, Lollapalooza, Reading, Leeds and more. The Dare's legendary DJ sets, honed at his signature downtown Manhattan party Freakquencies, have soundtracked parties for Gucci, Celine and Yves Saint Laurent and featured in Rolling Stone, GQ, PAPER and more. Amidst his meteoric rise, he has shared stages and collaborated with the likes of Charli xcx, Billie Eilish, PinkPantheress, Justice, Yves Tumor, Water From Your Eyes and a host of others. Most recently, The Dare shared a deluxe version of his debut album, What's Wrong With New York?: Afters, featuring three new tracks taken from sessions for his full-length debut.

data.body: Two Shell are an elusive UK electronic duo known for playful anonymity, warped UK bass, garage, techno, dubstep, jungle, and hyperpop-adjacent club music. Their project is built around digital breadcrumbs, strange online worlds, and a cartoonish, future-facing sound that has earned attention from outlets like Resident Advisor, The Guardian, Mixmag, and Pitchfork. Their self-titled debut album was released via Young in 2024, following earlier releases including Icons and Lil Spirits.

data.body:
Skradley, an underground downtempo leftfield dubstep bass artist from Calgary, blends emotional storytelling with unapologetic edge. As a staple in Western Canada’s bass circuit, he’s known for intuitively reading the crowd and delivering intentional, high-energy sets that keep the dance floor locked in from start to finish.

Rooted in heavy emotional bass therapy, his sound balances down to chill moments, with chest rattling impact, fusing head nodding low end with unexpected switch-ups.

Inspired by the Village Stage at Shambhala Music Festival, Skradley builds hypnotic, narrative-driven journeys designed for late-night systems, sparking true connection through music.

data.body:
ill.Gates has over 70 million plays across 300+ songs, releases with Grammy Winners, music in Star Wars Disneyland, and an Action Bronson remix which was featured in GTA V. He’s released 8 studio albums as ill.Gates, given over 1000 live performances on 5 continents and founded the Producer Dojo record label, which is rapidly approaching its 100th release.

Collaborators include 42 Dugg, Alanis, Andrew Huang, Apashe, Beats Antique, Bil Bless, Captain Hook, Chris Karns (Pretty Lights), CloZee, Conrank, Dead Kennedys, Def3, Defunk, Destroid, Dieselboy, DJ Vadim, Dub FX, Eko-Zu, Eprom, EyeOnEyez, Foreign Beggars, G Jones, Gucci Mane, ill-esha, Kandisha, KJ Sawka, Kraddy (Glitch Mob), Liquid Stranger, Mayor Ape, Mimosa, Minnesota, Mr Bill, Noisia, Opiuo, PAV4N, RIP Kenny, Stephan Jacobs, So Down, Souleye, SPL (Champagne Drip), Stylust, UHNK, Who Knew, DJ Yawn, and Zain Effendi (Pirates of the Caribbean, Dark Knight).

Producers around the world know ill.Gates as the first person to ever offer commercial Ableton templates (2006) and ill.Gates’ famous “128s” and “Mudpies” techniques have become so ubiquitous that their popularity eclipses even his own. More recently ill.Gates has expanded into programming, creating all-hardware “128s” style wavetables that now come stock with Serum 2 as well as a revolutionary new offline processing app called 128s Multisample Maker.

As an educator, ill.Gates has taught at the Ableton Education Summit, Berkelee College of Music, NYU, ACC (Austin Community College), the S.A.E. (School of Audio Engineering), and was featured on the Ableton ‘One Thing’ series and Andrew Huang’s ‘4 Producers Flip The Same Sample’ show.

In 2016 ill.Gates founded his own music school: Producer Dojo, teaching creativity and discipline to musicians using his signature combination of philosophy, psychology and practicality.

You can catch up with the ill one at www.illGates.com and learn all about the Dojo at www.ProducerDojo.com

data.body:
Justin Hawkes is one of the rare American voices reshaping the global Drum & Bass landscape. Since emerging in 2011 from the foothills of Southwest Virginia, he’s fused euphoric, festival-ready anthems with gritty, tech-driven warehouse grooves—earning support from icons like Sub Focus, Andy C, Wilkinson, RL Grime, Knife Party, Pendulum, Porter Robinson and Black Tiger Sex Machine.


After rebranding from Flite in 2020, Justin’s 2022 debut album 'Existential' announced him as an innovative rule breaker. From its scoreworthy symphonics to the viral, genre-bending country-rock single 'Better Than Gold,' the breakthrough LP showcased an alchemy of fearless songwriting and top-tier production, codifying his off-limits approach to bass music. Across platforms, his catalog has now topped 100 million streams, fueling tours in 14+ countries and sets at esteemed venues such as London's Alexandra Palace, Electric Daisy Carnival and Tomorrowland (Belgium & Brazil). As 2026 unfolds, Justin teases new explorations—including the launch of his label Drumcaste Collective, titling his sound "Experiential Drum & Bass Music".


Beyond clubs and festivals, Justin’s music powers AAA game titles like Rocket League and Fortnite, while his live sets—driven by improvisation, raw energy and an undeniable American edge—offer a one‑of‑a‑kind experience that raises heart rates and begs feet to move. Whether crafting ambient interludes, swelling cinematics or obliterative drops, he remains a genre-defying force at the cutting edge of Drum & Bass.

data.body:
LEVEL UP, aka Sonya Broner, feels most at home behind the decks. Originally a battle DJ under the mentorship of the legendary Rob Swift, she later dove into music production after discovering dubstep. Since then, she has continuously refined her sound and live performances, taking fans on an immersive ride through her music.
Blending heavy bass and future bass with her own flips of classic dance and emo anthems, LEVEL UP has carved out a distinct style that resonates with crowds worldwide.
Since 2021, she has headlined multiple tours across North America and performed globally, with standout appearances at iconic festivals and venues including Red Rocks, Lollapalooza, Electric Daisy Carnival Las Vegas, Bonnaroo, Lost Lands, Beyond Wonderland, Electric Forest, Bass Canyon, and Ultra Music Festival.
Her releases span powerhouse labels such as Monstercat, Deadbeats, GRVDNCR, and Buygore, alongside her own imprint, Summoning Circle - with plenty more music on the way. In 2025, she’s continued to soar with a sold-out Sapphire Souls headline tour, high-profile collaborations with rising and established bass acts, and mainstage performances at some of the world’s biggest festivals, solidifying her as one of the most dynamic artists in bass music today.

data.body:
Seven Lions, aka Jeff Montalvo, is known as the pioneer of melodic bass: blending dubstep, trance, and cinematic soundscapes into a sound that’s uniquely his own. Since his breakout remix of Above & Beyond’s “You Got to Go,” Montalvo has carved out a singular space in electronic music - bridging festival-ready energy with deeply emotional songwriting.
His debut EP, Days to Come (2012), quickly earned critical acclaim and fan devotion, landing on Vibe’s Top Electronic Albums list and receiving regular support from BBC Radio 1. Over the next decade, he continued to expand his sound with standout releases like Worlds Apart (2014), The Throes of Winter (2015), Creation (2017), and Find Another Way (2020), often collaborating with artists like Kerli, HALIENE, SLANDER, Jason Ross, and Wooli. His remix work, including tracks for Above & Beyond Tritonal, and Illenium, further cemented his status as one of the most versatile producers in the scene. In 2018, Montalvo founded his own label, Ophelia Records, which quickly became a home for genre-pushing melodic bass and a launchpad for rising talent. He released his long-awaited debut album Beyond the Veil in 2022, showcasing his growth as a songwriter and storyteller. In 2025, Seven Lions unveiled his second full-length album, Asleep in the Garden of Infernal Stars—a callback to his earlier years that embraces his journey of taking his own path. Drawing from across his catalog and early musical influences, the album marks a bold evolution in his sound: immersive, cinematic, and unbound by genre conventions.

data.body:
From the outskirts of London to global acclaim, Cameron Edwards and Joe Lenzie—the duo behind Sigma—have navigated a journey marked by number one hits as well as underground anthems that has cemented them as one of the most important acts in Drum and Bass and UK dance music.
The pair met in a record shop in the 2000s whilst at Leeds University. Cam, a sociable club promoter with a passion for dark, cutting-edge drum and bass, and Joe, a scratch DJ champion with a background in classical violin, began producing tracks together. They soon caught the attention of key players in the drum and bass scene, releasing music on labels like DJ Zinc’s Bingo, Hospital Records & Breakbeat Kaos. This led to them picking up Best Breakthrough DJs at the Drum and Bass Arena awards and establishing themselves as an integral part of the genre.
They broke through globally in 2014 with the multi-platinum single Nobody to Love, a euphoric rework of Kanye West’s “Bound 2”, that went to No.1 in the charts in the UK, New Zealand and Poland and became a defining track of the 2010s dance resurgence. A 2nd UK No.1 quickly followed with “Changing” featuring Paloma Faith. This success opened doors to massive festival stages and collaborations with A-List artists such as Rita Ora, Ella Henderson, Birdy & Labrinth. Sigma had arrived—and the drum and bass world would never be the same.
However, Cam and Joe have always credited their love for the underground—from gritty jungle beats to the anthemic soundscapes of early rave culture—as the foundation of their artistry. By 2020, Sigma began to feel the pull of these origins and the release of London Sound (2024) marked a return to the genre that first inspired them.
In 2025, Sigma left the major label that signed them way back in 2014 founding their very own label “DAY ONES” and becoming fully independent. Finally able to create on their own terms, they collaborated with legends like Dizzee Rascal and rising stars such as Julia Church and the result was Day One (2025). The album balanced nostalgia with forward-thinking innovation and enabled Sigma to forge a future that honoured their past. The success of Day One led to a performance on ITV’s Sunday Brunch, re-working Superstylin’ for the England Football Team and finally making their BBC Radio 1 Essential Mix Debut.
On stage, Sigma are renowned for delivering performances that feel more like full-scale live experiences than DJ sets. Seamlessly blending their own chart-topping records with cutting-edge drum and bass, dancefloor, and bass music, they create high-energy journeys designed to keep crowds locked in from start to finish. Their sets are tightly crafted yet instinctive, reading the room and elevating the atmosphere with double drops, euphoric build-ups, and perfectly timed vocal moments. From clubs like London’s XOYO to mega venues like Drumsheds and the Royal Albert Hall to festivals such as Reading/Leeds, Tomorrowland and Glastonbury, Sigma turn any stage into a full-throttle, hands-in-the-air experience.
Sigma enter 2026 independent, full of energy and ready to release the music they love.
data.name: Sigma

data.body:
Bass Heavy Duo PHIBES consist of real life UK born brothers Scott and Ryan Wild. With their infectious tracks, adrenaline fueled live shows and relentless momentum they have firmly rooted themselves in rave and Drum & Bass culture.
Now in their 7th Year attending Shambhala they have become mainstays and an act that always ups the ante with a fresh set of dubs and reworked classics all the while infusing each element of bass music with an urban falvour, its always a party and its never to be missed.

data.body:
Join husband-and-wife duo Quinton and Kendra Armstrong for a deeply restorative and immersive sound healing journey designed to help you recharge amidst the energy of the festival.

Kendra will guide participants through gentle breathwork while playing quartz crystal and Tibetan singing bowls, and Quinton will layer in ambient, psychedelic electric guitar to create a rich and expansive soundscape. These frequencies work to calm the nervous system, support emotional release, and bring the body into a state of balance and deep relaxation.

This experience invites you into a meditative, dreamlike state where you can rest, reset, and reconnect. Many participants simply drift into a peaceful, restorative “sound nap.”

Please bring anything you need to get cozy: a yoga mat, blanket, pillow or bolster, floatie, crystals, or any personal items that help you feel comfortable lying down. Come as you are, settle in, and allow yourself to fully receive.
data.name: Mindful Moon Sound Healing

Please bring anything you need to get cozy: a yoga mat, blanket, pillow or bolster, floatie, crystals, or any personal items that help you feel comfortable lying down. Come as you are, settle in, and allow yourself to fully receive.

data.body:
ROOTS FLOW blends yoga, mantra, movement, meditation, and somatic release. Guided by Rae Roots, the workshop weaves postures, breath, and partner exercises. A fun way to ground, inviting land connection and centering.
Sound supports the experience, sometimes as subtle background while exploring complex movement, and at other times encouraging dance, vocal release, and expression. Music draws from Jamaica, Africa, the UK, and beyond, spanning dub, reggae, breaks, ancestral rhythms, and meditative sounds.
Acknowledging the roots of yoga in India and drawing from wisdom gained through years of practice and study, Rae shares both guided sequences and leaves space to explore your own way of moving.

RAE ROOTS (E-RYT 500) with over 1000 hours of yoga teacher training, is a dedicated yoga practitioner, teacher, DJ, and radio show host with a background in biomedical sciences. Founder of the neighbourhood yoga home, ‘the shala’ in East Vancouver (@raerootsyoga) — a down-to-earth space rooted in care and respect for yoga’s origins — Rae sees yoga as a path to the heart, to connect wholly to our nature and to live with peace and joy. She teaches in a way that is both informative and welcoming, with an approach that is playful and attentive, keeping practice accessible while honouring the profound depth of yoga as a spiritual practice.

data.body:
Magisus is a Guatemalan experiential producer, music producer, hybrid DJ, and booking agent based in Mexico City & Guatemala, known for his signature latin pirate magic vibe.

Blending contemporary world music with downtempo, deep tech, afrotech, house, and midtempo, his sound has evolved into what he calls light house — a vibrant, mystic fusion of flirty basslines and unexpected grooves.

Each set unfolds as a living story, threaded with rhythm, culture, and playful mysticism.

data.body:
Rising out of Western Canada’s underground, Footprints has built a reputation not just as a DJ, but as a curator of environments—someone who understands that the experience extends far beyond the music itself. His roots in event production have shaped a career defined by intention, where sound, space, and energy intersect. From intimate late-night rooms to large-scale festival stages, his presence behind the decks carries the weight of someone who has spent decades refining not only his craft, but the atmosphere around it.

Years of traveling through global music circuits have exposed him to a wide spectrum of scenes and cultures, each leaving its imprint. These influences show up in his sets that are fluid and adaptive, never locked into a single genre or expectation. Depending on the moment, he moves seamlessly between house and breakbeat, dips into hip-hop, or leans into deep, bass-driven sound system frequencies. The common thread is not defined by genre, but feeling—an ability to read a crowd and respond with precision.

Over a 25-year journey, Footprints has developed a sound that feels both grounded and evolving. There’s a tension in his sets—a deliberate balance between shadow and light, grit and elegance. Dark, driving rhythms are layered with moments of unexpected warmth and melody, creating a narrative that pulls listeners in rather than overwhelming them. It’s this duality that defines his identity: music that doesn’t just play, but unfolds.

More than anything, Footprints represents longevity in a scene that rarely stands still. His consistency isn’t rooted in repetition, but in a clear sense of identity—one shaped by experience, discipline, and an instinct for what moves people. Rather than chasing trends, he leans into timeless elements of rhythm, space, and emotion, creating sets that feel both current and enduring. At its core, his work is about connection—linking sound to setting, energy to audience, and each moment on the dance floor to something deeper that lingers long after the music fades.
data.name: Footprints

data.body:
Koji Aiken is a Japanese-Canadian drum and bass artist redefining the future of bass music in North America, but before he was behind the decks, he was building a platform. Starting as a content creator and the 'DNB Plug,' Koji gained over 140,000 followers across TikTok
and Instagram by curating high-quality DnB content, championing underground artists, and building trust as a go-to source for fans of the genre. His following has helped fuel a movement, not just a music project.

After years of supporting the scene, Koji made the leap from influencer to artist. Known now for his high-energy productions and emotionally-charged sets, he blends the raw grit of jump-up with the soul of liquid and melodic bass. His music reflects a deep love for rave culture, storytelling, and making people feel seen, free, and unstoppable.

Koji has released on respected labels like Riot Records, High Tea Records, PUREFILTH, and Th3rd Brain. His viral remix of Billie Eilish’s Ocean Eyes turned the pop hit into a dreamy liquid anthem, while originals like FAKE and Same Thoughts showcase his balance of heavy basslines and emotion.

He’s played major festivals like Electric Love, Jungle Bells, Altitude Festival; made his U.S. debut at Exchange LA; sold out headline shows at Après Après and Coastal Sound Club, etc. He’s also supported legends including
Pendulum, ShockOne, Arcando, T&Sugah, Culture Shock, Reaper, AEON:Mode, and Sub Zero.

Beyond music, Koji founded KUMO, a promo group pushing bass culture on the Canadian West Coast. Through KUMO and his personal brand, he’s promoted hundreds of tracks, mentored emerging talent, and helped build a thriving community.

Rooted in Japanese philosophy, community empowerment, and a bold DIY spirit, Koji Aiken isn’t just another artist; he’s a movement in motion.

data.body:
Machinedrum is the long-running project of North Carolina–born producer and composer Travis Stewart, widely regarded as one of the most versatile and forward-thinking figures in electronic music. Across more than two decades, Stewart has built a catalog that moves fluently between drum and bass, hip-hop, jazz, R&B, footwork, and experimental club music, unified by a precise rhythmic sensibility and meticulous sound design. His solo albums Vapor City, Human Energy, and A View of U are widely considered modern touchstones, earning praise from Pitchfork, Resident Advisor, Mixmag, DJ Mag, Rolling Stone, and more, while his production and collaboration credits span artists such as Freddie Gibbs, Tinashe, Dawn Richard, Azealia Banks, DJ Rashad, and Sub Focus.

His recent work reflects both technical depth and personal reflection. The album 3FOR82 expands on themes introduced on 2023’s 4#TRAX EP, functioning as a love letter to his younger self, the #TRAX IRC community, and the demoscene where he first learned electronic music-making. Built using custom sound banks created in Impulse Tracker via a DOS emulator, the record carries a raw, tactile character shaped by sharp tempo shifts and intricate beat construction. Its companion release 3RMX82 features reinterpretations of the material by a curated group of producers, extending the album’s ideas into new club contexts.

Alongside Machinedrum, Stewart is also one quarter of VIER, a collaborative project representing his most recent body of work and exploring darker, more physical club territory. He is further known for projects such as J-E-T-S with Jimmy Edgar, Sepalcure with Praveen Sharma, and Dream Continuum with Om Unit, as well as scoring for film and television. As a DJ, Machinedrum is celebrated for seamlessly connecting disparate tempos and styles into cohesive, high-impact sets, with appearances at major festivals and venues worldwide including Coachella, Glastonbury, Sonar, Dekmantel, Primavera, and Electric Forest.

data.body: Sam Binga is at the intersection of dance music’s most dynamic styles. The Bristol-based cornerstone has built an unmatched reputation for high-energy, genre-fluid DJ sets and forward-thinking productions that fuse global club sounds with UK soundsystem grit. From collaborations with Skream, Bianca Oblivion and Nikki Nair, to UKG anthems with Bakey, leftfield techno with Sister Zo and electro experiments with Fracture, Binga embodies the raw, cheeky intensity of underground dance culture. His award-winning Pineapple label continues to spotlight rising stars like Cesco, Amy Kisnorbo and Sir Hiss, while his Club Orthodontics mixtape cemented his reputation as one of the most vital voices in modern club music.

data.body:
When you’re as iconic a figure as Josh Wink, it’s not easy to sum up a career in just a few words—but there are a couple of terms that help capture the essence of the influential electronic music producer’s body of work. One of the most fitting, certainly, would be longevity. A quick look at Wink’s impressive résumé shows: starting out in the early ’80s as a teenage mobile DJ; playing a major role in building Philadelphia’s warehouse scene during the house music explosion later that decade; creating international underground and chart-topping club hits like “Don’t Laugh,” “Higher State of Consciousness,” and “I’m Ready”; founding Ovum Recordings, one of the most respected labels in dance music; and, after more than thirty years, remaining one of electronic music’s most vital, innovative, and forward-thinking artists.
Wink’s dedication to innovation remains as strong today as ever. In recent years, he’s released a series of acclaimed singles and remixes including “Shoelaces,” “Balls,” “Autonomic Journeys,” and “Let Go.” His music continues to be embraced and supported by a new generation of artists such as John Summit, Dom Dolla, Mochakk, Peggy Gou, Charlotte De Witte, Eli Brown, and many more—testament to the timelessness and continued relevance of his sound. His “30 Years of Higher State of Consciousness” celebration and the ongoing “607 Sessions” have reintroduced his pioneering sound to a new generation, reaffirming his influence on modern techno and house. Wink has also expanded into curation with his Apple Music “Found Sounds” mix series—an exploration of ambient, deep, and experimental music that showcases another layer of his creative range.
Another word that captures Wink’s career is versatility. From the acid breakbeat innovation of “Higher State of Consciousness” to the organ-driven groove of “Stay Out All Night,” from the pulsating ambience of “Horizontal Dancing” to the cult techno of “Meditation Will Manifest” (reissued by R&S two decades after its debut), and through anthemic releases such as “Talking to You,” “Denial,” “Resist,” and “Shoelaces”—his output has consistently pushed boundaries while remaining authentic. “For me, ‘Resist’ was a way to bring awareness to people about their rights, their voice, their beliefs, their thoughts. They matter,” Wink says.
Drawing inspiration from a variety of genres, Wink has always resisted easy categorization. As a regular participant in West Philly DJ battles during his teens, his productions incorporate elements of techno, house, ambient, hip-hop, and drum & bass, creating a unique blend of styles. His remix credits spanning over 25 years include Radiohead, Moby, Sting, Depeche Mode, Radio Slave, and Dave Clarke, among others.
Ovum Recordings, now in its third decade, continues to thrive as a global tastemaker label. With early releases from Loco Dice, KINK, and Davide Squillace, along with essential records by D’Julz, Harry Romero, Chymera, Steve Bug, Levon Vincent, and Technasia, Ovum remains a platform for timeless music rather than fleeting trends.
Which brings us to a final, defining word: integrity. It’s a trait that Wink possesses in abundance—one that has carried him through the cycles and shifts of dance culture. While others have chased trends, Wink has continued to follow his intuition and passion. “I got into this because it was something that I lived and ate and breathed,” he says. “There was so much passion for the music—there was nothing else for me.”
Thirty years later, Josh Wink still stands as an unwavering artist at the crossroads of history and innovation—his sound as relevant, unpredictable, and inspired as ever.

data.body: Born and raised in the Kootenays, Avyon blends the swing of UK Garage with the immersive energy of Dubstep. Drawing inspiration from the UK dubstep scene in both her DJing and music production, she channels its deep, bass-driven roots into a sound that feels both authentic and powerful. Her sets bring something special to the dancefloor—deep, gritty, and full of energy. With her signature style, Avyon delivers music that moves bodies and shakes systems.

data.body:
Blending the richness of cutting edge electronic music with the depth and emotion of blistering guitar licks and live vocals

Chimp It’s signature move is to keep their fans guessing, as they weave their original
material into edits and remixes from their favourite artists and peers, moving through 140, UKG, Dubstep, DnB or whatever fresh gems they’ve uncovered in the far reaches of the music-verse.

Alongside releasing music on Gravitas and DoYu, Chimp It have been selected for some great time slots at events such as Shambhala, Future Forest, Wicked Woods, Electric Love, Friendzy Fest, Pachena Bay and many more. They have landed direct support slots and jammed live with acts such as Moontricks, Lazy Syrup Orchestra, Dirtwire, Smalltown DJs, Skiitour, The Librarian, Burchill, Woofax and Kytami & Phonik Ops.

data.body:
ASHEZ is a global bass music sensation hailing from Auckland, New Zealand. His innovative sound has captivated audiences worldwide, amassing millions of streams across various platforms.

Throughout his career, ASHEZ has collaborated with some of the biggest names in the bass music scene, including CloZee, Opiuo, Truth, Stylust, and K+Lab. He has toured extensively across the U.S., Canada, Australia, and New Zealand, performing at renowned festivals such as Lightning in a Bottle, Envision, Texas Eclipse, Shambhala, Rabbits Eat Lettuce, Splendour In The Grass, and Esoteric.

With releases on major labels like Warner Music Australia, Deep Dark and Dangerous, Wakaan, Odyzey, and Wormhole Music Group, ASHEZ continues to push the boundaries of bass music. Known for his high-energy performances and culturally creative style, he offers a refreshing take on the genre.

data.body: SHIMA is a DJ, producer, and songwriter from Tokyo, Japan. At age 14, she began her career as a J-pop singer after being scouted by a major label, and performed in a girl group while attending school full-time. Ultimately deciding that the commercial pop world was not for her, she moved to the United States at age 18 to study Music & Computer Engineering. During this time, she began to produce and write her own music as well as produce for her artist friends. After graduating University, she continued to make music in her free time while working full-time as a software developer. She quietly developed her craft and unique “sound” as an artist while saving up enough money to eventually quit her job and pursue music full-time. Three years in the making, her debut album “Welcome to SHIMAJIMA” via Young Art Records is the first look into a meticulously crafted artist project that extends beyond the music. A truly innovative blend of modern electronic dance music with elements of traditional Japanese folk music and culture, this meticulously crafted concept album is a showcase of high-level musicianship, razor-sharp vision, and contagious passion of an artist set free.

performances.body: SHIMA is a DJ, producer, and songwriter from Tokyo, Japan. At age 14, she began her career as a J-pop singer after being scouted by a major label, and performed in a girl group while attending school full-time. Ultimately deciding that the commercial pop world was not for her, she moved to the United States at age 18 to study Music & Computer Engineering. During this time, she began to produce and write her own music as well as produce for her artist friends. After graduating University, she continued to make music in her free time while working full-time as a software developer. She quietly developed her craft and unique “sound” as an artist while saving up enough money to eventually quit her job and pursue music full-time. Three years in the making, her debut album “Welcome to SHIMAJIMA” via Young Art Records is the first look into a meticulously crafted artist project that extends beyond the music. A truly innovative blend of modern electronic dance music with elements of traditional Japanese folk music and culture, this meticulously crafted concept album is a showcase of high-level musicianship, razor-sharp vision, and contagious passion of an artist set free.

data.body:
25-year-old LYNY, aka Alec Leinhauser’s growth as an artist has been inescapable. He is pushing the boundaries of electronic music by discovering and combining new cutting-edge sounds with melodic compositions. His approach to music is heavily influenced by Hip-Hop and trap beats with a splash of R&B. He strives to balance his songs with heart wrenching basslines, arpeggiated melodies, and hard hitting drums.

Some of LYNY’s stand out tunes include ‘Section’, ‘Victory Lap 5’, and ‘Light Up’ which have been played out by industry heavy hitters on massive stages around the globe. Along with the originals, LYNY has been known to create infectious edits such as his version of Bass Cannon by Flux Pavillion and ‘Jump Bros!’ which is a flip of ‘Jumpin’ by Destiny’s Child mixed with the ‘Super Smash Bros. Brawl’ theme melody. LYNY’s versatility has allowed him to touch many genres, progressing his sound to a very unique place. Many have simply known LYNY for his classic tag line, Luh-Luh-LYNY. The success of his music has earned major support from acts like Fred Again..., RL Grime, Hamdi, and Peekaboo among many more.

“Music has always been a big part of my life. I’ve been producing and writing music for as long as I can remember and it has become an essential form of self expression. I started the LYNY project in 2016 with a vision and goals in mind as I wanted to start fresh on a project with direction. It’s been a non-stop grind of learning the art of production, focusing on creating my own sound, and making tracks that are unusually unique while stressing on keeping them high-quality and clean. There is nothing I would rather be doing and I am super thankful for my journey thus far” - LYNY

data.body:
Zingara is an electrifying force in the bass music scene, blending her ethereal soundscapes with powerful bass to create an experience that deeply with audiences. One of the fastest-rising names in electronic music, her journey skyrocketed with her sold-out debut headline tour in 2022 + 2023, supporting her standout single Astra.

Following the success of her debut album The Code of Dreamz, Zingara dove deeper into an exploration of dream-inspired music with her For The Crystal Children EP in 2024. She followed that up in 2025 with her EP The Magic Garden, featuring collaborations with artists such as Jessica Chertock and Lowcation.

Notable festival appearances include Envision, Okeechobee, Lost Lands, Bass Canyon, Lightning In A Bottle, Electric Forest, and more. Zingara also supported LSDREAM on his Radical Audio Visual Experience tour across 34+ states in Fall 2023, and joined LSZEE (LSDREAM + CloZee) on their Spring 2025 tour.

This year, the bass powerhouse is embarking on an exciting co-headline tour with LEVEL UP. With her growing presence and upcoming projects, Zingara is one to follow in 2026 and beyond.

data.body:
With her spiritual and evolutionary sound, French producer Chloé Herry is a worldly and rhythmic figure in the electronic music atmosphere. Now based in Denver, CloZee has made a name for herself over the years by skewing the usual curves of bass music. In 2020, CloZee started a new journey with the launch of her own label, Odyzey Music, devoted to highlighting the underground artists of bass music. This same dedication translates to the live sphere as well, with Odyzey Music hosting official stage takeovers at festivals like Electric Forest, Elements, Resonate and many more, in addition to CloZee’s own Voyage Festival.

With the inception of Odyzey Music, CloZee also released her sophomore album Neon Jungle in 2020, which later saw official remixes by Lane 8, Opiuo, Emancipator, and Of the Trees, and was followed by her Nouvelle Era EP in 2021. As one of bass music’s foremost female innovators, CloZee has garnered attention across the electronic music spectrum, even gracing the cover of DJ Mag in December 2022. In July 2023, she then released her 3rd studio album “Microworlds,” and 2024 saw the debut album of her collaborative project LSZEE with LSDREAM. Since then, CloZee has continued to release groundbreaking tracks under both her solo project and LSZEE and tour major venues and festivals. Expect 2026 to be a milestone year for the diversifying producer.

data.body:
Kai Wachi has been a dominant force in bass music since his debut. His thrilling productions continue to break new ground as the Boise creative has become accustomed to bridging different styles for dynamic hybrid works. Although he has been highly acclaimed for extraordinary sound design and his hip-hop signatures, his emotionally driven musical sensibilities shine equally as bright within his vast catalog that holds over one hundred million streams on Spotify alone.

Over the years Kai has established himself as one of the industry’s most promising producers with support from the likes of Excision, NGHTMRE and many more. Records including his debut album, DEMIGOD, which includes collaborations with Famous Dex, Sullivan King, Grabbitz and more, and his Spotify Top 10 charting Nekrosis LP have been particularly integral to his more recent growth as they’ve mesmerized fellow artists, fans and curators at both Apple and Spotify. He’s also collaborated with the likes of Excision, Dabin, Dylan Matthew, PhaseOne and Apashe, as well as done official remixes for Tech N9ne, BTSM, Subtronics and Svdden Death.

Kai has been hailed as an MVP at festivals including Lost Lands, EDC Las Vegas, Paradiso and Wobbleland, as well as becoming a mainstay on Kannibalen Records while headlining several tours of his own and playing b2b sets with Black Tiger Sex Machine as Skull Machine. With one of the best independent labels and booking agencies at his back, there is no limit to Kai Wachi’s success in the future where he will continue to advance electronic music forward.

data.body:
A core presence within the Village and a hands-on builder of the Village Stage, Triple XL’s connection to the culture runs far deeper than performance alone.

From leading the build of the structures that carry the sound, to shaping the energy on the dancefloor, his presence is woven directly into the infrastructure and spirit of the Village itself.

Triple XL’s sets are fast-moving, unpredictable, and built around instinct. Blending multiple styles with sharp transitions and playful shifts in direction, he keeps dancers guessing without losing cohesion.

Whether in Victoria or on the Farm, his approach favors movement over stagnation—leaning into spontaneity while maintaining total control of the room.

data.body: [IVY] is a DJ and Producer in the Drum and Bass scene, blending genres to create a unique and energetic sound. Originally from rural Brittany, France, she developed a love for electronic music at European hardstyle and rave events. Her style incorporates influences from hard techno, jungle, and euphoric early 2000s dance tracks. With huge international & UK festivals like EDC LV, Glastonbury, Tomorrowland and Boomtown already under her belt, [IVY] continues to solidify her place in the industry by playing her own tracks out to massive crowds. [IVY] is celebrated as a triple-threat artist - seamlessly balancing busy touring and studio schedules with a strong social media presence. This multifaceted approach has contributed to her growing and diverse fanbase, and has landed her nominations for awards with DJ Mag and The DNB awards. Beyond music, [IVY] channels her creativity into Jazzy Garms, a rave fashion brand celebrating individuality and bold style. Scroll down to find [IVY]s full bio!

Paint & Create with Paint With Kelsie. Meet Kelsie, a professional artist and painting instructor from Vancouver Island, who welcomes you into a vibrant space designed to unblock your inner artist and provide a grounding moment of pure creation. We are offering two distinct workshop styles this year. All supplies are provided, just bring your open heart and experimental spirit! Guided Workshops: Follow along step-by-step to paint a whimsical festival keepsake to take home. We have two unique Shambhala-themed paintings this year. Intuitive Workshops: Ditch the rules in a freeform, meditative session. Focus entirely on the flow of paint, bold colours, and tap into pure creative bliss.
performances.name: Paint Night With Kelsie

performances.body: At Shambhala, two modern-day wizards invite you into an alchemical jewelry workshop where wire becomes art. In this hands-on session, participants will learn the fundamentals of wire wrapping while crafting pendants and rings using copper, bronze, and brass, and setting crystals with intention and style. Blending skilled instruction with playful magic, the workshop is welcoming to beginners and inspiring for experienced makers alike. Come create a wearable talisman and leave with new skills, good vibes, and a touch of wizardry. ✨
performances.name: Wire Wrapping with Wizards

performances.body: Meet yourself through movement & play as we explore the micro- and macrocosmic aspects of dance. Whether you’re a big mover or a small bopper when it comes to the dance floor—all flows are welcome in this workshop! Ever wondered how to get into a flow state but perhaps flow props are intimidating? Well-versed but want to explore something a little… different? This workshop blends Physics 101 LITE with Somatic Therapy in a curated but freeform dance offering from experienced dancer/facilitator Sheradan Mage (Sheradical). Who knows? This may just be what activates your own Theory of Everything! Gravitate in at any time! Strings provided, but no strings attached.
performances.name: String Theory Somatics

performances.body: Enough with the morning power flows, our bodies already did the most last night! This class is your go-to, zenned-out, bliss-focused 60 minutes to let simple restful postures and gravity do the work for you and get your body ready for tonight! Roll out of your tent and onto your mat to reset. Expect easy breathing, long hip openers and maybe some fun surprises to open your hearts and minds to the rest of the weekend, ragers! We gotta take care of ourselves and each other this weekend!
performances.name: Restore For Ragers Yoga

performances.body: This dance jam is for anyone who loves contact improv dance, and anyone who’s never heard of it and wants to come learn! This space will be lightly facilitated with prompts to get your creativity flowing. We’ll move to the sounds of surrounding sets (between stages here), and find our flow together. Both Blaize and Sophie will be on deck throughout to welcome and instruct newcomers and monitor for safety.
performances.name: Contact Dance Jam

performances.body: In this workshop you’ll pair resourcing practices from the Somatic Experiencing lineage with guided meditation, leading you softly into deep rest. Then Reiki Master Sophie and Blaize will come around to each participant offering energy healings while you relax. This Sunday workshop is the perfect deeply restful healing to close out your weekend.
performances.name: Somatic Resourcing Meditation and Reiki

performances.body: We’ve all been there—it’s Day 4, you’re exhausted, your homies' bad habits are grating on your last nerve, and suddenly you’re full-on feuding with the person you came to Shambs with to make lifelong memories! Enter: Somatics for Festie Bestie Communication. We all have a nervous system. This workshop not only breaks down the steps of non-violent communication in simple, easy-to-remember-even-at-4-am lingo, it pairs these conflict repair skills with body-based tools (somatics) that help you go from fired up to feud-free in seconds so you can get through the rough moments peacefully and go back to celebrating like you came to do (maybe after a nap). Come learn, practice playfully in small groups, and leave armoured with crisis-de-escalation and soothing skills that will help you the rest of the weekend and the rest of your lives!
performances.name: Festie Bestie Communication

performances.body: Unlock Your Shine is an empowerment- and somatic-based workshop designed around radical inclusion. Practiced in a circle to create a container of connection, collaboration, and belonging, this workshop is rooted in awareness, love, and acceptance. Beam will guide you through a nuanced practice of playfully deep offerings to help you align with your brightest, most authentic self. Her teachings encourage you to begin to become aware of where you put yourself “in a box” so you can reclaim your power and freedom, instead welcoming all your gifts and dreams. You can expect to feel expanded, stretched, uplifted, grounded, and nurtured as we move through different play-based embodiments, animals, elements, breathwork, tapping, energy clearing, stretching, shaking, scream therapy, group connection activities, and more unique practices allowing space for new insights and revelations. This offering is food for your soul. You are welcome here. Time to Unlock Your Shine!
performances.name: Unlock Your Shine

performances.body: A gently guided journey through somatic practices that can help you optimize your rave experience, including breathwork, grounding, and body-mind centering. Designed to provide participants with usable tools to use on and off the dance floor to support the nervous system and enhance the ability to connect to oneself, others, the land, and, of course, the music.

performances.body: Farmily Open Mic hosted by Christa Rae. Calling all musicians, poets, writers, and comics—here is the opportunity to showcase your talents and play a stage at Shambhala Music Festival. Come down and show us your magic.
performances.name: Farmily Open Mic

performances.body: Meraki Rising utilizes bowls and gongs to create a serene sound bath meditation that balances and activates the root, heart, and crown chakras. Sound meditation heals and balances the chakras through entrainment. Entrainment synchronizes brainwaves by providing a stable frequency, levelling out our beta state of consciousness. This, in turn, heals the body and mind on an internal level. What can sound healing help with? Anxiety, stress, depression, insomnia, PTSD and addictions. Attendees can expect reduced stress and anxiety, increased happiness and optimism, and a gentler integration into the festival experience.
performances.name: Chakra Sound Meditations

performances.body: This yoga asana (posture) sequence is a journey through the 5 elements. Ether, air, fire, water, and earth are the foundations of the world we live in. They are also the building blocks of our physical environment, and each element has inherent qualities that we will explore through breath and movement. To begin, we explore the air element through pranayama (breathing exercises). Second, we’ll ignite our inner fire through core activation and strong movements. The water element is then experienced through fluid motions, leading us eventually to grounding postures as we encounter earth. Lastly, we’ll transition from the heaviness of earth to the expansive and light nature of ether with a guided rest / Savasana to close the practice. What makes this sequence unique is the knowledge and awareness that is built based on the recognition of each element in our everyday lives (and bodies). Attendees will leave with a fresh perspective—feeling refreshed both mentally and physically.
performances.name: 5 Elements Yoga

performances.body: Alireza is a movement teacher and practitioner. Over the years, he has delved into various practices such as yoga, acrobatics, climbing, and dance. Alireza’s mission as a teacher is to inspire people to adopt a movement practice that goes beyond simply improving strength, flexibility, and aesthetic appeal. He believes that a meaningful practice allows for exploration of the body in the fullest way and facilitates playfulness, self-discovery, and connection.
performances.name: Morning Yoga

performances.body:
As the day winds down, join Georgia Aurelia for an evening practice designed to prepare your body for a night of adventure, and dancing. This flow will focus on warming up the joints and building mobility through different parts of the body so you can outlast the night.


Georgia invites you to drop into your body, step into the night feeling loose, connected, and ready to dance!
performances.name: Sundown Yoga Flow with Georgia

performances.body: Awaken your senses with Georgia Aurelia’s nurturing morning yoga session, a perfect way to prepare for a weekend of joy, movement, and connection. Celebrating her fourth year teaching at Shambhala, Georgia is thrilled to return and practice with you again. Her approach blends gentle flow, mobility, and strength-focused movement to leave you feeling refreshed, balanced, and inspired.
performances.name: Morning Yoga Flow

performances.body:
Foundations of Acroyoga (Beginner)
Introducing the concepts of stack, push and pull in a variety of grounded base positions and standing counterbalances. Here we include an extra focus on the art of spotting for beginner safety and success.
performances.name: Foundations of Acroyoga (Beginner)

performances.body:
This yoga asana (posture) sequence is a journey through the 5 elements. Ether, Air, Fire, Water and Earth are the foundations of the world we live in. They are also the building blocks of our physical environment, and each element has inherent qualities that we will explore through breath and movement.
To begin, we explore the air element through pranayama (breathing exercises). Second, we’ll ignite our inner fire through core activation and strong movements. The water element is then experienced through fluid motions, leading us eventually to grounding postures as we encounter earth. Lastly, we’ll transition from the heaviness of earth to the expansive and light nature of ether with a guided rest (Savasana) to close the practice. What makes this sequence unique is the knowledge and awareness that is built based on the recognition of each element in our everyday lives (and bodies). Attendees will leave with a fresh perspective - feeling refreshed both mentally and physically.
performances.name: Acro Yoga Elemental Flow (Intermediate)

performances.body: Exploring mono L-base balances, inversions and standing partner acrobatics.
performances.name: Advance Acro (Circus-style)

performances.body: A safe creative space to come play! We will spice things up with games and challenges to stimulate imagination and deepen connection.
performances.name: Guided Acroyoga Jam

performances.body: A welcoming drop-in creative sanctuary where festival attendees can learn how to transform a single sheet of paper into a miniature handmade book. Participants decorate their books using collage materials, stickers, markers, and found textures, then carry the books throughout the festival as evolving memory journals. Guests are encouraged to return to the booth any time they need a creative reset, quiet moment, or place to reflect and continue adding memories, drawings, poetry, notes, and experiences gathered throughout the weekend.
performances.name: Mini Memory book making at the Wishing Tree

performances.body: Join SMF Games hosts Dustin, Amanda, Jess, and Curt for a rotating lineup of delightfully interactive games, pop-up contests, and whimsical experiences designed to spark joy, laughter, and connection in the heart of Downtown Shambhala. From parachute games and UNO tournaments to bingo, karaoke, and spontaneous silliness, this is your invitation to play like a kid and vibe like a festival legend. Whether you’re competing for ridiculous prizes or just making new friends, every session brings something fresh. Inspired by the spirit of Shambhala—creative, kind, and full of surprises—these immersive activations invite you to be part of the magic. Come once or come every day. It’s different every time.

Once you find her, have a seat and let this enchanting experience bring back your childhood awe. This whimsical experience of bubble artistry will create joy, curiosity, and delight for all who wander through.

At the Wordplay Workshop we use rapping, rhyming and songwriting as tools for self-discovery, self expression and self-development. We connect to breath, sound and movement, play interactive vocal warm-up games, and then I guide participants through a structured process to help them learn to rap and work through any creative or personal blocks, offering participants an opportunity to connect with their voice, be seen and heard, and have ridiculous amounts of fun freestyling, writing poetry and performing together in a cypher format.
performances.name: Wordplay Workshop
