performances.body: The Bloom Brigade brings a high-functioning, nature-inspired ecosystem to the Ranch, blending professional circus skills with interactive community care. Our towering stilt flowers provide a visual anchor for the crowd, roaming the grounds to offer a literal "Dew-Drop Refresh" with cooling misters for overheated dancers. They are supported by our ground-level Busy Bees, a high-energy roving duo equipped with "pollen" self-care kits and construction tools to "repair" vibes and assist participants in need.
LED circus performance
performances.name: Salty Circus LED Stage Performance

performances.body:
This year’s concept centers on a female figure holding a kitsune fox mask, surrounded by abstract forms designed to create shapes and negative space for projection mapping. The mask symbolizes choice, allowing the wearer or the viewer to step into different facets of oneself. Drawing from kitsune fox mythology as a shapeshifter existing between worlds, the mural reflects Shambhala as a space of transformation where people reconnect with parts of themselves that may be hidden in everyday life. The fox introduces a secondary archetype, grounding the work in instinct and nature through a connection to local wildlife, while expanding opportunities for personal reflection. Projection-mapped layers will include poetry and intentional calligraphy that moves through the mural, adding a textual dimension that deepens the narrative and invites further interpretation.

This installation creates a uniquely immersive experience, blending visual and textual storytelling, symbolic archetypes that represent the felt experience of Shambhala, and spaces for pause; it offers a central gathering point where festival-goers can reflect, decompress, and reconnect with themselves. The installation encourages people to stop and rest, providing a safe space amid the festival’s intensity with the potential to help attendees stay balanced, creating a strong impact through both engagement and emotional grounding.

Kerzeka is a dynamic artistic duo formed by Kerz and Zeka, whose unique styles blend seamlessly to create impactful, large-scale murals and visual art. Tiziano Lombardo—Kerz (born in Rome, now based in Calgary)—is a muralist with over six years of experience in the field. Originally rooted in European street art, he has expanded his practice to embrace multiple styles of professional muralism. His work is inspired by abstract forms, technology, and the urban energy of street art. Combining hard-edged shapes with expressive strokes and mixed media, Kerz creates compositions that range from bold, abstract works to semi-realistic designs. Desiree Devloo—Zeka, a Canadian-born artist, explores a wide spectrum of styles, from abstract art to realism. Her work draws inspiration from the natural world and the power of communication. She often creates portraits, animal figures, and calligraphy, using a unique mix of abstract shapes, soft blending techniques, and contrasting textures. Her compositions guide the viewer’s eye toward realistic or impressionistic focal points, evoking a sense of depth and narrative. As Kerzeka, they demonstrate how collaboration can elevate art, combining their distinct styles to create a balanced fusion of structure and flow. Their work is a testament to the strength of creative partnerships, showing how two voices can come together to tell a more powerful, collective story.

Chris Devious—introducing Chris Devious, a veteran DJ and master of event visuals, whose journey began over 15 years ago in the heart of Western Canada. Inspired by the pulsating beats of the music scene, Chris embarked on a quest to become a powerhouse in the industry. From humble beginnings in Grande Prairie, Alberta, Chris quickly rose to prominence, securing residencies at major clubs and sharing the stage with iconic acts like Neon Steve, Stickybuds, Document One, Chris Webby, and Merkules. But Alireza’s talents extend far beyond the turntables. Alongside his career as a professional DJ, he has also mastered the art of event visuals at a top-tier level, collaborating with some of the greats in Western Canada. His ability to create immersive experiences through lighting and visuals has earned him widespread acclaim and admiration throughout the industry. Venturing to Calgary and Kelowna, Chris continued to shine, joining the Wubs in the Woods team and honing his skills in music production and concert-level visuals. Today, Chris Devious is known for his electrifying performances and his unparalleled talent in both music and visuals, creating unforgettable experiences for audiences everywhere.
performances.name: Lumen Drift

performances.body:

Projection-mapped layers will include poetry and intentional calligraphy that moves through the mural, adding a textual dimension that deepens the narrative and invites further interpretation.

This installation creates a uniquely immersive experience, blending visual and textual storytelling, symbolic archetypes that represent the felt experience of Shambhala, and spaces for pause. It offers a central gathering point where festival-goers can reflect, decompress, and reconnect with themselves. The piece encourages people to stop and rest, providing a safe space amid the festival’s intensity with the potential to help attendees stay balanced, creating a strong impact through both engagement and emotional grounding.

Kerzeka is a dynamic artistic duo formed by Kerz and Zeka, whose unique styles blend seamlessly to create impactful, large-scale murals and visual art. Tiziano Lombardo - Kerz (born in Rome, now based in Calgary) is a muralist with over six years of experience in the field. Originally rooted in European street art, he has expanded his practice to embrace multiple styles of professional muralism. His work is inspired by abstract forms, technology, and the urban energy of street art. Combining hard-edged shapes with expressive strokes and mixed media, Kerz creates compositions that range from bold, abstract works to semi-realistic designs.

Desiree Devloo - Zeka, a Canadian-born artist, explores a wide spectrum of styles, from abstract art to realism. Her work draws inspiration from the natural world and the power of communication. She often portrays portraits, animals, and calligraphy, using a unique mix of abstract shapes, soft blending techniques, and contrasting textures. Her compositions guide the viewer’s eye toward realistic or impressionistic focal points, evoking a sense of depth and narrative. As Kerzeka, they demonstrate how collaboration can elevate art, combining their distinct styles to create a balanced fusion of structure and flow. Their work is a testament to the strength of creative partnerships, showing how two voices can come together to tell a more powerful, collective story.

Introducing Chris Devious, a veteran DJ and master of event visuals whose journey began over 15 years ago in the heart of Western Canada. Inspired by the pulsating beats of the music scene, Chris embarked on a quest to become a powerhouse in the industry. From humble beginnings in Grande Prairie, Alberta, Chris quickly rose to prominence, securing residencies at major clubs and sharing the stage with iconic acts like Neon Steve, Stickybuds, Document One, Chris Webby, and Merkules. But Chris’s talents extend far beyond the turntables. Alongside his career as a professional DJ, he has also mastered the art of event visuals at a top-tier level, collaborating with some of the greats in Western Canada. His ability to create immersive experiences through lighting and visuals has earned him widespread acclaim and admiration throughout the industry. Venturing to Calgary and Kelowna, Chris continued to shine, joining the Wubs in the Woods team and honing his skills in music production and concert-level visuals. Today, Chris Devious is known for his electrifying performances and his unparalleled talent in both music and visuals, creating unforgettable experiences for audiences everywhere.
performances.name: Lumen Drift

performances.body:
Self Reflectron combines cymatics and video feedback to create visual meditation—unpredictable, yet not random.

For his performance, he will play and project live psychedelics using an analog visual instrument that blends two techniques: cymatics and video feedback. Starting with creating a visualization of the sounds around us by taking a feed from the soundboard and plugging it into a small amplifier connected to a speaker with a small dish of water. This water will vibrate to the music. He will film these vibrations and we will be able to see them. This is cymatics. Plugging a camera into a TV and pointing the camera at that TV creates an infinite-mirror effect. This is video feedback. Using a vintage video mixer to blend the cymatics and the video feedback into a curated psychedelic trip, guests will be able to lie back and enjoy projections. The entire process will be on display. The subtleties of these two systems leave much to chance. As such, the images are not created so much as invited. Cymatics is synesthesia. Video feedback is consciousness represented tangibly. The experience of both combined is a visual meditation. Unpredictable, yet not random.

We invite you to come, lie back, and enjoy the ride.
performances.name: Cymatics - Live Analog Projection

performances.body: The Psychedelic Selfie is an interactive installation that takes the participant on a cosmic journey of recursive fractal wonder. There is no computer used in this organic video feedback loop. Rather, it is created with vintage gear that self-observes and recycles each moment into the next in a repeating chain of cause-and-effect. The phenomenon of video feedback has been described as analogous to the process of consciousness by Douglas Hofstadter in his groundbreaking book I Am a Strange Loop. The effect is one of synesthesia—a blending of the senses that entices one into a flow state.
performances.name: Psychedelic Selfie

performances.body: I am a mixed-media artist educator based in Calgary, Alberta. After graduating with a Bachelor of Fine Arts and a Bachelor of Education from the University of Calgary, I began teaching in the public school system while exploring my own style and designs. My work explores the intersection of creativity and learning with a focus on ways to enhance understanding through the use of art.

performances.body: Since 2022, Andrew Hatlen has used 3D Projection Mapping to share unique and engaging perspectives. Irregular shapes and colors appear naturally in the world around us and in the inspiring creations of other artists. Andrew specializes in respectfully borrowing these shapes and colors to create new layers of reinterpretation through temporary projected installations. Playing with depth and color disparity, Andrew uses light as a tool to add augmented layers that breathe new life into existing works of art.
performances.name: Andrew Hatlen Project

performances.body: Not a store. Not a trade. A shared wardrobe of magic ✨ Bring your gently used rave wear, costumes, and treasures, and add them to the collective. Browse the tables, discover something new-to-you, and refresh your festival style. No trades, no pressure—just take what you love and leave what you can. No trading, just sharing. Please bring five to six items max, and only what you’re ready to release. Help keep items loosely sorted as you go. At the end, all remaining clothing will be moved to a camp trading table to be enjoyed throughout the weekend. ? Come for the clothes, stay for the connection.
performances.name: Wook Will Clothing Swap Meetup

Come celebrate yourself with flags, ribbons, rainbows! All are welcome to join us as we dance our way through downtown. Bring your best moves and make some noise in support of 2SLGTBQAI+ communities. Meet at the Circle Garden Theatre at 1:00 PM, or join-in en route! Don’t worry about what to wear - put on the costume that reflects your most authentic self and join the party.

Hosted by The Lucid Fairy - a performance artist and electro-pop enchantress, weaving high-vibrational music, live vocals, energizing movement, and magic into unforgettable experiences. With a colourful drag aesthetic and affirmational lyrics, she uplifts audiences into higher states of joy, empowerment, and collective awakening. Follow the magic with @the.lucid.fairy and find her music on all streaming platforms.
performances.name: Pride Parade Meetup at 1pm

performances.body: Light Pirate is a Calgary-based interdisciplinary artist creating immersive worlds through shadow puppetry, animation, liquid light shows, and live visual performance. Combining hand-crafted analog techniques with digital tools, she layers motion, color, and projected imagery into vibrant punk-goth dreamscapes inspired by magical realism and storybook traditions. Part performance, part visual ritual, her work invites audiences into playful and transformative journeys through memory, identity, and imagination.

data.body:
Simula burst onto the drum and bass scene in 2016 with a signature style rooted in gritty, gun-finger-inducing jump-up. His early releases on heavyweight labels like Biological Beats, Low Down Deep, and Playaz quickly earned him a reputation as a rising force in the genre. Tracks such as Introvert, Ravioli, and The Light (co-produced with Annix) became instant anthems, showcasing his ability to craft relentless basslines and infectious grooves. As the years progressed, Simula began to evolve his sound, drawing influence from the wider spectrum of drum and bass.
Alongside Annix, he co-founded his own label, providing a creative space to refine his craft. This period saw the release of standout tracks like Blood Worm and Angels, further cementing his reputation as a versatile and innovative producer. With an ever-expanding catalogue of celebrated releases and a reputation for electrifying live performances, Simula continues to push boundaries, solidifying his status as one of drum and bass's most influential and forward-thinking artists.

A big name MC of the drum and bass world who pioneered a sub genre of dubstep which coined the term “Riddim” Jakes has never stopped making music with passion and progression.

data.body: I’m the Bone Goddess, a creator that gets to the marrow. Through tactile explorations of form, function, flow and visual language, I create from the heart and make work that aims to expand inner awareness, create safe spaces for deep emotional experience, empower the creative human spirit and embellish the outer world with pleasing textures - through divine play. Archetypal performance, movement magic, intuitive mark-making, embellished body adornment and deep exploration of self are the modes in which I operate, with my main medium being curiosity. Healing shame and finding joy through embracing discovery are the guides of my process.
data.name: The Marrow of Making

data.body:
Like the medicinally rich black tar from which their name is derived, shelajit channels a deep, refined essence into everything they create. A visionary music producer, dancer, and performer, they fuse electronic production with the organic resonance of the guzheng, crafting sonic landscapes that feel both timeless and innovative.

Rooted in emotion and atmosphere, their compositions seamlessly weave ethereal vocals with intricate sound design, blending traditional Chinese instrumentation with modern electronic textures. Their music is an exploration of contrast: organic and synthetic, delicate and raw, structured yet deeply intuitive.
data.name: Shelajit

data.body:
“I had to learn to get out of my own way,” says Matthew James Brewer, the British songwriter, producer, and multi-instrumentalist better known as Frameworks. “Instead of going out there chasing after songs, I had to learn to sit still and be present enough to let them come to me.”
In that sense, Reflections, Frameworks’ entrancing new LP, is as much a work of cultivation as it is craftsmanship, a meditative exercise in tuning into the subconscious and capturing moments as they arrive. Recorded at home and primarily alone, the album is spare and hypnotic, eschewing the ornate orchestration of earlier Frameworks albums in favor of a lean, direct sound more akin to an internal monologue. The songs here mix driving electronic beats with layers of analog synthesizers and chopped up vocal samples, and the production is close-up and intimate to match, swirling around the kind of late-night thoughts that raise more questions than they answer. The result is a poignant contemplation of growth, purpose, and catharsis built on instinct and intuition, a bittersweet soundtrack for both connection and isolation, for discovery and loss, for the crowded, sweaty dance floor and the long, lonely walk home.
“Even the more club-influenced tracks on this album have an element of vulnerability to them,” explains Brewer. “They might be songs you could dance to, but they’re also songs you could listen to on your headphones in bed well after the rest of the world has gone to sleep.”
Brewer’s been blurring the lines between dreams and reality for most of his career now. A Manchester native, he first rose to fame in 2015 with his critically acclaimed debut, Tides, which brought Frameworks’ cinematic mix of instrumental hip-hop and downtempo, organic house music to an international audience. In the years that followed, Brewer would go on to release two more similarly well-received full length albums, perform everywhere from Coachella to Electric Forest, collaborate with the likes of Ninja Tune’s Jono McCleery and fellow UK standout JP Cooper, and rack up millions of streams across platforms. When the pandemic hit, however, Brewer found himself forced off the road for the first time in years and unsure of what came next.
“I was really inspired by architecture at the time, so I thought I’d make an album called Forms that would be really focused on shape and structure and composition,” he explains. “I dove into it headfirst, but pretty quickly I realized that I was going about it all wrong. The more I forced it, the less natural it felt, and that’s when it became clear to me that I had to stop trying to control everything and just let go.”
And so Brewer slowed down and tuned in, treating his mind more like a receiver than a generator. He held his heart up to the light and examined what he saw: his hopes and desires, his doubts and fears. He reveled in the extra time at home with his family and in the beauty and strength they brought to his daily life.
“My wife and I have a son who was born with mitochondrial disease,” explains Brewer. “They told us he wouldn’t make it to his first birthday, but he’s seven years old now, and though he may be non-verbal, I swear he’s the happiest person I’ve ever met. He’s always present and always inspiring and every day with him is an absolute gift.”
The more Brewer surrendered himself to living in the moment, the more experimental and playful his music became. Songs began pouring out in spontaneous fits driven by emotion rather than intention, and soon a very different album than the one he’d originally imagined started taking shape.
“The best things came very quickly,” he explains. “If you press pause and try to come back to an idea later, it can be hard to get into the same state, so when a song would come to me, I’d just try to lay as much of it down as I could all at once.”
That urgency forms the bedrock of Reflections, which opens with the relentlessly hopeful “Twenty Two.” Inspired by a half-marathon Brewer ran for his son, the track presses forward with a seemingly unstoppable momentum as manipulated vocals skip and dance their way across the surface of the music. Like much of the album, it’s a deeply evocative track, one that manages to create an entire emotional world even without any explicit lyrical narrative. The pulsing “Blue Light” (one of three tracks featuring guest vocals from Australian singer Cleopold) conjures up visions of cruising the empty streets of a city late at night, while the invigorating “Cold” captures the tension and liberation of emotional release, and the mesmerizing “Circles” finds peace and resolve on a journey deeper and deeper inside the self.
“The video for ‘Circles’ depicts a young woman dealing with her own internal struggle,” Brewer explains. “The moments underwater reflect the battle within, and the final scenes show this moment of complete surrender and acceptance.”
Acceptance is an integral notion on Reflections, and it comes up throughout the album. The insistent “No Time,” for instance, circles around the same riff over and over like a mantra in effort to sit with (and welcome) the feelings that emerge; the hazy “Ports” draws inspiration from the strength and resilience of children—not just his own—that Brewer’s seen battle difficult illness with unyielding optimism; and the reassuring “Sleep” works through the stress and anxiety that often leaves us lying awake in the middle of the night.
“I was having real problems falling asleep during the pandemic, and ‘Sleep’ was a direct response to that,” explains Brewer. “I wanted it to feel like a loving arm to wrap around someone struggling with insomnia, a comforting voice to let them know they’re not alone.”
And ultimately, that’s the true magic of Brewer’s music. Though these songs were created in isolation, they remind us of our shared humanity, of the unifying frequencies we can all tap into if we allow ourselves to slow down, be present, and embrace the moment. Still waters, after all, offer the truest reflections.

data.body: Michael Red is a Vancouver-based music producer, film composer, live performer and event organizer, known for helping shape underground electronic music culture in Western Canada (both as an event curator and DJ). Some of his other projects include Low Indigo (events, label, community), Oro Azul (with Ultima Esuna), LA FE (with NAP), Light Blues (sound wellness project), Souns (ambient alias), and an extensive list of other projects and collaborations. This year at Shambhala, Michael will be returning to one of the roles he is best known for -> delivering a sunrise set on the final morning of the festival.
data.name: Michael Red

data.body:
JUNK — an acronym for Just Understand Nobody Knows — isn’t just a name, it’s a manifesto. A genre-defying, and battle-tested rapper of German, Italian, & Canadian descent, JUNK has carved out a singular legacy in the world of independent hip hop. Hailing from Vancouver, BC, he’s not only a fierce lyricist and electrifying performer but also the co-founder and owner of Household Records, a label and collective redefining West Coast Canadian hip hop from the ground up.

JUNK has shared the stage with some of the most respected names in the game, including Ludacris, Method Man, Redman, DJ Premier, Tech N9ne, Xzibit, Conway The Machine, Freddie Gibbs, Ghostface Killah, Raekwon, Talib Kweli, Jack Harlow, Action Bronson, MGK, Yelawolf, Masta Ace, and more — earning his stripes through skill, hustle, and an unmistakable presence.

His reach extends far beyond his hometown. JUNK has toured Europe with icons like Mobb Deep, Onyx & Snak The Ripper, and has hit stages across Canada with Meth & Red, Mayday, and Locksmith, while headlining his own national tours since 2018.

In the studio, JUNK’s discography boasts notable collaborations with elite artists such as Diamond D, Cyhi The Prynce, Millyz, Caskey, Flipp Dinero, Mickey Factz, and more — a testament to his respect within the culture and his ability to move seamlessly across sounds and circles. Whether on stage or on wax, JUNK continues to push boundaries, elevate his craft, and leave an indelible mark on independent hip hop.

Lifelong DJ! 45 Vinyl Specialist! Member of DJ Jazzy Jeff's Playlist Retreat Family! Known as Canada’s Hip Hop Ambassador, Flipout is a Vancouver-based DJ, producer, and vinyl collector with a legacy spanning over two decades. Renowned for his deep knowledge of music, Flipout has a special affinity for 7" 45rpm vinyl records. His love for these small but powerful records led him to co-found Flip N Jay Records with longtime collaborator Jay Swing. Known for his refined approach to collecting and DJing, Flipout has built a reputation as a true specialist in the 45 format, embracing its unique history and sound quality. Alongside his vinyl passion, Flipout is also a respected radio host, author, and the long-time emcee for the Red Bull 3Style World DJ Championships. Whether he’s digging for rare gems or curating eclectic sets, his deep connection to vinyl continues to define his career, making him a key figure in the world of music and DJ culture.
data.name: Flipout & Junk

data.body:
Sonic architects of songs with an emotional heft as heavy as the subwoofer rupturing basslines that underpin their work, TRUTH are one of New Zealand's finest creative exports. Respected across the globe since inception, TRUTH have as both award winning producers and world-class DJs, cut a clear pathway through the international dubstep scene.
On the global touring circuit, the fruits of their labor have included hundreds of shows & festivals across Europe, New Zealand, Australia, Asia, South & North America, as well as key hosted Deep Dark & Dangerous takeovers at some of the world’s top festivals, including Shambhala, Outlook Festival & Infrasound.
Building their performance presence through years of prolific production output, weekly mixes & streaming, and key DJ support from the likes of Mala, Skream, Liquid Stranger, Ganja White Night, Boogie T, to name just a few.
The duo have released music on their very own Deep Dark & Dangerous label, founded in 2012 and now home & incubator to a growing host of acts focused on the unmistakable DDD sound - as well a diverse list of others including Deep Medi Muzik, Disciple, Wakaan, and more.
Through dedication to their craft, TRUTH has seen their stock rise placing them as in-demand remixers and sound designers, their music finding its way into major television productions such as ‘American Horror Story’, receiving numerous awards including ‘Best Electronic Artist’ in the New Zealand Music Awards, and earning a following of ardent listeners worldwide.
Acclaimed by critics both within and outside the dubstep scene, their momentum is undeniable and impossible to ignore. Face the truth or get well outta the way.

PAV4N is the founding member of the critically acclaimed electronic rap group
Foreign Beggars and one of the most prolific MC’s of Indian origin of all time. He is
the first rapper of Indian descent to have a top 10 single in the UK Charts.
PAV4N has created alongside a plethora of incredible producers including Skrillex,
Noisia, Steve Aoki, Knife Party, Dj Vadim, Sez on the Beat, Flux Pavilion, Ganja White
Knight, Alix Perez, Visages, Truth, Tommy lee of Motley Cru and has 60 + releases

since the launch of his solo project in 2020.

With 7 Studio albums under his belt and 17 years of live show experience, PAV4N is
known for his explosive, high energy performances spanning genres of hip hop,

grime, dubstep & DnB.

Since 2021 ,PAV4N has performed at Glastonbury Festival, Boomtown, Shambhala,
Nozstock, Locus, NH7 Festival, Breezer Shuffle & he has just come off the back of

extensive India, UK & North American Tours,
data.name: Truth w/ PAV4n

data.body:
Teran Chilton Faust, aka TCHiLT, is a musician, producer, and DJ who's mission is simple: to make people dance. Born and raised in the San Francisco Bay Area, TCHiLT draws from old-school funk, hip-hop, and jazz to create a modern sound that touches the soul. Infectious bass lines meet nostalgic melodies while playful vocals dance atop organic percussion, breathing life into the electronic world. From House and Breaks to Midtempo Bass, TCHiLT is known for his all-original sets sprinkled with remixes of iconic hip-hop and funk classics.

Known for sets filled with unreleased anthems, emotional vulnerability and his unique brand of drum n bass, MNTRA has made a big impact on the West Coast underground music scene. His ability to invoke a visceral response from his audiences combined with his dedication to an ever-growing crew of fans, friends and fellow artists has lead him to where he is today - a beloved part of his community and a pioneer of a new sound.
data.name: TCHilT B2B MNTRA

data.body:
John Rolodex stands as one of North America’s most respected figures in drum and bass. From Alberta, Canada, he has built an international legacy usually reserved for UK artists, earning releases on iconic imprints such as Metalheadz, V Recordings, Hospital, and Suburban Base.
Across more than two decades, Rolodex has remained a forward-thinking producer whose sound continues to evolve. His music spans the full emotional and sonic spectrum, from vocal anthems like “That Jungle Vibe” (with T.Power) to heavyweight devastators such as “Dragon” and the cinematic depth of “The Rainmaker.” Each release bridges timeless jungle and rave sensibilities with precise modern aesthetics, reflecting both innovation and deep cultural roots.
As founder of Machinist Music, Rolodex continues to champion breakbeat-driven experimentation and authenticity. He has performed across North America and Europe and curates the Village Jungle Rinseout at Shambhala Music Festival, sharing his enduring passion for the art and culture of drum and bass with audiences worldwide.

Miss Fudge brings fire with her soulful lyrics and haunting vocals. A long-time admirer of deep bass lines and amen breaks, her haunting sound fuses effortlessly with DnB.

Brady Webster AKA MC Maponus is a master on the mic. Based in Calgary, where he was raised with a strong influence from Hip-hop culture, Brady later discovered Drum & Bass. He quickly found his lane by bringing an iconic style and ‘wow’ factor that seamlessly ties DJ sets together.
He consistently performs alongside the industry’s finest talent. Maponus is tried, tested and true wielding the microphone!
He is the resident host and MC for RunnIT Productions.
In recent years, Maponus has also emerged as a talented producer and DJ, bringing the same level of creativity and storytelling to his sets as he does to his mic work. He continues to develop a unique sound that blends weight, groove, and intention. His DJ sets are intentional and dynamic, often built around themes and moods, and showcase a deep knowledge of sound system music across tempos and styles.

Donna Dada is a Vancouver-based DJ whose sets move through UKG, speed garage, jungle, breaks, and bass with ease. Her Trinidadian-Nigerian background and long love affair with UK dance culture shape her sound which is always built for the dancefloor.
She’s played festivals like Bass Coast, Shambhala and Harvest Music Festivals, and has shared the stage with artists including Nia Archives, Sherelle, Hitech, Bushbaby, Sam Binga, and Bakey. Whether she’s in a club or an open-air stage, Donna Dada brings a distinctive energy that connects people through bass.

With a foundation in performance and formal classical music, IN2IT stepped into the electronic music scene in the early 2000s as both a DJ and music producer. His passion for rhythm, dance, and sound led him to purchase a sound system at age 23 and begin hosting renegade forest parties—sparking a lifelong devotion to music and associated gatherings.
Now living on the unceded ancestral lands of the Sinixt, Ktunaxa, Secwepemc peoples, IN2IT continues to channel that same fire. As co-owner of North Lake Sound, he carries forward the spirit of those early gatherings. He has continued to progress and sharpen his music production and DJ skills over the years. He plays at a variety of British Columbia rave and dance events, and has been featured in Shambhala Music Festival's Village Stage Jungle Rinse Out in 2024 and 2025. In 2025 he released a debut two-track single on In Da Jungle Recordings (Montreal), and is looking towards an upcoming international release for Spring/Summer 2026.
His sets move through a wide spectrum of dance music, with a sharp and soulful focus on DnB and Jungle. For him mixing is storytelling, promotion, and self-care - a way to share the artists and tracks he loves most, and a ritual that feels as much like therapy as it does celebration.

Pete Shredz was born in Toronto, now reppin' Salmo as a DJ, producer and promoter spinning Jungle, DnB, UK Garage, Dubstep, Footwork, 2-step and anything else that will help you keep your gun fingers in the air. Shredz brings his high energy, technical mixing to the decks whether he is playing digital or Vinyl. Shredz runs Capital Jungle & GFP Records and holds a monthly slot on Subtle Radio.

Strange Manner is on a constant journey to spread the message of the music that he loves. His blend of ragga, jungle and dnb is a testament to this musical pilgrimage as he showcases the past, present and future of these genres.
As a teen, Strange Manner was exposed to many different styles ranging from DJ Shadow, to John Holt, but it wasn’t until he heard the sound of the legendary Shy FX that opened him up to jungle and drum & bass and eventually started his DJ career. In 2015, he was a DJ in the Ragga Jungle Rinseout at Shambhala Music Festival, and has returned to play every year since. Strange Manner continues to expand his reach in Calgary and beyond as an artist, a DJ, and most of all
– a lifetime music enthusiast

Substance is a dubstep producer based in Sherbrooke, QC, Canada. His journey began in 2011 after discovering PK Sound and the vibrant West Coast Canadian rave scene. By 2012, he started promoting events with friends under the DirtyCake brand, bringing major dubstep acts like Truth, Goth Trad, and Hatcha to his hometown. These efforts helped build a dedicated bass music community and provided platforms for rising artists like Wraz.

After a decade of promoting shows and working at festivals across Canada, Substance shifted focus to his own productions. He quickly gained recognition, earning releases on renowned labels such as Sentry Records, Deep Dark & Dangerous, Wheel & Deal, and Nomine Sound. Expanding his repertoire, Substance has also dropped tracks on Boogie T’s Drama Club Recording and has releases on J:Kenzo’s Artikal Records. Collaborations with artists like Antennae and Abstrakt Sonance further showcase his range and ability to cross genres within bass music.
In addition to producing, Substance founded Gunfingaz Thursday, a popular Twitch stream that has become a go-to platform for highlighting emerging DJs and producers in the North American dubstep scene. His live performances have graced some of the continent’s top festivals, including
Shambhala Music Festival, Bass Coast, Infrasound, and Lightning in a Bottle. These appearances, combined with consistent releases and community involvement, cement his reputation as a dynamic force in bass music. With an exciting futur on the horizon, Substance continues to push boundaries, blending styles and elevating the global dubstep scene.

MITTZ is a Calgary-based DJ known for her high-energy sets, smooth transitions, and deep roots in drum & bass culture. Since launching her career in Edmonton in 2009, she has built a reputation across Western and Central Canada for delivering heavy basslines, rolling melodic vibes, and versatile broken-beat selections spanning DnB, dubstep, breakbeats, and hip hop.
An original member of the DnB Girls and Girls on Decks, Mittz has performed at countless clubs and festivals, including her first international appearance at the Boogaloo Art Car and Music Festival in 2017. Having worked with crews such as 403dnb, Funk Bunker, and Break Beat Dojo, she continues to make waves across the North American bass music scene.

DJ raSta (est. 2000) is a drum & bass and breaks veteran known for relentless, high-energy streaming sets and recurring appearances at Shambhala Music Festival Village Stage. No filler—just impact.
data.name: Village Rinse Out Hosted by John Rolodex

data.body:
A legendary staple of the Fractal Forest, the powerhouse pairing of Fort Knox Five & Qdup brings the ultimate high-octane funk experience to Shambhala. Led by Steve Raskin and the genre-bending precision of Qdup, their signature four-deck DJ sets are a masterclass in technical chemistry—seamlessly blending Ghetto Funk, Mid-tempo breaks, and global bass.

With a mission to "Funk 4 Peace," these D.C. heavyweights transform the Salmo River Ranch into a high-energy dance floor fueled by soulful grooves and heavy hitting rhythms. Whether you’re a long-time "Farmily" member or a first-timer, catching a Fort Knox Five & Qdup set is a non-negotiable Shambhala rite of passage.

Expect: Funk, Breaks, Nu Disco, Bass, and pure party vibes.
data.name: Fort Knox 5 & Qdup

data.body:
One of the world’s most respected dance music trailblazers, Conducta has been setting pace for close to a decade now.

Breaking through in the mid-2010s, it was in the latter half of that decade when the Bristol-born artist came into his own, releasing his most successful run of singles to date. As well as a succession of celebrated solo hits, there was also ‘Whippet’, his inescapable collaboration with Sammy Virji, and ‘Ladbroke Grove’, a production for AJ Tracey that not only went double platinum in the UK charts, but officially now stands as the best-selling UK garage record in history.

As a DJ, Conducta's stock also sky-rocketed, with a packed worldwide diary and a regular schedule of acclaimed DJ mixes, including a contribution to the BBC’s flagship Essential Mix series and his Kiwi Krush mixtape - the latter chosen as Resident Advisor’s number one mix of 2019. Subsequent years would see him star on the cover of both DJ Mag and Mixmag, establishing himself at the top table of UK dance music talent.

Of course, Conducta’s story is not complete without mentioning the label he formerly ran, Kiwi Rekords. Launched in 2019, Kiwi not only became synonymous with the cutting edge of modern UK Garage, championing the most exciting talent from the then-burgeoning scene, but also allowed Conducta full control of his own musical narrative and messaging. Although he closed Kiwi in 2024, having completed the label’s mission, it is widely lauded as one of the most important and exciting dance labels of recent times, winning Record Label of the Year at the 2020 DJ Mag awards.

Conducta has continued his upward trajectory into 2024, expanding what he does in pretty much every direction and introducing a more diverse musical palette - as exhibited on his genre-spanning ‘In Transit’ EP, a flagship five-track release that draws from all corners of UK soundsystem culture, and on a recent North American tour which saw him play extended back-to-back sets with both modern club pioneer Swisha and dance music royalty in Todd Edwards. As comfortable performing at New York’s most respected underground spots like Nowadays (which he sold out this last May) and Sustain Release as he is bringing down the house night after night on a sold-out UK tour, Conducta has carved out an incomparable niche - and done it on his own terms.

Born in Philadelphia, shaped by Los Angeles, and now immersed in Brooklyn, DJ Swisha is known for his high-octane, cross-genre approach to club music. His fast-paced sets and productions span footwork, house, jungle, Jersey/Baltimore club, ghetto house and more. Over the past decade, he has built a reputation for polyrhythmic, nostalgic, dancefloor-ready tracks that never compromise on a hard-hitting beat​.
data.name: Conducta B2B DJ SWISHA

One of the world’s most respected dance music trailblazers, Conducta has been setting pace for close to a decade now.

Breaking through in the mid-2010s, it was in the latter half of that decade when the Bristol-born artist came into his own, releasing his most successful run of singles to date. As well as a succession of celebrated solo hits, there was also ‘Whippet’, his inescapable collaboration with Sammy Virji, and ‘Ladbroke Grove’, a production for AJ Tracey that not only went double platinum in the UK charts, but officially now stands as the best-selling UK garage record in history.

As a DJ, Conducta's stock also sky-rocketed, with a packed worldwide diary and a regular schedule of acclaimed DJ mixes, including a contribution to the BBC’s flagship Essential Mix series and his Kiwi Krush mixtape - the latter chosen as Resident Advisor’s number one mix of 2019. Subsequent years would see him star on the cover of both DJ Mag and Mixmag, establishing himself at the top table of UK dance music talent.

Of course, Conducta’s story is not complete without mentioning the label he formerly ran, Kiwi Rekords. Launched in 2019, Kiwi not only became synonymous with the cutting edge of modern UK Garage, championing the most exciting talent from the then-burgeoning scene, but also allowed Conducta full control of his own musical narrative and messaging. Although he closed Kiwi in 2024, having completed the label’s mission, it is widely lauded as one of the most important and exciting dance labels of recent times, winning Record Label of the Year at the 2020 DJ Mag awards.

Conducta has continued his upward trajectory into 2024, expanding what he does in pretty much every direction and introducing a more diverse musical palette - as exhibited on his genre-spanning ‘In Transit’ EP, a flagship five-track release that draws from all corners of UK soundsystem culture, and on a recent North American tour which saw him play extended back-to-back sets with both modern club pioneer Swisha and dance music royalty in Todd Edwards. As comfortable performing at New York’s most respected underground spots like Nowadays (which he sold out this last May) and Sustain Release as he is bringing down the house night after night on a sold-out UK tour, Conducta has carved out an incomparable niche - and done it on his own terms.

Born in Philadelphia, shaped by Los Angeles, and now immersed in Brooklyn, DJ Swisha is known for his high-octane, cross-genre approach to club music. His fast-paced sets and productions span footwork, house, jungle, Jersey/Baltimore club, ghetto house and more. Over the past decade, he has built a reputation for polyrhythmic, nostalgic, dancefloor-ready tracks that never compromise on a hard-hitting beat​.

data.body:
ill.Gates has over 70 million plays across 300+ songs, releases with Grammy Winners, music in Star Wars Disneyland, and an Action Bronson remix which was featured in GTA V. He’s released 8 studio albums as ill.Gates, given over 1000 live performances on 5 continents and founded the Producer Dojo record label, which is rapidly approaching its 100th release.

Collaborators include 42 Dugg, Alanis, Andrew Huang, Apashe, Beats Antique, Bil Bless, Captain Hook, Chris Karns (Pretty Lights), CloZee, Conrank, Dead Kennedys, Def3, Defunk, Destroid, Dieselboy, DJ Vadim, Dub FX, Eko-Zu, Eprom, EyeOnEyez, Foreign Beggars, G Jones, Gucci Mane, ill-esha, Kandisha, KJ Sawka, Kraddy (Glitch Mob), Liquid Stranger, Mayor Ape, Mimosa, Minnesota, Mr Bill, Noisia, Opiuo, PAV4N, RIP Kenny, Stephan Jacobs, So Down, Souleye, SPL (Champagne Drip), Stylust, UHNK, Who Knew, DJ Yawn, and Zain Effendi (Pirates of the Caribbean, Dark Knight).

Producers around the world know ill.Gates as the first person to ever offer commercial Ableton templates (2006) and ill.Gates’ famous “128s” and “Mudpies” techniques have become so ubiquitous that their popularity eclipses even his own. More recently ill.Gates has expanded into programming, creating all-hardware “128s” style wavetables that now come stock with Serum 2 as well as a revolutionary new offline processing app called 128s Multisample Maker.

As an educator, ill.Gates has taught at the Ableton Education Summit, Berkelee College of Music, NYU, ACC (Austin Community College), the S.A.E. (School of Audio Engineering), and was featured on the Ableton ‘One Thing’ series and Andrew Huang’s ‘4 Producers Flip The Same Sample’ show.

In 2016 ill.Gates founded his own music school: Producer Dojo, teaching creativity and discipline to musicians using his signature combination of philosophy, psychology and practicality.

You can catch up with the ill one at www.illGates.com and learn all about the Dojo at www.ProducerDojo.com

Night Nurse: Two people trying to help heal the world through the joyful sharing of sound.

Hudson Lee is a producer and performing DJ from Winnipeg, currently based in Vancouver. Hudson effortlessly navigates through a multi-genre landscape while maintaining their immediately recognizable style characterized by a chromatic sound design aesthetic. Their most recent album, Light Dancer, is a perfect synthesis of their stylistic leanings, which they are able to express with bouncy basses, vibrant melodies, and a myriad of textures and movements that fall within and around the spectrum between those two complementary extremes.
Hudson Lee, in collaboration with Frequent, is also the co-owner of UPSCALE recordings, a driving force in the underground of electronic music, having worked with artists such as Mr. Bill, Vorso, Au5, KOAN Sound, and many more defining electronic acts.

data.body:
Established in 2009, DnB Girls has grown into the largest female Drum & Bass collective in North America, representing a massive network of over 30 artists from across the continent. Defined by unique individual styles moving as a cohesive unit, the group has become a powerhouse of the underground, delivering a sound that spans the entire emotional and physical landscape of the genre. Their curated showcases are a masterclass in contrast—balancing moments of euphoria with relentless, heavyweight pressure. By uniting the collective’s elite roster with hand-picked guest artists, they blend deep technical precision with raw, high-energy impact. Bridging the gap between soulful melodies and bone-rattling low end, the DnB Girls continue to prove why they are the most expansive and influential force of their kind in North American DnB.

Hailing from the Pacific Northwest, EndZyme is a Portland-based artist with a passion for Drum and Bass, as well as deep roots in Experimental and Freeform Bass. Her sets are defined by bold, unpredictable sound choices and a focus on creating fully immersive experiences. Beyond the decks, EndZyme curates music with the same care that she brings to her sets, always pushing herself and the audience beyond the expected.

There are few artists who combine live instrumentation, high energy, and gritty bass with the same ferocity and precision as violinist Kytami. Renowned for her explosive performances, she has made it her mission to create a sound that is instantly recognizable as her own—melding lyrical and melodic violin with the drive of Drum & Bass.

Kytami has toured extensively, performing at most major festivals all across Canada as well as international appearances in Bristol, Estonia and Singapore. Her work has earned her spots producing exclusive guest mixes for the esteemed DnB Show on BBC Radio 1 and BeatLab Radio and a Western Canadian Music Award for Electronic/Dance Recording of the Year. With an ever-growing catalogue of impactful releases and a reputation for spectacular live shows, Kytami continues to build a devoted global fan base while pushing the boundaries of what live bass music can be.

To.Get.Her is a high-energy drum & bass artist delivering explosive dance floor bangers designed to unite and uplift. She blends driving basslines, euphoric melodies, and powerful drops that ignite the crowd. She is also one half of the duo DJ group STARS33D from Central Alberta. As a passionate musician, devoted mother, and with the unwavering support of her amazing husband, her performances radiate strength, connection, and vibrant feminine energy — bringing people together
through bass.

If you mixed Jem and the Holograms with a rockstar Junglist, what would you get? VEXA - a vocal powerhouse that brings love, magic and expression to the stage. Based in Vancouver BC, VEXA is the performance alter of festival legend Vicki Perreault. Known for her showstopping sound and baptized by the bass, her enticing vocals will have you under her spell.
She returns for this years Village Takeover, channelling the divine feminine with Western Canada's best femme DNB talents.
data.name: DNB Girls Takeover Hosted By Crystal Fresh

data.body:
Mat the Alien is nothing shy of a prolific and iconic DJ who is emblematic to the electronic music scene. Since the late 80’s Mat has been exploring diverse facets of sound and elevating them to an otherworldly level so his stage name is quite appropriate. Mat has recorded a diverse array of music exploring bold soundscapes, goosebump inducing beats and dance floor erupting sonic explosions. Mat has played at legendary Venues & festivals across the globe from Russia to Asia and South America in Venues from Fabric to Low End Theory. He is notably a local favorite in the BC festival
industry. His performances at Shambala (for 18 years no less) and Basscoast is a massive attraction to audience members. Mat has also sustained a 8 month residency in Las Vegas, Toured with Monster Energy DJ's and DJ Jazzy Jeff for 7 Inches of Pleasure across Canada and was officially sponsored by Monster Energy Drink as an established artist for 8 years. If that wasn’t impressive enough, his music has been featured on BBC Radio 1 and Rinse FM many times, he has performed at some of the world’s best festivals and was the winner of the first ever Red Bull 3Style Dj Contest. While he is a household DJ name his music defies electronica, his material belongs in any music lovers collection.Mat has played at legendary Venues & festivals across the globe from Russia to Asia and South America in Venues from Fabric to Low End Theory He is notably a local favorite in the BC festival industry. His performances at Shambala (for 18 years no less) and every Basscoast is a massive attraction to audience members. Mat’s life changed when he experienced a catastrophic mountain biking accident in Oct 2020. This left him paralyzed from the Armpits down, transforming his world into something completely different. His resilience throughout this experience has truly illuminated his undeniable strength.

The worldly wide versatile pidgin rap experimentalist known as Magugu, who’s mission is to connect the African and western culture together with his Pidgin Rap style ( A local dialect in Nigeria consisting of broke English and local slangs ) Magugu has been making global waves with his iconic vocal tone, unique and distinctive unlike anything else you’ve heard before.
The native Nigerian based in the U.K who recently signed to Ninja tunes publishing is making waves in the underground scene with support from Dj’s like Giles Peterson, Skrillex, Target, NiNa Las Vegas, Supa Jamz etc. With millions of streams & viral videos circulating around the net His global sound has been filling up the dance floors from Europe to Africa to Asia to America and his style ranges from Dancehall, hip hop, dub, grime, bass, drum and bass, etc.
His energetic and positive attitude has won him praises as a top performer with his commanding presence and dance moves. Outspoken and ambitious magugu is aiming to take over the planet with his pidgin rap style & pump good energy to all his listeners
data.name: Mat the Alien & Magugu

data.body:
Alex Unger is a music producer and DJ based in Salt Lake City, Utah. His primarily downtempo productions combine the humanistic, instrument-driven grooves of jazz, funk, and soul with the punch and precision of hip hop and modern electronica, evoking a novel sense of nostalgia and inviting an intentional, engaged listening experience.

Behind the decks, he’s known for his versatility, genre fluidity, and diverse selections, often pushing the boundaries of what “club music” can mean and what the atmosphere on a dancefloor can feel like. Both his music and live sets reflect his dedication to honoring the time-tested classics that inspire him, while simultaneously paving his own way in today’s sprawling electronic landscape.

data.body: pheel., aka Philip Gallo, hails from the east coast and is currently residing in Denver. Since he moved in 2016, he has immersed himself in developing a unique perspective on bass music, drawing influences from a mixture or his roots as well as the local Colorado community. pheel. strives to take his audience on a sonic adventure with each set, guiding them through equal parts hip-hop and “bass music”. He pays respect to the classics of his youth simultaneously reimagining these tracks with modern production techniques and rumbling bass lines.

data.body:
"Energetic, genre-blurring, and unashamedly fun, PINEO & LOEB are the Canadian DJ/producer duo electrifying dance floors worldwide. Performing 100+ shows a year across the globe, they’ve ignited major festivals like Shambhala, Escapade, Friendship, and Burning Man—earning a reputation for high-octane sets that blend dance, hip-hop, and rock into a seamless party soundtrack.

Fresh off winning the iHeartRadio Future Star, their 2026 single “SPIN” broke into the Canadian Billboard Top 40- marking a major milestone in their rapidly growing career.

With 12M+ streams and releases on labels ranging from Universal to Warner, their studio work is as impactful as their live shows. Collaborations include Kiesza, William Orbit, and k-os, with music placements across Netflix and HBO, and radio support from BBC Radio 1 and multiple SiriusXM channels.

Sharing the stage with icons like Chris Lake, Tchami, and Sofi Tukker, PINEO & LOEB deliver a magnetic fusion of classics and cutting-edge sounds—uniting fans and leaving an unmistakable mark on today’s electronic music scene."

-----

"­Martina Sorbara, better known as the electro-pop outfit Dragonette, has lived multiple musical lifetimes over the past 15 years. A Juno-winning artist and sought-after songwriter, Sorbara has scored numerous global hits, including “Pick Up the Phone,” “Let it Go,” and the chart-topping “Hello” with Martin Solveig. She’s collaborated with the world’s biggest DJs (Basement Jaxx, Kaskade, and Galantis, among others) and released five acclaimed albums, including the Juno-nominated Fixin to Thrill (2009) and Bodyparts (2012). Along the way, Dragonette has headlined shows across the globe; supported the likes of Duran Duran, New Order, Ke$ha, and Miike Snow; and played to thousands of fans at Coachella, Electric Daisy Carnival, Electric Forest, Glastonbury, and Lollapalooza.

A longtime priority songwriter, the Toronto-based Sorbara also regularly works with acts under UMG, Warner Music, Armada and Spinnin’. Her musically diverse credits include songs for Keith Urban, Morgan Wallen, Dua Lipa, Cyndi Lauper, Pretty Sister, Mike Mago, and Carly Rae Jepsen with The Knocks.

Following, Sorbara’s acclaimed album Twennies, released in 2022, Dragonette moves into the newest chapter of her illustrious career, looking forward with more wisdom, experience and confidence than ever before, and creating her best works to date.

Last year, she released “Revelation” with the Knocks following their GRAMMY® Nominated 2022 hit, “Slow Song”. Their collaborative performance includes Aquaria (Revelation Technoligies’ use and CEO) and has been delighting fans across the country. ‘Revelation LIVE’ debuted at Pride Events in 2025 including OutLoud WeHo (LA), Nashville, Seattle, Boise and Denver. After successful shows last December in NY and LA they have planned more performances this Spring in Austin, SF, Chicago and Washington DC.
This spring, Martina embarks on another album release. Tiny Sun is the music art project of

Dragonette, The Henrys’ front man Don Rooke, and film score writer and arranger Jonathan Goldsmith.

Written over the course of several years the pieces range in style from cinematic lush soundscapes to lap steel country twang with many deviations on the way. The only prevailing constant is the length of the pieces: All under 2 minutes.
An idea born in a time well before the Spotify-whittled-down song length so prevalent in mainstream music. The pieces, through the only constraint of brevity, string together like little distinctive beads in a necklace, each unique but belonging together.
The release of the album was accelerated with a performance at Koerner Hall in Toronto which will include the Royal Conservatory Orchestra and include Tiny Sun and Dragonette songs. It will be a night to be remembered."
